themes.name: Foundation models (LLM/VLM/VLA) embedded as components, not end-to-end brains
themes.whats_new: The novelty has moved from 'use an LLM to plan' to wiring foundation models into specific slots of classical pipelines: a VLM guides backtracking inside a kinodynamic TAMP search tree (ThI1I.160), SIGLIP refines pseudo-labels for open-vocab 3D detection (ThI2I.25), LLM agents generate formal temporal-logic specs for fuzzing flight controllers (WeI2I.190, ThI2I.129), and LLM-based explainers are orchestrated per robot module (TuBT1.1). On the VLA side the frontier is cheap specialization, not pretraining: ExpReS-VLA adapts OpenVLA/pi0 on-device with retrieval-augmented replay (WeI2I.241), and WeI1I.286 attacks the long-tail data distribution of generalist policies.

themes.name: Multi-sensor SLAM/odometry with explicit uncertainty and learned front-ends in classical estimators
themes.whats_new: Beyond standard tight coupling: DoF-wise constraint-strength analysis with closed-form uncertainty propagation into BA (ThI1I.97), DUSt3R-style end-to-end pointmap SLAM gaining recurrent spatial memory and submap registration (WeI2I.251), semantic networks deciding per-voxel Gaussian-vs-plane residual models inside an IEKF (ThI2I.359), and exotic minimal-sensing variants: inertial-only odometry made interpretable via rotor-speed aerodynamics (ThI2I.68), event-camera-to-LiDAR-map localization (TuI1I.81), and metric monocular VO recovered from a deformable platform's spring physics (TuI2I.121).

themes.name: Tactile sensing as a fast reflex layer and a vision replacement
themes.whats_new: Tactile is graduating from 'extra modality' to primary control signal: sub-50ms reflex grasp stabilization combining piezoelectric slip detection with model-based internal-force QP (WeI2I.152), 200Hz tactile-reactive grasp adjustment with no object model (WeI2I.148), fully tactile (vision-denied) bimanual search-and-identify (ThI2I.192), and sim2real via differentiable path-tracing that calibrates a tactile simulator from just 3 real images (ThI2I.308).

themes.name: Diffusion/flow-matching spreading beyond manipulation policies into planning and trajectory optimization
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is now a constrained trajectory optimizer: team-level kinematically-constrained trajectories for coupled quadruped payload transport (ThI2I.88), residual refinement of coarse A* paths in 200m-scale 3D maps with 12.6x speedup (ThI2I.391), and model-based diffusion with flow-matching linear probability paths replacing variance-preserving noise schedules plus RL-tuned step counts (TuI2I.135). Conditional flow matching also fine-tunes motion manifolds from a handful of real throws (ThI2I.161).

themes.name: RL quadruped locomotion as commodity; research moves to composition and cross-embodiment
themes.whats_new: Single-policy sim-trained locomotion is treated as solved plumbing; the new questions are composing multiple terrain-specialist policies via learned Feasibility-Nets queried by a classical planner (WeI2I.45), distilling experts trained on randomly generated embodiments into one wheeled+quadruped navigation policy via action chunking transformers (ThI2I.339), and using a learned torso-leveling policy as a service so a UAV can dock on a walking quadruped on stairs (TuI1I.320).

themes.name: Data generation pipelines and richer benchmarks as the headline contribution
themes.whats_new: Datasets are getting adversarially realistic and synthetic pipelines are the method: GraspClutter6D's 62.6% occlusion scenes with 9.3B grasp labels (WeI2I.39), centimeter-aligned day/night driving pairs collected via trajectory re-tracking (TuI2I.116), single human demo expanded to unlimited sim demonstrations for flow-matching policies (TuI1I.104), and RoboEval replacing binary success rates with fluency/precision/coordination metrics for bimanual policies (ThI1I.341).

methods_shift: The dominant pattern is learning-inside-classical, not learning-replacing-classical. PPO-style RL-in-sim is the unquestioned default for quadruped locomotion (ThI1LB.4, TuI1I.320, WeI2I.45) — nobody argues for it anymore, they compose it. For safety-critical control, the default stack is firmly classical: QP-based control with Control Barrier Functions appears as the universal safety wrapper (SEP-NMPC's HOCBFs for slung payloads WeBT4.4, BarrierIK for shared autonomy TuI1I.273, CBF-QPs in multi-robot sensing ThI2I.324), with MPPI (ThI1I.328) and MPCC (WeI2I.385) as default local planners and IEKF/factor-graph estimators in SLAM. Within generative policies, flow matching is visibly displacing DDPM-style diffusion: LP-MBD explicitly replaces variance-preserving schedules with linear probability paths (TuI2I.135), and both DA-MMP (ThI2I.161) and Real2Gen (TuI1I.104) train flow-matching rather than diffusion policies. VLAs have become pretrained base models to specialize (OpenVLA, pi0, OpenVLA-OFT in WeI2I.241) — the fine-tuning-era playbook from NLP arriving in robotics. Off-the-shelf foundation models are now standard parts: FoundationPose as the baseline to beat (ThI2I.337), DUSt3R as the SLAM backbone (WeI2I.251), SIGLIP for pseudo-labeling (ThI2I.25). One fresh hybrid: actor-critic networks used as heuristics inside parallel best-first search at inference time (WeI2I.297), i.e., search as the deployment strategy for model-free RL.

notable.why: A flow-matching motion-manifold policy for ring-tossing that surpasses trained human experts in success rate and generalizes to targets beyond the training range.
On-device VLA specialization in 31 seconds from 12 demonstrations on a single RTX 5090, lifting OOD task success from 32% to 98% over naive fine-tuning.

First-ever NeRF training on a sub-100mW microcontroller aboard sub-30g nano-drones, with federated learning across drones to exceed single-drone memory limits (95% memory cut, only 17% accuracy drop).
A UAV dodges human-thrown projectiles by reading the attacker's body pose like a baseball batter, predicting the throw before the projectile is released.
An 11-institution EU project argues for intentional collisions in depalletizing — impact-aware grabbing cuts task time 29% and energy 35% versus quasi-static manipulation.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Torque-controlled adaptive arms; QP nullspace optimization yielding human-like elbow postures that passively decouple motion/force dynamics for robust force control.
Map-free autonomous driving; semantic scene-flow LiDAR perception for detection/SLAM without HD maps.
Spatially-grounded VLMs for autonomous-driving VQA with projective camera-parameter conditioning instead of external point-cloud modalities.
Warehouse picking of multi-part deformable objects (cable-attached appliances, pouch drinks) — pose estimation on rigid sub-parts beats FoundationPose 87.2% vs 22.8% picking success.
Open-vocabulary indoor 3D object detection with multimodal compensation, aimed at indoor perception products.
Legged-robot planning that composes multiple terrain-specialist RL policies via learned feasibility prediction.
Vibratory 2-DoF in-hand manipulation — stick-slip waveform surfaces in a parallel-jaw gripper that translate and rotate grasped parts.
Simulation infrastructure: LeHome, a deformable-object household manipulation simulator targeting low-cost robot embodiments.
Robot-assisted endovascular navigation using topology-encoded vessel graphs for anatomical path disambiguation.

contrarian.finding: A first-principles, learning-free placement planner (contact-points-first instead of pose sampling) beats five benchmarked algorithms and is 8x faster than the SOTA sample-and-evaluate method.

Negative result for the generalist-policy narrative: conventional long-tail remedies like re-sampling fail to fix tail-task performance in imitation learning; data scarcity specifically destroys spatial reasoning, requiring a targeted augmentation fix.
T-shirt unfolding solved with seam-feature extraction and a human-demo-initialized decision matrix, explicitly trained on real data with no simulation and no deep policy — and it generalizes.

A training-free framework using explicit geometric priors beats trained 2D-matching methods for unaligned scene change detection, arguing learned appearance matching drifts under large viewpoint change.

Cautionary ablation: masking LiDAR pointcloud input at inference causes catastrophic stair-climbing failure, showing the learned policy's 'implicit terrain knowledge' is load-bearing, not complementary.
Purely analytical force-torque balance along a wire estimates external contact force location and magnitude from observed shape alone — no force sensors, no learning.

humanoid.signal: Unitree Go2 as the commodity research platform: GRU-recurrent policy over LiDAR pointcloud + proprioception handles stair ascent/descent despite body-mounted LiDAR blind spots.
Locomotion-as-a-service: an RL torso-leveling policy (Hybrid Internal Model with Horizontal Alignment) turns a walking quadruped into a stable UAV landing pad on 17cm stairs and 30-degree slopes.
Recipe shift from one generalist locomotion policy to a library of terrain-specialist policies, each paired with a Feasibility-Net so a classical planner can route over policy capabilities.
Closely-coupled multi-quadruped payload transport across 3D terrain via constrained diffusion for team trajectories plus per-robot velocity optimization — legged teams as a logistics primitive.
Cross-embodiment navigation: one distilled policy zero-shot transfers across unseen wheeled and quadrupedal embodiments, trained on randomly generated morphologies.

Humanoid-scale hardware on the cheap: an inflatable torso with an origami folding spine matches/exceeds human spinal range of motion, with tendon+pneumatic actuation doubling lateral stiffness — a soft path to deployable humanoid bodies.
Actuator data point for pHRI/legged joints: a compact rotary SEA with curved compression-spring array delivering 18Nm capacity, 43.8Nm/rad stiffness, bidirectional load without antagonistic preload.

Text-to-motion generation (Mamba-based, FID 0.068 on HumanML3D) is being positioned explicitly as a humanoid-robot motion-synthesis front-end, coupling periodicity and keyframe saliency for stable long sequences.

themes.name: Frozen foundation models as plug-in modules in classical robot stacks
themes.whats_new: Rather than end-to-end retraining, papers bolt zero-shot foundation models into conventional pipelines: GENIE uses SAM2 for traversability prediction inside a path-fusion planner (won Earth Rover Challenge), AIM-SLAM uses VGGT pointmap predictions inside a keyframe-prioritized monocular SLAM backend, OVerSeeC chains LLM intent parsing + open-vocab segmentation to synthesize executable costmap code from satellite imagery, S3TS feeds event-camera pseudo-images to a frozen OWLv2 for training-free grasping, and TIGER/GSON/USPilot use LLMs/LMMs as task or social planners. The new pattern is modular composition with frozen weights, not fine-tuning.

themes.name: Data engines: massive synthetic generation and teleop datasets for manipulation
themes.whats_new: Scale and infrastructure are the contribution, not the policy architecture: UltraDexGrasp generates 20M frames of optimization-synthesized bimanual dexterous grasps and gets 81.2% real-world zero-shot success from synthetic-only training; Humanoid Everyday ships 10.3k humanoid teleop trajectories across 260 tasks with a cloud-based policy evaluation platform; M3Bench auto-generates 30k whole-body mobile manipulation demos; EgoMI captures synchronized head+hand egocentric human data retargetable to semi-humanoids. Benchmarks now ship with automatic demonstration generators.

themes.name: Sampling-based MPC revived via learned value functions and GPU batching
themes.whats_new: MPPI and trajectory optimization are being hybridized with learning and parallel hardware instead of replaced by it: TD-CD-MPPI plugs a temporal-difference-learned terminal value function into MPPI to get infinite-horizon reasoning with short rollouts on real legged robots; CDF-MPPI collapses the horizon to one step using configuration-space distance field gradients and runs at 750+ Hz; GATO co-designs a GPU-batched nonlinear TO solver with 18-21x CPU speedups for edge MPC; a GPU time-layered DAG planner makes wrench-feasibility filtering tractable for aerial manipulators.

themes.name: Diffusion models spreading beyond action imitation
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is becoming a generic conditional denoiser for non-policy problems: DiffVL reformulates visual localization as GPS-trajectory denoising conditioned on BEV features and OpenStreetMap, reaching sub-meter accuracy without HD maps; ABB/CMU's disentangled point-cloud diffusion for object placement explicitly beats SE(3) diffusion on precision insertion; GAF extends 3D Gaussian Splatting into a 4D action field with an action-vision-aligned denoising head; an inference-stage projection adapts pretrained diffusion policies to unseen manipulators with zero retraining.

themes.name: Uncertainty quantification as a runtime control signal, not an offline metric
themes.whats_new: Calibrated uncertainty now directly modulates behavior: Random-Set Neural Networks predict belief functions over class sets and dynamically throttle an autonomous racing vehicle's speed; UniFField builds a generalizable neural feature field with per-modality (visual/semantic/geometric) uncertainty that drives active object search; touch-only localization uses spatially-aware belief entropy to select information-maximizing contact actions; a multi-modal lane-change transformer quantifies confidence at modality, fusion, and prediction levels.

themes.name: RL-in-sim maturing into recipe science and emergent behavior
themes.whats_new: Sim-to-real RL is treated as solved-in-principle; papers now ablate what makes it work or push reward minimalism: SimpleFlight identifies five concrete factors (rotation-matrix actor inputs, time-vector critic inputs, action-difference regularization, selective randomization, large batches) cutting Crazyflie tracking error >50%; multi-agent competitive racing shows agile flight and overtaking strategy emerging from a sparse win-the-race reward, transferring to real hardware better than dense progress rewards; an exoskeleton controller trained entirely in a reflex-based musculoskeletal simulation cuts human metabolic cost 9.1% with no motion-capture data.

methods_shift: The default toolkit is clearly hybrid. Frozen vision/language foundation models (SAM2, VGGT, OWLv2, Qwen-VL, GPT-class LLMs) are displacing task-specific trained perception networks as the standard front end — used zero-shot inside SLAM, traversability, costmap, and task-planning pipelines (ThI2I.341, ThI1I.304, ThI1I.141, ThI2LB.6). In control, GPU-batched sampling methods (MPPI, batched DDP-style TO) augmented with offline-learned value functions are displacing both CPU single-solve nonlinear MPC and pure end-to-end RL for whole-body and legged control (ThI1I.394, ThI2I.218, TuI1I.137). For manipulation, diffusion policies are the assumed baseline being patched rather than introduced — inference-time projection for cross-embodiment transfer (TuI2I.45), disentangled point diffusion replacing SE(3) diffusion for precision placement (WeI2I.132). VLAs (OpenVLA) appear as a known-flawed default being repaired with training-time visual prompting (WeI1I.145) and distilled force tokens (ThI2I.154) rather than scaled. RL-in-sim with domain randomization is now commodity for locomotion/flight; the research moved to ingredient ablation (TuI1I.8). Meanwhile classical estimators quietly persist where they win: EKF radar-inertial odometry with online temporal calibration (TuI2I.10), GM-PHD Bayesian tracking with semantic birth models (ThAT3.4), and kernel SVMs for tactile intent (ThI2I.217).

notable.why: Agile drone flight AND racing strategy (overtaking, blocking) emerge purely from a sparse win-the-race reward in multi-agent competition, and the resulting policies transfer to real hardware more reliably than conventional dense progress-reward training on identical sim and randomization.

FD-VLA's distilled (hallucinated-from-vision) force token outperforms actual force-torque sensor measurements for contact-rich manipulation — a striking claim that learned force priors beat the physical sensor they imitate.
81.2% real-world success on bimanual dexterous grasping of novel objects from a policy trained exclusively on 20M frames of synthetic data — zero real demonstrations.
A hip-exoskeleton controller trained entirely in a reflex-based musculoskeletal simulation (no human experimental motion data at all) reduces real users' metabolic walking cost by 9.1% on deployment.

First demonstrated autonomous clip positioning for laparoscopic cholecystectomy on a physical phantom — 0.75mm precision at 100% execution success, trained from only 60 hand-labeled point clouds plus 128k synthetic ones.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: STEP: preference-conditioned transformer RL for online 3D bin packing that trades packing density against robot operational time, cutting cycle time 44% in warehouse automation.
Disentangled point-cloud diffusion for high-precision industrial insertion and object placement, beating SE(3) diffusion baselines.
Imagine2Act: imagined-goal-conditioned 3D imitation learning for high-precision relational object rearrangement on their humanoid/manipulation platforms.
Humanoid Everyday: a 10.3k-trajectory, 260-task open-world humanoid manipulation dataset (RGB/depth/LiDAR/tactile) plus a cloud-based standardized policy evaluation platform.
Social-Qwen: instruction-tuned compact VLM for multiparty group engagement understanding, targeting real-time social intelligence in their robots.
Waliner: an NPU-embedded plugin wall-feature mapping module to make mass-produced home service robots robust in featureless white-wall interiors.
DiffVL: diffusion-based GPS denoising localization on standard-definition maps to get sub-meter vehicle localization without HD map maintenance costs.
A high-stiffness capacitive joint torque sensor using a hybrid Scott-Russell/parallelogram flexure for cobot joints.
EgoMI: egocentric human demonstration capture (synchronized head + end-effector trajectories) retargetable to semi-humanoid robots with actuated camera heads.
Semantic-guided progressive object removal in Gaussian-splatting scenes for XR/scene-editing pipelines.

contrarian.finding: User study on UMI-style handheld grippers for demonstration collection: even the best gripper design remained substantially slower and less effective than bare hands at a real hospital task, a negative result for the popular handheld-gripper data-collection paradigm.

A decision-based black-box attack (no gradients, no model internals) achieves 41-81% success at flipping perceived driving intentions of SOTA trajectory predictors (Trajectron++, Grip++) with sub-0.45m perturbations — learned prediction stacks are fragile in ways classical methods aren't.

HetroD benchmark shows state-of-the-art prediction and planning models systematically fail on heterogeneous VRU-dominated traffic: they cannot predict lateral motorcycle/cyclist movements or unstructured maneuvers.

M3Bench evaluation finds SOTA whole-body motion generation models broadly struggle to coordinate base-arm motion under environmental constraints — the learned mobile-manipulation pipeline is far from solved despite benchmark saturation claims elsewhere.

Moth lands micro-drones within 10cm using three photodiodes and an IR beacon for under $83 — matching vision-based learned landing pipelines at a fraction of the energy and compute, classical sensing beating perception stacks.
A geometry-aware kernel SVM (CK-SVM) beats four learned baselines for tactile human-intent recognition (97.1% sim / 90.8% real), classical ML winning where data is scarce and structure is known.

humanoid.signal: Toyota Research Institute's Humanoid Everyday: 10.3k teleoperated humanoid trajectories over 260 tasks including locomotion-integrated manipulation and human-humanoid interaction, with multimodal sensing (tactile, LiDAR) and a cloud evaluation platform — humanoid data infrastructure is becoming a corporate-funded commons.

EgoMI shows egocentric human data can drive semi-humanoid robots with actuated camera heads if you explicitly model coordinated head-hand motion and add memory-augmented policies — a concrete recipe for bridging the human-to-humanoid embodiment gap without robot teleop.

Locomotion control recipe: TD-learned terminal value functions let MPPI run drastically shorter horizons on real legged robots while keeping infinite-horizon behavior, with constraint handling via discount modulation instead of hand-tuned penalties.

Quadruped autonomy on cheap hardware: multi-layer elevation maps plus terrain augmentation and knowledge distillation, trained in sim and deployed on an explicitly low-cost quadruped — capable rough-terrain traversal no longer requires premium platforms.

Quadrupeds are now routine industrial tools: hierarchical adaptive inspection planning validated through deployments in real subterranean mines, where the research question is replanning around changed site morphology, not locomotion itself.

themes.name: Foundation models (VLA/LLM/MLLM) retrofitted as modular robot components
themes.whats_new: The work has moved from training new VLAs to bolting lightweight plug-ins onto frozen ones: InSpire (WeI2I.72) prepends a spatial-reasoning question to kill spurious correlations with no extra data; MAP-VLA (WeI2I.162) adds retrieval of learnable soft memory prompts for long-horizon tasks; SSM-VLA (TuI1I.185) fixes latent action models with geometry-aware encoders and a visual chain-of-thought. LLMs also show up as planners/mission managers (ThI2I.67 belief-graph replanning, TuI2LB.9 local-LLM UAV mission manager constrained to ROS 2 tool calls, ThI1I.307 LLM-derived semantic priors for zero-shot object-goal nav) rather than end-to-end controllers.

themes.name: Second-generation diffusion/flow policies: fixing inference pathologies, not introducing the method
themes.whats_new: Diffusion and flow matching are now assumed; the novelty is in repair work. ThBT2.2 shows more Euler steps universally hurts flow-matching policies (non-Lipschitz velocity field near t=1) and proposes U-shaped time scheduling plus dense-jump integration. PPGuide (ThI1I.282) steers a pre-trained diffusion policy at inference with a self-supervised failure predictor gradient. ECAHD (ThI2I.280) splits diffusion hierarchically (sparse global / collision-guided local) to halve inference time. Diffusion is also spreading to new targets: goal-shape distributions for deformables (TuI1I.256), embodiment-conditioned fail-active trajectories (WeI1I.294), SE(3) flow matching for pose distributions (WeI1I.194).

themes.name: Tactile sensing goes sim2real and generative
themes.whats_new: Instead of better sensor hardware alone, papers synthesize or transfer touch: ViTacGen (TuI1I.348) generates contact-depth images from vision to drop the physical tactile sensor at deployment; GCS (WeI2I.201) achieves zero-shot sim2real RL insertion with raw 3-axis magnet-based (u-skin) readings, going beyond binarization; CG-THWM (ThI2I.282) builds a temporal haptic world model for fine-tolerance peg-in-hole; DexScrew (ThI2I.276) uses sim RL policies as teleop primitives to harvest real tactile demos. Sensor-design papers (translucent-marker GelSight skin ThI1I.164, barometric arrays TuI2I.47) target the marker/markerless and elastomer-dynamics trade-offs.

themes.name: Learning wrapped in safety certificates and classical regulators
themes.whats_new: The deployment recipe is now RL-plus-filter rather than RL alone: PPO exploration policies with a safety filter that overrides infeasible waypoints (WeI2I.183), classical planners (DWA/TEB) used as provable trust regions to regulate RL action selection (TuI2I.143), neural Lyapunov + barrier certificates learned jointly with the dynamics with conformal-prediction guarantees (TuI1I.410), CBF-based connectivity control under adversarial jamming (WeI2I.177). Safety is treated as an architectural component, not a reward term.

themes.name: Humanoid whole-body control matures into recipes plus data infrastructure
themes.whats_new: The teacher-student sim-RL recipe is consolidating (ExBody2, TuI1I.76, with an explicit feasibility-vs-diversity dataset-curation principle), and the bottleneck has shifted to data collection: TWIST2 (WeBT3.1) is a mocap-free VR teleop stack collecting 100 whole-body demos in 15 minutes. Novel control decompositions appear (per-limb multi-agent RL with centroidal-angular-momentum rewards producing emergent arm swing, WeBT3.8), plus radically cheap hardware (22-DOF tendon hand under $60 in structure cost, WeI2I.393).

themes.name: Closed-loop autonomy in medical robotics via novel sensing modalities
themes.whats_new: Medical papers pair exotic sensing with real-time closed-loop control rather than teleoperation: OCT-voxel-driven sampling MPC for autonomous volumetric laser resection (ThI2I.271), 5-DoF magnetic localization at 20 ms latency with control-localization field decoupling for multi-capsule robots (TuI2I.341), 6-DoF magnet tracking with head-up-display guidance for compression anastomosis validated ex vivo (WeI2I.344).

methods_shift: Three defaults are visible. (1) For manipulation imitation, diffusion/flow-matching generative policies have fully displaced deterministic BC regression — papers no longer argue for them, they patch them (ThBT2.2's integration-step degradation fix, PPGuide's inference-time guidance, ECAHD's hierarchical split), and flow matching is starting to displace DDPM-style diffusion (ThBT2.2, WeI1I.194 SE(3) flow for pose). (2) For humanoid/legged control, RL-in-sim with privileged teacher-student distillation and domain transfer is the unquestioned recipe (ExBody2 TuI1I.76, WeBT3.8), displacing model-based whole-body QP — though wheeled-bipedal work still ships classical ZMP+LQR+QP with no learning (TuI2LB.3). (3) For semantics and task planning, frozen VLAs/LLMs with plug-in adapters (prompts, memory retrieval, tool-constrained action sets) are displacing both end-to-end retraining and hand-built task planners (WeI2I.162, ThI2I.67, TuI2LB.9). Meanwhile classical optimal control is not retreating in real-time domains: sampling-based MPC/MPPI keeps advancing on its own terms (single-instance-sampling MPPI at high frequency TuI1I.362, OCT-guided sampling MPC ThI2I.271, passivity-aware MPC admittance ThI1I.209), and a hybrid pattern — learned components inside certified classical wrappers (CBFs, safety filters, trust regions: WeI2I.183, TuI2I.143, TuI1I.410) — looks like the emerging deployment standard.

notable.why: Shows the counterintuitive, apparently universal result that adding Euler integration steps at inference DEGRADES flow-matching policy performance, traces it to a non-Lipschitz velocity field near t=1, and turns the fix into a 23.7% gain.

Claims the first closed-loop autonomous volumetric robotic laser tissue resection guided by OCT, with sampling-based MPC running directly on OCT voxels and sub-millimeter calibration — an unusually complete surgical autonomy result.

TWIST2 collects 100 whole-body humanoid demonstrations in 15 minutes with near-100% success using a mocap-free VR rig and a ~$250 custom robot neck, then open-sources the entire system and dataset — directly attacking the humanoid data bottleneck.

DEFT achieves 99.5% success generating trajectories for arbitrarily damaged 7-DoF arms versus RRT's 42.4%, with zero-shot generalization to unseen actuator failures — 'fail-active' operation as a learned capability.

An MIT 'sober look' showing GPT-4o-class MLLMs fail as driving world models — they read single frames well but cannot synthesize coherent multi-frame narratives of ego dynamics or actor interactions — a rare published negative result on a hyped direction.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: V2V-GoT: MLLM-based vehicle-to-vehicle cooperative driving with a graph-of-thoughts reasoning framework, plus a released QA dataset for occlusion-aware cooperative perception/planning.
Autonomous valet parking: probabilistic future-occupancy estimation of parking spots and a planner with wait-and-go behaviors under partial observability.
SE(3) flow matching for full 6D pose distributions, feeding uncertainty-aware grasping and active perception.
PPGuide: inference-time classifier guidance that steers pre-trained diffusion manipulation policies away from predicted failures.
Riemannian Time Warping: multi-signal temporal alignment on manifolds for robot motion learning, validated on an LBR iiwa.
Point-tracking-based object-centric history representations to give visuomotor manipulation policies memory beyond the Markov assumption.
Single-instance-sampling MPPI for high-frequency real-time task-space MPC on 7-DoF manipulators.
TWIST2: portable mocap-free humanoid teleoperation and whole-body data collection, plus a hierarchical egocentric visuomotor policy stack.
Farsighted-LAM / SSM-VLA: geometry- and dynamics-aware latent action models for VLA pretraining on unannotated video.
ActiveVLN: multi-turn GRPO-based RL post-training for MLLM vision-and-language navigation agents, replacing DAgger-style imitation.

contrarian.finding: Negative result: state-of-the-art MLLMs (incl. GPT-4o) are poor world models for driving — they fail at ego dynamics, actor interaction, and trajectory reasoning across frames despite excelling at single-image interpretation.

Large empirical study finds the simplest recipes — directly replaying offline demonstration data and behavior-cloning initialization — consistently beat more sophisticated offline-RL pretraining for online sample efficiency.

More compute at inference hurts: increasing integration steps in flow-matching policies universally degrades performance, cutting against the 'more denoising steps = better' intuition imported from image generation.

Unregularized RL navigation underperforms; constraining the RL policy to a provable trust region around classical planners (DWA/TEB) beats both pure learning and pure classical baselines — classical structure remains load-bearing.
A wheeled-bipedal robot handles slopes, stairs, and disturbances with entirely classical perception-less control (ZMP planning + LQR + QP torque allocation) — no learning, no terrain sensing.

Cheap infrastructure beats better perception: fiducial markers embedded in the road, fused with confidence gating, outperform vision-only and SD-map-augmented lane-topology models by 27% under occlusion — a bet against the pure-onboard-perception orthodoxy.
GeoPF revives 1980s potential fields with explicit geometric primitives and beats modern reactive planners at up to two orders of magnitude lower compute with a single parameter set.

humanoid.signal: ExBody2 codifies the expressive whole-body control recipe: sim RL with privileged teacher distilled to student, keypoint tracking decoupled from velocity control, and — notably — an explicit feasibility-vs-diversity trade-off driving automatic motion-dataset curation before fine-tuning.

TWIST2 makes humanoid whole-body data collection cheap and portable: PICO4U VR teleop (no mocap), a custom 2-DoF neck for egocentric vision at ~$250, 100 demos per 15 minutes, fully open-sourced hardware/software/dataset — the humanoid data flywheel is commoditizing.

MIT trains arms and legs as separate RL agents (centralized critic, decentralized actors) sharing only base state and centroidal angular momentum; natural arm swing emerges from CAM-tracking rewards and improves push recovery, walking, stairs on a real mit-humanoid.

Cost floor for dexterous hands keeps dropping: 22-DOF tendon-driven biomimetic hand, 750 g including forearm, structure cost under $60 (3D-printed, excl. actuators), 100% Kapandji and GRASP-taxonomy pass, open-sourced.

Wheeled-bipedal platforms remain a classical-control stronghold: ZMP-based whole-body planning + LQR wheel torque + QP allocation handles slopes and stairs with zero terrain perception and zero learning.

Robustness tooling for legged contact dynamics is maturing: saltation-matrix-based parametric sensitivity yields trajectory tubes that stay feasible under uncertainty in dynamics, guard conditions, and reset maps.

themes.name: RL-in-sim escapes locomotion into unconventional domains
themes.whats_new: The locomotion recipe (build a fast parallel simulator, train PPO/SAC, distill teacher-to-student, deploy zero-shot) is now applied far outside legged robots: whole-body control of soft surgical robots via a learned hysteresis-aware neural sim (ThI1I.352), hand-eye calibration pose tuning with SAC-Discrete (ThI2I.346), demonstration-free knot-tying over topological actions (WeI2I.185), vibration-minimizing trajectory generation for flexible-link arms paired with a PDE controller (TuI1I.23), and vision-only outdoor drone flight with privileged learning + curriculum (WeI2I.423). The new move is learning a neural surrogate of hard-to-model dynamics first, then running massively parallel RL inside it.

themes.name: Neural rendering (3DGS/NeRF/implicit surfaces) becomes the SLAM map representation
themes.whats_new: Gaussian splatting and neural fields are no longer demos on TUM-RGBD; they are being hardened for robotics edge cases: first multi-camera RGB-only 3DGS SLAM with multi-cam bundle adjustment (WeI2I.200), scale-adaptive 3DGS that works on both KITTI and room-scale without hyperparameter tuning via Polyak step size and 'gaussian fission' (TuI1I.19), Gaussian splatting rasterized under an acoustic image-formation model for imaging sonar including azimuth-streak modeling (WeI2I.416), uncertainty-guided static NeRF maps that scrub dynamic objects for localization (TuI1I.358), and GP implicit surfaces yielding scene flow, 6DoF object tracking and reconstruction in closed form (WeI2I.262).

themes.name: Synthetic data engines for imitation learning
themes.whats_new: Instead of collecting more teleop, papers generate action-LABELED data: fine-tuned Stable Diffusion synthesizes third-person RGB-D observations of novel robot poses with matching joint-space actions and contact constraints (WeI1I.57), a pretrained video diffusion model is extended with a parallel action-diffusion branch plus Bridge Attention to co-generate video-action pairs (ThI1I.212), LLMs annotate and retarget a single human demo into large datasets with Thompson-sampling-optimized annotations (TuI1I.336), quality-diversity evolutionary search generates placement poses Graspit!-style (WeI1I.124), and AVs mine surrounding vehicles' trajectories as free demonstrations (TuI2I.303). The labeled-action bottleneck, not the image bottleneck, is the explicit target.

themes.name: Cross-embodiment generalist policies via specialist distillation
themes.whats_new: The standard pipeline is now: pre-train on one embodiment (IL or teacher RL), adapt per-embodiment with residual RL, then distill into a single policy conditioned on an embodiment embedding. COMPASS gets a 5x success improvement over the base IL policy and zero-shot sim-to-real (ThI1I.227); RING trains purely in simulation with large-scale randomization over body size, pivot point and camera parameters, hitting 78.9% success across 4 real platforms including a wheeled humanoid (ThAT1.4); the same multi-skill distillation recipe powers a quadruped highway inspector (WeI1I.137).

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs as a semantic layer wrapping classical planners, not replacing them
themes.whats_new: Rather than end-to-end VLAs, the dominant pattern is VLM-informed costs and LLM-generated code feeding classical machinery: a VLM infers which objects tolerate contact to build an anisotropic cost map for a contact-aware A* planner (WeI1I.64), LLMs emit code-as-policy waypoint edits with a second LLM explaining the code to non-experts (WeI2I.339), and a multi-agent LLM/VLM system reads appliance manuals and falls back to web search for unseen appliances (TuI2I.126). Where VLAs do appear, the additions are reflection and selectivity: CollabVLA solicits human help under uncertainty (WeI2I.109) and AdaThinkDrive learns via GRPO when to skip chain-of-thought entirely (WeI2I.303).

themes.name: GPU/HPC-parallelized classical optimal control
themes.whats_new: Classical MPC and HJ reachability are being rebuilt for accelerators instead of abandoned: a primal-dual iLQR using parallel associative scans on the KKT system in JAX beats acados/crocoddyl by 60-700% and runs MPC-in-the-loop RL on GPU (WeI2I.387); MPC supervision stabilizes adversarial DeepReach training for two-player zero-sum safety value functions (WeI1I.300); and SHARP offloads hash-distributed multi-goal A* to a remote HPC cluster with 30ms round trips (WeI2I.118).

methods_shift: RL-in-sim with teacher-student distillation is now the unmarked default for any control problem, displacing hand-designed controllers even in soft robotics, calibration, and deformable-object manipulation — the enabling move is learning a fast neural surrogate simulator (hysteresis NN in ThI1I.352, observer-predictor in ThI2I.179) when no analytic sim exists. Diffusion models in this batch are used less as policies and more as data factories (ROPA WeI1I.57, CoVAR ThI1I.212) and world models (FlowDreamer WeI1I.403, which makes 3D scene flow the explicit motion representation rather than implicit latent dynamics). In dense SLAM, 3D Gaussian Splatting is displacing both NeRF and classical point/voxel maps as the default map primitive, now extended to multi-camera rigs and even imaging sonar; meanwhile the sensor mix is shifting toward Doppler-capable 4D radar and FMCW LiDAR (WeI1I.347) and event cameras with continuous-time Gaussian-process preintegration replacing discrete IMU preintegration (ThI2I.344). LLMs/VLMs are settling into a semantic-front-end role (cost maps, code-as-policy, manual reading) on top of classical A*/optimization planners rather than end-to-end control. Notably, classical MPC is not being displaced — it is being GPU-parallelized in JAX and merged with learning (WeI2I.387), and certifiable factor-graph optimization keeps advancing on its own track (WeI2I.273). Conspicuously rare in this batch: pure diffusion-policy manipulation papers and monolithic VLA manipulation models.

notable.why: A 7-DOF arm dodges foam projectiles using A* search offloaded to an HPC cluster 300 km away with 30 ms total planning latency and 88% avoidance success — making remote supercomputing a credible real-time control substrate.

A single embodiment-agnostic navigation policy trained only in simulation hits 78.9% success across 4 real robots (Stretch RE-1, LoCoBot, Unitree Go1, RB-Y1 wheeled humanoid), matching or beating embodiment-specific policies.
Parallel-scan primal-dual iLQR on GPU beats acados by up to 700% and computes centralized whole-body MPC for 16 legged robots in under 25 ms, with Tesla among the affiliations.
An underwater knee exoskeleton for scuba divers cuts air consumption 22.7% and quadriceps activation 20.9% in real dives — a first-of-its-kind extension of exoskeletons to underwater locomotion.
A film-based electromagnetically actuated soft micromanipulator reaches 2117 deg/s and 1.6 m/s while keeping ~6.3 micron precision, directly contradicting the assumption that soft robots are slow.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Hi-Drive, a hierarchical POMDP behavior/trajectory planner for urban autonomous driving that infers other drivers' hidden styles — a planning-based production AV stack.
AdaThinkDrive, an end-to-end VLA driving model trained with GRPO to adaptively skip chain-of-thought reasoning, cutting inference time 14% while raising Navsim PDMS.
M-VTOP, a retraining-free visuo-tactile particle-filter pose estimator achieving sub-millimeter accuracy for high-precision industrial assembly from only a CAD model.
CoVAR, video-action co-generation via coupled diffusion models to mass-produce action-labeled training data for manipulation policies.
Static NeRF mapping that scrubs dynamic objects for robust vehicle localization in changing environments.
Efficient instance-centric traffic-agent behavior models trained with adversarial IRL for scalable multi-agent driving simulation.
GRACE, a unified multi-robot path-planning simulator and benchmark spanning grid, roadmap, and continuous representations for warehouse/intralogistics planning.
COMPASS, a cross-embodiment mobility foundation policy built from single-embodiment IL plus residual RL and distillation — generalist navigation as a platform play.
A pitch-variation filter making 2D-LiDAR SLAM viable on two-wheeled self-balancing robots, pointing at a consumer self-balancing robot platform.
CEM with reward models to co-design 3D-printed tendon-driven soft hands from teleoperation data — cheap compliant end-effectors for manipulation at scale.
RAMPA, a Meta Quest 3 AR interface for programming industrial arms by demonstration with on-the-fly ProMP training.

contrarian.finding: Explicitly states it introduces no new algorithms: careful system integration of established methods (LiDAR clustering, JIPDA tracking, finite state machine) achieves fully autonomous ferry docking in a live harbor with moving vessels — engineering beats novelty.
A classical hierarchical POMDP planner from SenseTime outperforms state-of-the-art learning-based planners on real-world urban driving benchmarks.
Always-on chain-of-thought reasoning makes driving worse: adaptive thinking beats the always-Think baseline by 1.4 PDMS while cutting inference 14% — more reasoning is not better.

The conventional practice of transforming raw IMU data to global coordinates destroys observability for UAV inertial odometry; simply keeping the body frame yields a 66.7% accuracy gain — a standard preprocessing step shown to be harmful.

With informed vehicle-selection sampling, 10% of the imitation-learning dataset matches or exceeds full-dataset performance, and naive random data augmentation degrades it — against the scale-is-all-you-need narrative.
Distributed classical A* search on an HPC cluster, not a learned policy, delivers below-human-reaction-time projectile dodging on a real manipulator.

humanoid.signal: Locomotion recipe for humanoid soccer kicking: 4-stage teacher-student RL (chase, directional kick, distillation, constrained-RL refinement) with explicit sensor-noise modeling closes sim-to-real for continual ball-kicking on a real humanoid — constrained RL in the final adaptation stage is flagged as essential.

Anthropomorphic 'Folding Hand' with a single-actuator reconfigurable folding palm and five tendon-driven underactuated fingers; all 6 actuators + 5 sensors cost under $180 — extreme cost compression for humanoid hands.

GPU-parallel primal-dual iLQR (JAX, parallel scan) makes whole-body MPC for legged robots 60% faster than acados and enables RL training with MPC in the loop directly on GPU; scales to 16 robots in <25 ms.
Variable-stiffness tension sensor for tendon-driven hands attacks the bandwidth/impedance/force-sensing triple trade-off, with force-control bandwidth rising 12.7 to 19.5 Hz as tension scales.
RING navigation generalist deploys zero-shot on the RB-Y1 wheeled humanoid in a real kitchen — humanoids inheriting cross-embodiment policies for free rather than getting bespoke training.

Learned observer-predictor with UUB stability guarantees gives limb-level full-body motion prediction for MPPI planning on a Vision 60 quadruped — neural models replacing simplified kinematics for collision-aware legged planning.
Dynamically extensible five-bar leg linkage mechanically morphs between height-advantaged and force-advantaged configurations — variable mechanical advantage as an alternative to bigger actuators.

20-channel FSR tactile glove streaming at 300 Hz over WiFi as a cheap scalable data-collection platform for visuo-tactile humanoid hand control — tactile sensing cost being pushed down to commodity parts.

themes.name: VLMs/LLMs as plug-in reasoning modules across every robotics subfield
themes.whats_new: VLMs are no longer just perception or task-planning front-ends — they now modulate low-level control (LISN's VLM agent tunes costmaps and controller parameters in a fast-slow hierarchy), call external geometric tools for centimeter-precision computation (TIGeR synthesizes code and invokes libraries instead of estimating), and get test-time compute treatment borrowed from LLM research (ThI2I.132 uses beam search over futures plus a learned critic with confidence-based early exit). The pattern extends to underwater monitoring (DREAM), industrial multi-agent assistants (MICA), and even a VLA that outputs raw voltages for a magnetic soft robot (TMR-VLA).

themes.name: Cross-embodiment transfer via embodiment-agnostic intermediate representations
themes.whats_new: Instead of paired datasets, papers route skills through neutral interfaces: contrastively-aligned latent action spaces unifying human hands, anthropomorphic hands, and parallel grippers (TuI1I.63, +25.3% success); sparse optical-flow trajectories extracted from human videos (TrajSkill, TuI2I.249); and 3D object flow reconstructed from generated video as a manipulation target (Dream2Flow, TuI2I.123). The shared bet: factor out morphology, keep object/scene dynamics.

themes.name: Industrializing the demonstration-data supply chain
themes.whats_new: Data collection itself is the research contribution: COBALT runs 256 concurrent smartphone teleoperators on 8 GPUs at sub-100ms latency and crowdsourced 7,500+ demos across 9 countries in 5 days, showing phones rival specialized teleop hardware; HumanoidExo uses a wearable exoskeleton so humanoid policies learn whole-body skills (even walking) from human-worn data plus only 5 real-robot demos; STONE auto-labels 3D traversability with no human annotation. Annotation-free / crowdsourced pipelines are displacing lab-scale teleop rigs.
themes.share: ~8% (plus ~10% more pure dataset/benchmark papers: COSMO-Bench, SurgSync, DynOPETs, PedestrianQA, FailBench, iVISION-2DCD)

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting + foundation-model priors as the default 3D perception stack
themes.whats_new: 3DGS has fully replaced NeRF as the assumed scene representation, and the new work is about hardening it: reflection-aware deferred rendering with 3D-foundation-model geometric priors (RGS), feed-forward generalizable diffusion enhancers that fix any pretrained Gaussian scene in minutes without per-scene optimization (GenRe, for AV closed-loop sim), multi-view error-guided pruning (MVC-GS), and triplane-based camera tokenization that cuts AV policy tokens 72% (ThAT3.9). Separately, relative-depth foundation models calibrated by sparse range anchors are becoming the standard depth-completion recipe (OASIS-DC).

themes.name: RL-in-sim graduating to heavy, dirty, field-scale hardware
themes.whats_new: Sim-trained RL is leaving lab quadrupeds: a policy controlling a 12-ton hydraulic excavator extracts 0.4-0.7m boulders from real soil using just 20 LiDAR points per rock (ThI2I.228); two-stage RL motion tracking transfers zero-shot to a Unitree G1 under terrain, force, and payload disturbances (Any2Track); end-to-end DRL drives articulated tractor-trailers in CARLA with trailer-aware rewards (ThI1I.173); and FLIP calibrates granular-media sims via Bayesian inference on powder flowability to train lab-automation policies that beat domain randomization 3x.

themes.name: Diffusion models as general infrastructure, not just policies
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is being repurposed beyond action heads: score-based diffusion generates guiding vector fields for geometric path-following control where classical GVFs fail on branching topologies (SGVF), diffusion hybridizes with Mamba/SSM backbones for coarse-to-fine action granularity (DiSPo, +81% success on multi-granularity benchmarks), and video diffusion serves as a zero-shot physics reasoner whose outputs are distilled into 3D object flow for control (Dream2Flow).

methods_shift: Three defaults are visible. (1) For locomotion/whole-body control, RL-in-sim with zero-shot sim2real plus a history-informed online adaptation module is now the unquestioned recipe (Any2Track ThI1I.179, boulder excavation ThI2I.228, tractor-trailers ThI1I.173, crowd navigation TuI1I.4) — model-based whole-body controllers barely appear. (2) For manipulation imitation, diffusion policies are the assumed baseline being extended rather than introduced — latent-action diffusion for cross-embodiment (TuI1I.63), diffusion+Mamba hybrids (DiSPo TuI1I.163), quantized-hand-state diffusion (DQ-RISE WeI2I.204) — displacing plain behavior cloning and ACT-style transformers; VLAs (Octo, OpenVLA, Pi-Zero) are treated as commodity infrastructure to be benchmarked and supported by tooling (Robot Control Stack TuI1I.250). (3) VLMs are displacing hand-written task planners and even encroaching on the control layer via costmap/parameter modulation (LISN TuI1I.234) and tool-calling (TIGeR ThI2I.315). Counter-current: classical optimization is not retreating at the contact/safety layer — contact-implicit MPC took an award-session slot beating learning at general pushing (WeAT1.5), and MPC remains the executor underneath learned guidance everywhere (SaferPath ThI1I.272, GoC-MPC WeI2I.235, CoorGrasp TuBT3.3). In perception, 3DGS has fully displaced NeRF, and foundation depth models calibrated with sparse range are displacing trained-from-scratch depth completion (OASIS-DC WeI1I.332).

notable.why: Pure model-based contact-implicit MPC (C3+, no learned policy) achieves 98% success pushing 33 scanned objects to tight pose tolerances, including 4-object de-cluttering, in real time — an award-session result showing classical trajectory optimization at 'Push Anything' generality.
A sim-trained RL policy controls a real 12-ton hydraulic excavator to extract 0.4-0.7m boulders from varied soil using only 20 LiDAR points per rock, hitting 70% success vs 83% for human operators.

A hardware photonic reservoir computer (semiconductor laser dynamics) predicts UAV residual aerodynamic forces with millisecond training and nanosecond inference, matching or beating TCN/LSTM baselines while training only a ridge-regression readout.
COBALT crowdsourced 7,500+ teleop demonstrations (50+ hours) across nine countries in five days using ordinary smartphones, with a user study showing phones match or beat specialized teleop hardware.

Caspar auto-generates CUDA kernels from symbolic Python expressions and runs bundle adjustment 5-20x faster than the best existing solver with less memory — a co-authored Skydio/NTNU infrastructure release inside SymForce.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: GPU-accelerated symbolic nonlinear optimization (Caspar, released in SymForce) for onboard estimation/bundle adjustment on drones.
Diffusion-guided generalizable enhancement of 3D Gaussian scene reconstructions for closed-loop self-driving sensor simulation.
Triplane-based multi-camera tokenization cutting tokens 72% and inference time 50% for end-to-end autonomous-driving transformer policies.
Cloud-based crowdsourced smartphone teleoperation infrastructure for large-scale robot-learning data collection.
A general humanoid motion-tracking RL stack (Any2Track) deployed zero-shot on Unitree G1 hardware, robust to real-world disturbances.
Wearable exoskeleton system (HumanoidExo) for scalable whole-body humanoid demonstration data collection.
Cross-embodiment latent action spaces so one diffusion policy controls anthropomorphic hands and grippers alike.
Tactile-driven MPC for coordinated multi-contact execution of dexterous grasps (15k sim grasps, 3 robotic hands).
A VLA model that maps language and endoscopic frames to low-level voltage commands for in-vivo magnetic soft robots.
Intention-aware multimodal trajectory prediction, ranked first among LiDAR-free methods on Waymo Motion and deployed on production vehicles.
Reflection-aware Gaussian Splatting for accurate reconstruction of specular objects (AV simulation/perception).
A unified encoder fusing HD/SD vector maps, raster maps, and satellite imagery into BEV features for online mapping and end-to-end planning.
X-band-radar-only place recognition for autonomous maritime vessel navigation.
Confidence-aware structural point-cloud localization (Struct-Loc) with 100x map compression vs COLMAP for industrial environments; also panoramic-video warehouse wireframe mapping (ThI1I.235).
Robot Control Stack — a lean sim-to-real software ecosystem purpose-built for training and deploying VLA/generalist policies (evaluates Octo, OpenVLA, Pi-Zero).
Paddling-propulsion gaits letting wheel-legged AMRs traverse sand where wheels fail.
End-to-end DRL driving policies for articulated tractor-trailer trucks (port logistics) in CARLA.

contrarian.finding: Contact-implicit MPC with zero learning generalizes from first sight to 33 objects and multi-object pushing at 98% success — the kind of open-world generality usually claimed as requiring learned policies, presented in an award session.
Honest negative margin: the sim-trained RL excavation policy reaches 70% success vs 83% for human operators — field-scale RL still loses to skilled humans.

Negative transfer result: established noisy-label learning methods from image classification 'adapt poorly' to LiDAR semantic segmentation under domain shift, requiring a purpose-built approach (DuNe).

End-to-end learned visual navigation models (ViNT, NoMaD) 'frequently fail' in cluttered/narrow indoor settings; success requires wrapping them in a classical safety-constrained optimization + MPC layer.
A spiking neural network beats classical ANNs on IMU velocity estimation (20% lower positioning error) while using 70% less energy — counter to the assumption that SNNs trade accuracy for efficiency.

After a multi-layer sim-to-real controller funnel for gaze-driven neck exoskeletons, 'no single controller is universally preferred' — a generalization negative result pushing toward per-user personalization.

humanoid.signal: Any2Track (Galbot/Tsinghua/Delft): the emerging humanoid recipe is a single general RL motion-tracking policy plus a history-informed adaptation module for online dynamics identification, transferring zero-shot to Unitree G1 and surviving terrain changes, external forces, and physical-property shifts — Unitree G1 is the de facto standard research platform.

HumanoidExo (Midea): wearable exoskeletons are emerging as the cheap whole-body data-collection layer for humanoids — policies learn stand-squat manipulation from 5 real-robot demos plus exo data, and acquire walking from exoskeleton data alone, attacking the humanoid data bottleneck without teleop rigs.

Legged-platform signal: physics-informed neural networks (dynamics-consistent loss) plus composite adaptive control give quadrupeds anticipatory, not reactive, torque compensation for dynamic payloads — a recipe relevant to any walker that must carry varying loads.

For humanoid-style arm+dexterous-hand systems, quantizing high-DoF hand states so they don't dominate the coupled arm-hand action space (DQ-RISE) is a concrete fix for the action-space imbalance problem in whole-upper-body visuomotor policies.

themes.name: Human video as the new robot-data substrate (manipulation without teleoperation)
themes.whats_new: Not just learning-from-demonstration: fully automated pipelines that convert unlabeled, in-the-wild egocentric human videos into robot-format training data. TuI2I.295 (Microsoft Research Asia) auto-segments 1M episodes / 26M frames of hand activity into VLA-aligned atomic tasks with 3D hand + camera motion; WeI1I.222 (Masquerade) inpaints the human arm and overlays a rendered bimanual robot to close the visual embodiment gap, getting 5-6x over baselines from only 50 robot demos per task; WeBT2.6 (EMMA) co-trains egocentric human full-body data with static-robot data to skip mobile teleop entirely; ThI2I.197 (OCRA) adds VGGT 3D priors plus 1M tactile images to transfer from human videos.
themes.share: ~5-6% of batch, but the most coherent agenda in it

themes.name: Diffusion (and now flow matching) as the default policy/planner head
themes.whats_new: Diffusion policies are no longer the contribution — they are the assumed component, showing up in assistive dressing (ThI2LB.10), shared teleoperation (ThI2I.122), skill composition (TuI2I.142, TuI1I.215), exploration planning (ThI1I.156), and decentralized multi-agent IL with centralized-training/decentralized-execution (ThI1I.136). The frontier has moved to (a) flow matching as the cheaper successor (TuI1I.284 DreamFlow, ThI1I.257 conditional latent flow matching), (b) training-free model-based diffusion with safety shields inside the denoising loop (WeI1I.120), and (c) diffusion for perception, e.g. single-frame radar-to-LiDAR point clouds (TuI1I.302).

themes.name: Frozen foundation models bolted into classical robotics stacks via adapters
themes.whats_new: The pattern is no longer fine-tuning: keep DINOv2/CLIP/VGGT/LLM frozen and attach lightweight adapters or symbolic machinery. WeI2I.321 puts two bypass adapters on frozen DINOv2 for altitude-aware place recognition (90% memory cut); ThI2I.147 generalizes semantic mapping with Dense CLIP using text-only training transferred at test time; WeI2I.405 has an LLM emit a Prolog knowledge base that is compiled into an MDP and solved by the Storm model checker; ThI1I.250 (PSALM-V) induces PDDL pre/post-conditions from interaction, doubling ALFRED success (37%→74%) over raw Claude-3.7 planning.

themes.name: Data quality and curation beat data quantity
themes.whats_new: A self-critical turn in imitation learning: ThI2I.153 (QoQ) uses influence functions to score individual demonstrations and beats prior data-selection methods; TuI2I.269 shows retargeting artifacts — not reward engineering — are what break humanoid tracking policies, with open-source GMR nearly matching Unitree's closed-source data; ThI2I.182 shows instrumented objects (sensors embedded in a clothes hanger) lift diffusion-policy success 14-25 points and can distill back into vision-only students; ThI1I.381 replaces costly human demos with NMPC-generated ones at equal quality.

themes.name: Adversarial stress-testing and formal wrappers for learned robot policies
themes.whats_new: Security/verification is catching up to deployment: WeI2I.289 builds viewpoint-consistent 3D adversarial objects (differentiable rendering + EOT) that fool visuomotor policies from moving wrist cameras with black-box transfer; TuI1I.272 (J-DAPT) defends robot VLMs against jailbreaks via multimodal domain adaptation; ThI1I.249 makes million-parameter generative motion planners formally verifiable by wrapping samples in a small certified tracking controller and verifying the closed loop; TuI2I.26 (Bosch/CMU SEAL) and ThI1I.257 generate realistic safety-critical AV scenarios with learned adversaries and flow matching rather than naive adversarial optimization.

methods_shift: Diffusion policies have completed the transition from contribution to commodity — they are the unmarked default IL head across dressing, shared control, multi-agent coordination, and skill learning — and conditional flow matching is already starting to displace them for speed (TuI1I.284, ThI1I.257), while GUIDE (ThI1I.156) explicitly advertises 'reduced denoising steps'. VLA pretraining is the assumed scaling path for manipulation, with the data bottleneck attacked via human video conversion rather than more teleop (TuI2I.295, WeI1I.222, WeBT2.6). For legged/humanoid platforms, massively-parallel RL-in-sim with motion tracking (BeyondMimic-style) plus sim-to-real identification is the settled recipe (TuI2I.269, WeI2I.21, ThI1I.118, ThI1I.259); the contested layer is now upstream data (retargeting) and actuator modeling (WeI2LB.3), not the RL algorithm. Crucially, classical control is not being displaced — it is being recombined: NMPC still owns cranes (TuI2I.66), robotic fish (WeI2I.425), and demo generation for RL (ThI1I.381); CBF-QP remains the default safety layer, now geometry-aware via Bernstein-polynomial SDFs (WeI1I.312) and CLF-synthesized (TuI1I.382); and the strongest grasping result in the batch is a hybrid EBM-prior + ICP/SVGD optimizer that doubles AnyGrasp (ThI2I.96). Frozen foundation features (DINOv2, Dense CLIP, VGGT) with lightweight adapters are displacing task-specific perception training.

notable.why: Microsoft Research Asia converts unscripted egocentric human videos into a 1M-episode, 26M-frame hand-VLA pretraining dataset with zero manual annotation and shows zero-shot transfer to real robots — the most credible 'internet-scale data for manipulation' result in the batch.

Masquerade gets 5-6x improvement on long-horizon bimanual kitchen tasks from only 50 robot demos per task by literally painting a robot over humans in in-the-wild videos, with performance scaling logarithmically in edited-video volume.

Toyota Research Institute drifts a real Toyota Supra at the friction limit and shows online adaptation alone causes spin-outs — active pre-deployment information gathering is required, a rare honest negative result on adaptive control.

Ultrasonic lubrication as an actively switchable grip/glide friction mechanism enables inchworm and ovipositor-style locomotion at >90% efficiency across rigid, granular, and biological-tissue surfaces — a genuinely new actuation primitive.

First viewpoint-consistent 3D adversarial object attack on visuomotor manipulation policies, working under moving wrist cameras with black-box transfer and real-world demonstration — opens a physical attack surface most of the field is ignoring.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Scalable VLA pretraining pipeline for dexterous manipulation fed by automated analysis of in-the-wild human hand videos (1M episodes).

industry_signal.company: Bot Auto / Archer Aviation / Prexa AI
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Production highway localization stack for autonomous trucking (dual-likelihood LiDAR front end + Control-EKF), validated over 1M+ km of road testing, with a 163 km public dataset.
Bayesian last-layer meta-learned vehicle dynamics with active data collection for obstacle-avoidance drifting at the limits of handling, tested on a real Supra.
Safe Model Predictive Diffusion — a training-free diffusion trajectory planner with a safety shield inside the denoising loop for tractor-trailer-class systems.
SEAL: skill-enabled adversarial scenario generation for closed-loop AV validation and robustification.
Miniaturized tendon-driven continuum robot (0.02 mm repeatability) delivering direct laser deposition for in-situ aeroengine blade repair without engine disassembly.
ZERITH Z1 humanoid prototype with a Parameterized Motion Generator for human-like locomotion from compact motion data plus VR teleoperation and sim-to-real motor parameter ID.
FunCanon: pose-aware, functionally-canonicalized action primitives with diffusion policies for category-general industrial manipulation.
Spatio-temporal motion retargeting from hand-held video to quadrupeds, deployed on four different real robots including terrain-aware backflips.
MTRDrive: memory + tool-augmented VLM reasoning for autonomous driving corner cases (SOTA on NavSim, new Roadwork-VLM OOD benchmark).
Conditional Flow-VAE for generating realistic safety-critical traffic scenarios for AV training and benchmarking.
Friction-aware actuator torque models identified under no-load conditions to shrink the sim2real gap when external torque sensing is used.

contrarian.finding: A hybrid learned-EBM-prior + classical ICP/Stein-variational optimizer hits 60.9% grasp success where the pure-learning AnyGrasp gets 31.1% — geometric optimization nearly doubles the flagship learned grasper.

Negative result on online learning: online adaptation alone is insufficient for zero-shot control at the handling limit and can cause spin-outs on a real vehicle; explicit pre-deployment active data collection is required.

Honda/RIKEN report that simply adding data augmentation — the standard RL robustness trick — does not improve imitation learning under visual domain shift; a separate adversarial feature learning plus unsupervised adaptation stage is needed.

Humanoid tracking-policy failures are mostly caused by retargeting artifacts in the reference data, not insufficient reward engineering or domain randomization — fix the data and suppress the reward tuning.
Directly contradicts the scale-is-all-you-need narrative: influence-function-based curation of demonstrations (quality over quantity) beats training on more data in sim and real.

NMPC-generated demonstrations match expensive human demonstrations for deformable-object RL, and the lightweight task-specific system is positioned explicitly against 'computationally intensive large-model approaches'.

Factorized OOD study of driving policies finds naive multi-frame temporal input does not beat the best single-frame baseline, and all non-foundation-model policies collapse below 50% success under three simultaneous distribution shifts.

humanoid.signal: Stanford's GMR open-source retargeter nearly closes the gap to Unitree's closed-source motion dataset for RL motion tracking (BeyondMimic training, LAFAN1) — the humanoid data moat is in retargeting quality, and it is being commoditized.

Zerith Z1 humanoid: parameterized motion generation replaces large mocap datasets for human-like locomotion, paired with optimization-based sim-to-real motor parameter identification and VR teleoperation — a startup recipe for deployable humanoid control without big data.

Compliant/articulated-soft humanoids (BRUCE) get push recovery via iterative-learning CoM impedance control with DCM convergence proofs — no precise dynamic model or heavy online optimization, validated on real hardware.

Passive-wheel quadruped skating via model-free RL cuts cost of transport 70.9% versus standard legged locomotion (1.5 m/s on real hardware) — energy efficiency argument for hybrid wheel-leg morphologies in urban use.

RAI Institute/ETH retarget noisy hand-held-camera human video to four different real quadrupeds including box backflips — morphology-bridging kinematic+dynamic retargeting is becoming platform-agnostic.

AIDIN Robotics isolates actuator friction torque under no-load conditions to fix sim2real torque mismatch — actuator-level identification, not policy-level domain randomization, framed as the sim2real bottleneck.

Impact-robust posture optimization embedded in a TSID whole-body controller cuts post-impact state spikes up to 45% on humanoid/quadruped models — kinematic redundancy as a free robustness resource for contact-rich humanoid work.

themes.name: VLAs hit the deployability wall: pruning, memory, streaming, and failure detection
themes.whats_new: Papers no longer propose new VLA backbones — they retrofit them for real deployment. LightVLA (ThI2I.69) does differentiable Gumbel-softmax visual token pruning and finds pruning IMPROVES success (+2.9%) while cutting FLOPs 59%; Notes-To-Self (ThI1I.224) adds a language scratchpad to give stateless VLAs spatial/temporal memory for non-Markovian tasks; StreamVLN (ThI2I.263) uses slow-fast context with KV-cache reuse and 3D-aware token pruning for real-time streaming VLN; I-FailSense (ThI2I.75) trains lightweight heads on internal VLM layers to detect semantic-misalignment failures; NavSpace (WeI2I.210) benchmarks 22 agents and exposes weak spatial reasoning.

themes.name: Imitation policies get fast and smooth: speed/latency is the new axis of competition
themes.whats_new: The bottleneck has shifted from 'can it do the task' to 'can it do it at human speed without jerk'. ABPolicy (ThI2I.158) moves flow-matching into a B-spline control-point action space with asynchronous inference to kill inter-chunk discontinuities; ALOHA Lightning (WeI2I.216) collects near-human-speed kinesthetic demos on a backdrivable bimanual rig and hits 80%+ success at human speed from 50 demos; UMI-on-Air (WeI2I.220 id WeI1I.220) injects controller tracking-cost gradients into diffusion sampling so an embodiment-agnostic UMI policy becomes feasible on an aerial manipulator at inference time.

themes.name: RL-in-sim for legged robots, now with injected structure instead of pure reward shaping
themes.whats_new: PPO-in-Isaac-style training is assumed; the novelty is what structure gets bolted on: A2CF (ThI2I.317) trains a second 'assistive force' agent that acts like a coach's hands and fades out (30% faster convergence on walking/dancing/backflips); NaviGait (TuI2I.257) has RL select and minimally morph trajectory-optimization gait libraries, drastically simplifying rewards; MILD (WeI2I.388) builds a discrete-element deformable-terrain contact solver into training for bipeds on yielding ground; START (WeI2I.402) reconstructs explicit local heightmaps from onboard vision as an intermediate representation for sparse footholds; TuI2I.289 wraps RL inside Bayesian-optimization hardware co-design for quadruped skating.

themes.name: Degradation-proof multi-sensor odometry, with event cameras and 3DGS entering the fusion stack
themes.whats_new: Event cameras graduate from niche to fused SLAM modality: FAST-LIEO (TuI1I.3) claims the first tight LiDAR+IMU+event+RGB fusion; MotionGS-SLAM (WeI2I.316) uses event streams to modulate Gaussian-splat rasterization, modeling blur as a forward problem instead of deblurring. Meanwhile STEAM-LIVO (WeAT4.4) targets explicit sensor-degradation resilience in aquatic-terrestrial scenes, GeoGS-SLAM (WeI1I.140) seeds 3DGS maps from feed-forward geometry foundation models on uncalibrated RGB, and ProbPer-LiLo (WeI1I.400) does lifelong mapping with probabilistic quasi-static object classification.

themes.name: Diffusion spreads beyond action policies into maps, scenes, and grasps — steered at inference time
themes.whats_new: The pattern is generation plus inference-time guidance instead of retraining: Octree Latent Semantic Diffusion (WeI1I.306) does scene completion/extension via latent inpainting/outpainting conditioned on partial LiDAR, zero-shot cross-domain; dPWM (WeI2I.111) uses DDPM map prediction to give exploration planners long-term foresight (18.5% shorter paths); DAGDiff (TuI2I.207) denoises dual-arm grasp pairs in SE(3)xSE(3) with stability/collision classifier guidance rather than region priors.

themes.name: Cheap morphological sensing: the gripper body becomes the sensor
themes.whats_new: Instead of dense taxel arrays, sensing is embedded in compliant structure: FORTE (TuI2I.420) reads air channels inside 3D-printed fin-ray fingers for 0.2N force accuracy and 100ms slip detection (92% success on raspberries/chips); ThI1I.68 puts a speaker+microphone inside a jamming gripper cavity, using acoustics through the soft body for size (2.6mm), orientation (0.6 deg) and material ID without compromising compliance; AFOP-ML (ThI1I.317) attacks tactile data scarcity with meta-learning (88.7% in 36-way-1-shot).

methods_shift: RL-in-sim (PPO variants, Isaac-style massively parallel training) is now the unquestioned default for legged locomotion — no paper argues for it, they argue about what structure to add (gait libraries in NaviGait TuI2I.257, assistive-force curricula in A2CF ThI2I.317, physics-grounded terrain solvers in MILD WeI2I.388). In manipulation, diffusion/flow-matching policies have displaced plain behavior cloning and ACT as the default imitation backbone, and the frontier has moved to making them real-time and feasible: B-spline action spaces with asynchronous inference (ABPolicy ThI2I.158), controller-gradient guidance at sampling time (UMI-on-Air WeI1I.220), classifier-guided grasp diffusion (DAGDiff TuI2I.207). VLAs/VLMs are the assumed layer for anything language-conditioned, with effort shifting to token pruning (LightVLA ThI2I.69), memory scratchpads (ThI1I.224), and failure detection (I-FailSense ThI2I.75). Classical control is not dying but hybridizing: MPPI absorbs RL reward-shaping insights to go reference-free (ThI2I.241), differentiable LQR layers sit inside perception networks (D2MFusion TuI2I.422), and HJ-reachability safety value functions get amortized into observation-conditioned networks (OCR filter TuI2I.380). CBFs remain the default safety vocabulary (ThI2I.403, ThI2I.412). In perception, feed-forward 3D geometry foundation models are displacing multi-stage SfM pipelines as priors (GeoGS-SLAM WeI1I.140, ColonAdapter ThI2I.419), and event cameras are moving from curiosity to fused modality (FAST-LIEO TuI1I.3, MotionGS-SLAM WeI2I.316, ThI1I.187).

notable.why: Northwestern shows a robot that redesigns itself during its lifetime by deliberately thrashing redundant links until they break off ('kinematic self-destruction'), controlled by a single autoregressive sequence model that transfers to reality and generalizes to unseen kinematic trees — a genuine first.

ALOHA Lightning (Stanford) closes the speed gap with humans: 80%+ success on folding shorts and battery insertion at or near human speed from only 50 kinesthetic demos, attacking the most visible weakness of learned manipulation.

LightVLA finds that pruning 59% of FLOPs from a VLA via differentiable token selection INCREASES task success by 2.9% — efficiency and performance are not a trade-off, which upends the assumption that more visual tokens help.

A non-invasive SSVEP brain-computer interface achieves 4-DOF continuous drone control with a brain-to-hand ratio near 1 — noninvasive EEG matching manual joystick performance is an unusually strong claim.

Autonomous root canal surgery: a robot guides a thin flexible endodontic file through a curved canal with real-time patient-pose tracking and file-deflection compensation, validated experimentally — surgical autonomy in a regime where errors mean fractured files in teeth.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Memory-augmented VLAs (language scratchpad for non-Markovian manipulation) — consistent with Qualcomm pushing on-device/edge robot AI.
LightVLA token-pruning to run VLAs on resource-constrained platforms in real time — an EV maker investing in efficient embodied-AI inference.
Event-inspired perception that makes imitation-from-video robust to visual domain shift without domain randomization.
HD-map-free automated driving: learned location-specific latent behavior priors that match or beat HD-map baselines on Lyft Level 5.
Robust multimodal dynamic-object segmentation for static scene reconstruction — perception for their delivery-robot/AV fleet.

industry_signal.company: Chongqing Afari Intelligent Drive + Moonshot AI
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: KiGRAS, a kinematics-constrained autoregressive agent simulator for AD that ranked first on the Waymo SimAgents/WOMD leaderboard with fewer parameters.
CRAFT: bimanual long-horizon cable routing with a low-friction roller caging gripper for assembly/manufacturing automation.
Dynamical-systems control for robust dual-arm object handover on warehouse-style manipulators.

contrarian.finding: Explicitly titled 'Beyond Domain Randomization': replacing RGB with event-like temporal-gradient representations eliminates appearance entirely, beating the standard randomization recipe which the authors call computationally expensive and inherently reactive.

A hand-derived analytical Sliding Pivot model of throwing-release dynamics matches an implicit-integration simulator within 10% while running 20x faster — physics modeling, not learning, fixes a contact problem where the standard rigid-body-plus-patch-friction model is shown to be pathological.

Classical sampling-based MPPI with a gate-progress objective borrowed from RL reward shaping rivals or exceeds learned and reference-based approaches in drone racing — no policy network needed, just a better objective in a classical solver.

A purely geometric adaptive patch-wise region-growing plane segmenter runs at 600+ FPS on a mid-range CPU with no parallelization and beats the SOTA (1.46x) on both speed and quality — no deep learning involved.

Negative-leaning late-breaking result: federated multi-robot learning under realistic non-IID goal distributions degrades model performance and destabilizes training relative to centralized learning across algorithms.

Documents that existing robust CBF formulations are so conservative they become infeasible or demand excessive control effort under state uncertainty, requiring online parameter adaptation to be usable at all.

humanoid.signal: H-Zero (Shanghai AI Lab/SJTU): cross-humanoid locomotion pretraining yields a base policy that transfers few-shot to unseen humanoids (and upright quadrupeds) within 30 minutes of fine-tuning — embodiment-generic locomotion checkpoints are emerging, which directly cuts the per-robot RL tuning cost for a new humanoid design.

A2CF assistive-force curriculum (dual-agent: a 'coach' applies state-dependent forces then fades): 30% faster convergence and 40% lower failure on humanoid walking, dancing, and backflips, validated on real hardware — a reusable recipe for hard humanoid skills without reward-engineering pain.

NaviGait (Georgia Tech): RL navigates an offline trajectory-optimization gait library instead of learning from scratch — drastically simpler rewards, faster training, motions that stay close to interpretable references; a middle path between HZD-style optimization and end-to-end RL for bipeds.

MILD: a discrete-element deformable-terrain contact solver inside RL training lets bipeds adapt online to real yielding surfaces — rigid-floor sim is being called out as the limiting factor for bipedal sim2real on soft ground.

Bi-level differentiable-estimator calibration that jointly learns noise covariances AND kinematic parameters, validated on humanoids — replaces hand-tuned EKF covariances, a practical pain point for any new humanoid state estimator.

UC Berkeley/UPenn: as human-inspired end-effectors narrow the embodiment gap, human motion-prediction models run zero-shot on robot keypoints and substitute for dense rewards, retargeting, and curricula — human video data becoming directly usable for humanoid sensorimotor training.

HMC (CUHK-Shenzhen/UCSD): heterogeneous meta-control combining learned policies with compliance/impedance control for contact-rich humanoid loco-manipulation — whole-body contact tasks, not just walking, are the next humanoid battleground.

themes.name: Human data as the new training substrate for manipulation
themes.whats_new: Moving past 'human video helps robustness' to direct motion-level zero-shot transfer: MotionTrans (WeI1I.96) transfers motions for 9 tasks zero-shot from VR human data via weighted cotraining; Aina (TuI2I.111, Meta) learns multi-fingered hand policies from Aria Gen 2 smart-glasses data with NO robot data at all; TREAD (WeI1I.199) skips new collection entirely by VLM-relabelling existing datasets; ThI2I.234 uses offline RL to absorb suboptimal/play data into IL policies.

themes.name: Dual-system VLM/VLA architectures (fast executor + slow reasoner)
themes.whats_new: The System-1/System-2 split is becoming an explicit architecture pattern: G0 (ThI2I.82) couples a VLM planner with a VLA executor trained via a three-stage curriculum; FASIONAD (TuI1I.295) adds a Laplace uncertainty gate so the slow VLM is queried only when needed, cutting latency; WeI1LB.7 wraps a weak VLA (9% baseline on LIBERO-10) with a GPT-4o planner to hit 90%. Proprioception is also being injected into MLLM planners (Proprio-MLLM, WeI2I.117).

themes.name: Safety machinery moving from runtime filters into training and inference-time editing
