themes.whats_new: CBF-RL (ThI1I.217) enforces control barrier functions during RL training so the deployed humanoid policy needs no online safety filter; LAE (WeI2I.135) imports activation steering from LLMs to edit a frozen policy's latent activations at inference, cutting collisions ~90% on real Crazyflies; deadlock-aware CBF stacking (WeI1I.415) and viability-based torque constraints (TuI2I.305) round out a clear 'certifiable wrappers for learned controllers' subfield.

themes.name: SLAM/odometry rebuilt on new representations: 3DGS, NeRF, continuous-time splines
themes.whats_new: Gaussian splatting and radiance fields are now inside the SLAM loop, not just post-hoc reconstruction: GauSem-SLAM (WeI2I.94) does semantic 3DGS submaps with DINOv2-based loop closure; SiLVR (WeAT3.8) adds principled epistemic uncertainty to lidar-visual NeRF at 20,000 m^2 scale; RESPLE (TuI2I.54) replaces error-state EKFs with recursive B-spline estimation; cubemap intensity LIO (WeI1I.60) and online incremental dynamic SLAM (WeI1I.418, 5x speedup) push classical pipelines toward degraded/dynamic scenes.

themes.name: RL-in-sim + domain randomization as the default recipe for any embodiment
themes.whats_new: The Isaac-style massively parallel sim + domain randomization + sim2real recipe is now applied to exotic platforms as routine engineering: inverted blimps (WeI1I.100), quadruped skateboarding via phase-conditioned FiLM (TuI1I.301), individual-wheel-drive drifting at the friction limit (ThI1I.51), wheeled-bipedal stair climbing with contact-gated behavior cloning (TuI1LB.12), whole-arm deformable-cluster clearing with zero-shot transfer to real branches (WeI1I.411). What's new is hybridization: model-based oracles supervising residual RL (WeI1I.435) and CBFs inside training.

themes.name: Benchmarks, datasets, and rigor as first-class contributions
themes.whats_new: Beyond yet-another-dataset: TuI2I.29 runs 5,040 grasping experiments across two labs and reports sim/real discrepancies and repeatability; TOPO-Bench (TuI1I.275) introduces the first quantitative measure of perceptual aliasing; Bi3 (WeI1I.241) is a bicultural (USA/France) two-platform social-navigation dataset; DMRP-Bench (ThI2I.47) and DualTHOR (WeI2I.117) target multi-robot dynamic planning and dual-arm humanoids respectively.

methods_shift: RL-in-sim with domain randomization (mostly Isaac Lab/Sim, PPO/TD3/SAC) is the unmarked default for locomotion and contact-rich control — papers no longer argue for it, they just use it; the novelty has moved to hybridization, with model-based controllers demoted to 'base policies' or 'oracles' supervising residual RL (WeI1I.435) and CBFs folded into training rather than runtime (ThI1I.217). In manipulation, diffusion policies are the assumed baseline being upgraded rather than introduced — via geometric algebra encoders (hPGA-DP, WeBT2.3) and tactile conditioning (DexFruit, WeI1I.437) — while flow matching is starting to displace diffusion for trajectory generation (MMFP, WeI1I.27). VLAs are becoming standard plumbing for generalization, but their weakness as standalone executors is acknowledged, driving fast-slow dual systems where a VLM/LLM plans and the VLA or E2E policy executes (ThI2I.82, TuI1I.295, WeI1LB.7). Offline RL is being repositioned as a tool to robustify imitation learning with non-expert data (ThI2I.234, TuI2I.76) rather than a standalone paradigm. A notable import channel: techniques from the LLM world (activation steering -> LAE WeI2I.135; fast/slow reasoning; VLM data relabelling WeI1I.199). Meanwhile classical estimation is not retreating — iterated EKFs, factor graphs, splines, Hough transforms, and dynamic inversion still own state estimation and tight real-time control (TuI2I.54, WeI1I.346, TuI2I.389, TuI2I.401).

notable.why: First transfer of LLM-style activation steering to robot control: editing a frozen policy's latent activations at inference cuts cumulative collisions ~90% on real Crazyflies without touching weights or retraining.
Meta shows multi-fingered hand policies learned purely from Aria Gen 2 smart-glasses recordings of humans in the wild, deployed with zero robot data, no RL, no simulation, and no online corrections.

A Unitree G1 humanoid climbs stairs and avoids obstacles in the real world with safety internalized at training time via CBFs — no runtime safety filter at all, inverting the standard safe-RL deployment recipe.
A tensegrity robot that jumps 1.93 body lengths vertically with directional steering and survives 21.5 m (35 body-length) falls, field-tested in a planetary analog.
Normal-flow-based event-camera motion segmentation claims a ~800x speedup over the open-source state of the art while staying accurate, turning a batch task into a real-time one.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Large-scale open-world robot dataset collected on a single consistent embodiment in real homes/workplaces, plus G0, a dual-system VLM-planner + VLA-executor foundation model with a three-stage training curriculum.

Aina: pipeline turning Aria Gen 2 smart-glasses recordings of everyday human activity into deployable multi-fingered robot manipulation policies — positioning their glasses as the robot-data acquisition device.
ObjectVLA: synthetic image-text data pipeline (Search2Scene) that lets home-appliance robots generalize manipulation to 100 novel objects (64% success) without new demonstrations.
Offline-RL machinery to robustify imitation-learned manipulation policies with cheap non-expert/play data instead of more expert teleop.
FASIONAD: uncertainty-gated fast-slow autonomous driving stack where a VLM reasoner is invoked only on detected uncertainty, cutting collisions 28% versus end-to-end baselines.
SEF-MAP: mixture-of-experts camera-LiDAR fusion for HD map prediction that stays robust when one modality degrades (low light, sparse points).

industry_signal.company: Toyota Motor East Japan (with Tohoku University)
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Classical Hough-transform geometric registration for AMR docking in live factories: ±5.06 mm / ±1.11 deg with 100% success over 72 trials.
CUBE-LIO: LiDAR-inertial odometry using cubemap-projected intensity for robustness in geometrically degenerate spaces, sensor-agnostic across spinning and solid-state LiDARs.
Motion Manifold Flow Primitives: flow-matching-based movement primitives for language-conditioned trajectory generation.
RoA-Planner: rotatable-area collision checking for fast SE(2) path planning of rectangular-footprint robots in dense, narrow industrial spaces.

contrarian.finding: For decoding multi-finger motions from lightmyography signals, a random forest significantly outperforms both CNNs and MLPs — classical ML beating deep learning on a real sensing task.
Pure classical geometry (Hough line detection + global registration) achieves ±5 mm, 100%-success factory docking, a 70% error reduction over existing methods — no learning anywhere in the pipeline.

A classical forward-backward velocity-profile algorithm (FBGA) matches optimal-control baselines within 0.11-0.36% on lap time while being up to three orders of magnitude faster — hand-crafted structure beats generic optimization.
A standalone VLA scores only 9% on decomposable LIBERO-10 tasks, evidence that VLAs memorize training data rather than understand task semantics; the fix is an LLM planner on top, not a bigger VLA.

Embodied mechanical intelligence over learning: a scoop-shaped gripper add-on eliminates the need to learn non-prehensile pushing actions entirely and reduces RL training steps — morphology doing the work policy learning usually does.

A 5,040-trial cross-lab grasping benchmark documents systematic discrepancies between simulation and real-robot results and shows learned vs analytical algorithm rankings flip with lighting, camera noise, and gripper choice.

humanoid.signal: EAGLE (Shanghai AI Lab): one unified whole-body RL policy controls heterogeneous humanoids — Unitree H1, G1, Fourier N1 — via iterative generalist-specialist distillation, no per-robot reward tuning; cross-embodiment fleet-level control is becoming real.
Unitree G1 walks stairs and avoids obstacles safely with CBF constraints baked into RL training, removing the runtime safety filter — a cleaner deployment recipe for humanoid locomotion.

TU Wien locomotion recipe: DCM planner + whole-body controller as base policy, residual RL supervised by a privileged model-based oracle — sample-efficient torque-level bipedal walking without heavy reward shaping.

DualTHOR: a dedicated dual-arm humanoid simulator with contingency mechanics, plus Proprio-MLLM showing stock MLLMs fail at dual-arm embodied planning unless proprioception is encoded in (+19.75% planning).
Cost floor dropping: integer-quantized SAC/TD3 policies with a GRU encoder run a low-cost quadruped entirely on an ESP32-S3 microcontroller using proprioception only — no GPU anywhere in deployment.

Wheeled-bipedal (2W2L) stair climbing solved in Isaac Lab via contact-event-gated PPO + behavior cloning from a frozen teacher, with no terrain sensors — sparse wheel-contact events used as the curriculum signal.

CareBot-H: a humanoid nursing robot doing real patient transfer with biomimetic human-scale arms, distributed tactile skin, and tactile-informed ZMP trajectory deformation — humanoids entering physical care tasks.

themes.name: Open-vocabulary language maps go online and real-time
themes.whats_new: CLIP/VLM features embedded in maps is no longer offline batch processing: implicit neural language fields replace explicit per-voxel feature storage (LAMP), object-level CLIP aggregation with learned descriptor merging runs online inside SLAM with loop closure (OVO, FindAnything), and dual global/local map representations handle dynamic scene changes during navigation (DualMap). Memory footprint and onboard deployability (MAVs, edge devices) are now the explicit benchmarks, not just segmentation mIoU.

themes.name: VLMs/LLMs as the high-level brain, deliberately kept out of the control loop
themes.whats_new: The division of labor is crystallizing: VLMs handle 'where and what', low-level policies handle 'how'. PEEK fine-tunes VLMs to emit point-based paths and masks overlaid on observations (policy-agnostic, 41.4x real-world gain); Waymo applies RL fine-tuning to MLLMs with token-level planning rewards rather than next-token imitation; Select2Plan gets planning entirely training-free via VQA + in-context retrieval; GLaMP and Fraunhofer's ontology work compile LLM output into verifiable behavior trees instead of trusting end-to-end execution.

themes.name: Manipulation reaches beyond vision: tactile and audio as first-class policy inputs
themes.whats_new: Not new sensors per se, but new integration patterns: contact audio added to a VLA via AudioCLIP + LoRA (Audio-VLA), contrastive sim-to-real alignment of tactile arrays enabling zero-shot transfer of GPU-trained RL (ConTact, Tencent), tactile shear-field representations transferring policies to robots that lack torque sensing (Built Different), and robot-free human-worn visuo-tactile data collection at 3M-image scale (FreeTacMan).

themes.name: Generative action policies slim down: flow matching + lightweight backbones
themes.whats_new: Diffusion policies are the incumbent being optimized away: flow matching replaces multi-step denoising, and UNet backbones are swapped for RWKV/KAN hybrids with 86.8% fewer parameters (KAN-We-Flow), conditional flow matching fused with optical-flow streams (MTP), and cascaded diffusion that predicts object motion first and conditions action generation on it as a plug-in module (MBA).

themes.name: Sampling-based MPC matures into the default control substrate
themes.whats_new: MPPI-family controllers are being hardened rather than invented: sensitivity-aware MPPI with nested Monte Carlo disturbance rollouts for quadrotors (SA-MPPI), the Trajectory Bundle Method unifying MPPI with sequential convex programming to recover constraints and multiple shooting derivative-free, GPU-parallel information-theoretic LMPC with normalizing-flow value functions (SIT-LMPC), and neural infinite-horizon value functions as MPC terminal costs for global stability (TuI1I.15).

themes.name: Generative editing of existing demos replaces collecting new ones
themes.whats_new: Instead of more teleoperation, papers surgically rewrite the data they have: LLM-guided inpainting to replace/restyle/remove distractors with action labels preserved (NICE), physically-based inverse rendering plus fine-tuned Stable Video Diffusion to relight whole demonstration videos (+38.75% under unseen lighting), and physics-informed GANs synthesizing microscope imagery for microrobot pose training.

methods_shift: The default stack is bifurcating by altitude. At the top, pre-trained VLMs/LLMs are now the assumed planner — fine-tuned (PEEK, MAGNIFIED via RLFT), prompted training-free (Select2Plan, ProVox), or compiled into behavior trees for verifiability (GLaMP, ThI1I.240) — displacing both hand-built task planners and end-to-end policies for high-level reasoning. At the policy level, diffusion is the incumbent and flow matching is the insurgent: papers treat 'diffusion action head' as the baseline to accelerate (KAN-We-Flow cuts the UNet, MTP uses conditional flow matching), a displacement of the displacement. In control, GPU-parallel sampling-based MPC (MPPI variants: SA-MPPI, SIT-LMPC, Trajectory Bundle Method, C-ROVER, ThI2I.274) is displacing derivative-based MPC as the default for nonlinear/contact-rich problems, with learned value functions patching the short-horizon weakness. In perception/mapping, CLIP-feature open-vocabulary maps are displacing closed-set semantic segmentation as the standard map payload. Meanwhile classical methods aren't dead — they're getting GPU/SIMD-accelerated (AORRTC), formally verified (RoboPrec, Edge-Theorem LPV), or deployed on Mars (ENav) — surviving precisely where guarantees and compute budgets matter.

notable.why: JPL documents the actual flight algorithm that let Perseverance increase average daily Mars driving distance by an order of magnitude over Curiosity — on a single-core CPU, with ~90% of 32.1 km driven autonomously.

PEEK claims a 41.4x real-world improvement for a 3D policy trained only in simulation by having a VLM overlay end-effector paths and attention masks onto observations — an unusually large, policy-agnostic gain.
A single adversarially-textured sphere placed in view drops Pi0 and RDT task success by 31-40% (near zero in complex scenes) in both sim and real — a physical universal attack on flagship VLAs.

KINESIS controls musculoskeletal humanoids with up to 290 muscles from 1.8 hours of motion data and the learned muscle activations correlate with real human EMG — bridging robotics RL and biomechanics.

RoboPrec auto-generates fixed-point code for rigid-body dynamics that is 122x faster than double on embedded processors while carrying mathematically guaranteed error bounds lower than float's worst case.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: RL fine-tuning (RLFT) of multimodal LLMs for AV motion planning — token-level planning rewards instead of next-token imitation, cutting off-road rate 38.9% over SFT on Waymo Open Motion Dataset.
Convex, differentiable contact models (Hunt & Crossley + Coulomb friction) shipped in open-source Drake for sim-to-real manipulation at interactive rates.
Language-in, simulation-out AV test scenario orchestration: foundation models translate natural language into constraints solved for ego-reactive closed-loop test cases.
OpenPyRo-A1: a ~$14k open bimanual humanoid platform with 0.2mm repeatability, 5kg/arm payload, and a pip-installable Python control stack for embodied AI data collection.
High-speed GPU tactile simulation plus contrastive sim-to-real alignment (ConTact) enabling zero-shot transfer of tactile-only RL manipulation policies.
World-model-based failure classification and OOD anomaly detection with conformal prediction thresholds, deployed in real time on Boston Dynamics Spot for industrial gauge inspection.
Crowd-sourced HD map generation from production cars with only monocular camera + consumer GNSS/IMU, using Bayesian B-spline fusion across drives.
NICE: LLM + generative-inpainting scene surgery on existing robot demos to robustify VLA/VLM manipulation policies without new data collection.
Streaming spiking-neural-network LiDAR object detection — 95% memory reduction, 25x less energy on neuromorphic hardware for automotive perception.

contrarian.finding: The most impressive autonomous driving result in the batch is pure classical robotics — two-stage path selection with conservative kinematic-settling approximation on a single-core CPU, no learning anywhere — and it runs on Mars.

A classical sampling planner (RRT-Connect extended via the AO-x meta-algorithm) solves high-DoF problems in milliseconds where state-of-the-art asymptotically-optimal planners fail in seconds — speed came from algorithm design and SIMD, not learning.
A training-free VLM pipeline (VQA + in-context retrieval) beats end-to-end trained navigation models by ~24%, undercutting the case for task-specific policy training.
Flagship VLAs (Pi0, RDT) are brittle enough that one optimized-texture sphere in view collapses task success — a direct counter to the VLA-robustness narrative.

Even with physics-informed wave-optics rendering inside the GAN, the synthetic-data-trained pose estimator still trails the real-data-trained one by ~5% — an honest quantification that sim-to-real gaps persist after sophisticated domain adaptation.

Stable walking achieved with constant torque drive and 19th-century-style mechanical hip stoppers — gait stability from mechanics, with analytical singular-value proofs, no controller learning at all.

humanoid.signal: Cost floor keeps dropping: Huawei's OpenPyRo-A1 bimanual humanoid is ~$14k with 0.2mm repeatability and 5kg payload per arm, with a pip-installable Python control stack — directly relevant pricing benchmark for low-cost humanoid platforms.

Locomotion recipe beyond torque control: model-free RL imitation on musculoskeletal models with up to 290 muscles, trained on only 1.8h of mocap, producing EMG-plausible activations — points at overactuated/compliant actuation as the next humanoid control frontier.

Legged platforms are exiting the lab commercially: ANYmal surveying 1-ha forest plots in under 30 minutes with 2cm tree-diameter accuracy over 1.5 years of field trials in three countries — plus candid lessons that state estimation and hardware maturity remain the bottlenecks.

Hardware-first stability: mechanical stoppers at the hip plus constant torque yield provably stable wheel-gait walking — a reminder that passive mechanics can substitute for control complexity (and cost) in legged design.

Quadruped planners are starting to optimize foot-end terrain features (via sparse variational GPs on point clouds) rather than body-center paths, trading shortest-path metrics for Z-axis momentum stability on rough terrain.
Gaussian-process gait optimization used to cut physical trial counts on a cable-driven soft quadruped — sample-efficient hardware-in-the-loop tuning rather than sim-first RL.

themes.name: End-to-end driving gets a reality check: uncertainty, corridors, and benchmark realism
themes.whats_new: The novelty is no longer the e2e architecture itself but fixing its trust problem: explicit uncertainty heads (Laplace regressors over both static map and dynamic agents in WeI1I.135), safety-interpretable intermediate representations (spatio-temporal corridors as differentiable constraints in WeI1I.3), DPO-style preference finetuning of motion forecasters using a VLM as zero-shot rater (ThI1I.181), and replacing IDM traffic agents with learned reactive agents (SMART) to expose inflated planner scores (ThI1I.123).

themes.name: Humanoid/legged RL anchored to model-based or human-motion priors
themes.whats_new: Pure reward-shaped RL is being abandoned in favor of tracking dynamically-feasible references: DDP trajectory optimization generating contact-consistent, torque-annotated references for RL tracking on Digit (TuI1I.344), a 'real-world-ready' primitive skill space distilled into a unified latent for whole-body reaching (TuI1I.429), phase-aware AMP-style human priors with automatic breakout into task optimization (WeI1I.235), and a learned world model + sampling MPC for contact-seeking (not contact-avoiding) humanoid behavior from ego depth (WeI1I.268).

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs as planners, wrapped in deterministic executors and safety layers
themes.whats_new: The pattern has converged: the LLM never touches actuators directly. Offline LLM planning compiled into FSM execution with schema validation, dry-run simulation, and human-in-the-loop exception handling for nuclear-industry manipulation (WeI1LB.23); a dual-process design pairing a deliberative multimodal LLM planner with a Video-LLM for continuous monitoring and replanning triggers (TuI1I.318); voice-commanded variable-autonomy assistance for bimanual teleoperation (WeI1I.371); and LLMs as generative operators inside an evolutionary morphology search loop (ThI1I.189).

themes.name: Neural scene representations (3DGS/NeRF) pushed into sensor-degraded robotic regimes
themes.whats_new: 3DGS/NeRF work has moved past clean benchmark scenes to robot-specific failure modes: frequency-domain denoising for low-light 3DGS with 46% storage reduction for onboard deployment (TuI2I.190), unsupervised 4D-superpoint instance decomposition of dynamic urban scenes from RGB+LiDAR without bounding boxes (ThI2I.84), and a NeRF built purely from mmWave radar range-angle heatmaps for 6DoF pose in smoke/fog conditions (ThI1I.219).

themes.name: Structure-exploiting classical optimization quietly delivering order-of-magnitude speedups
themes.whats_new: Instead of learning replacements, several papers mine algebraic structure for speed: variable projection with gauge-symmetry handling giving 2-35x faster SLAM/SfM solves (WeAT3.3), B-spline gradient sparsity and closed-form derivatives giving a 26.9x trajectory-optimization speedup on a UR5e (ThI2I.290), a faster algebraic-connectivity solver for graph sparsification (WeI1I.195), and closed-form spatio-temporal-tube safety replacing online CBF-QP optimization (ThI1I.273).

themes.name: Digital twins and automated asset/data factories as the sim2real bottleneck-breaker
themes.whats_new: The scarce resource is no longer the simulator but simulation-ready content and calibrated dynamics: automatic affordance annotation (fluid sources, heat emitters) on Objaverse-XL meshes via DINOv2/TRELLIS distance fields for BEHAVIOR-1K (ThI1I.340), patient-specific cardiac digital twins generating training data for autonomous catheterization transferred to physical phantoms (ThAT2.8), likelihood-free inference posteriors over deformable-object physics driving domain randomization (TuI1I.12), and ocean/road digital twins coupled to live in-situ data (TuI2LB.14, WeI1I.384).

methods_shift: RL-in-sim on Isaac Gym/Lab is now the unmarked default for anything legged or dexterous (TuI1I.344, WeI1I.235, WeI1I.356, ThI2I.83, WeI1I.335), but naked RL is being displaced by RL-as-tracker: the policy tracks references from DDP trajectory optimization (Opt2Skill), human motion priors (AMPCO), or pre-validated primitive skill spaces (R2S2). Diffusion models have become the default generative engine wherever multimodal outputs matter — joint human trajectory prediction inside a bilevel MPC (WeI1I.48/SICNav-Diffusion), anchor-free trajectory planning for driving (ThI2I.435), depth completion in endoscopy (ThI1I.111) — displacing CVAEs/GANs without comment. VLAs appear but the frontier work is post-hoc surgery on them rather than new ones (training-free attention realignment of OpenVLA/FLOWER in ThI2I.321). LLMs/VLMs have settled into the planner/judge slot with deterministic FSM or MPC executors underneath (WeI1LB.23, TuI1I.318, ThI1I.181). Meanwhile classical MPC, factor-graph optimization, and closed-form control are not being displaced — they are being hybridized as the safety/execution layer (bilevel MPC safety filters, constraint-consistent torque control in TuAT1.5, Koopman-linearized QP safety in ThBT3.6) and are notably winning on speed (WeAT3.3, ThI2I.290) and, in driving, on robustness (PDM beating imitation-learned planners in TuI2I.72).

notable.why: Shows that giving planners PERFECT future predictions does not improve driving performance, and that a simple rule-based lane-follower (PDM) beats imitation-learned planners at proposal generation — undermining the core premise of integrated prediction-and-planning.

First evaluation of 14 nuPlan planners against learned reactive traffic agents, finding IDM-based benchmarks systematically overestimate performance and that all learned planners degrade abruptly in edge cases while rule-based ones stay sane.
A 32-gram single-actuator samara-inspired monocopter achieves 26 minutes of position-controlled hover at 9.1 g/W — an endurance/efficiency point far beyond comparable micro air vehicles.

First acoustic detection of drone package deliveries (e.g., prison contraband drops) from a ground microphone array, identifying 96% of delivery events at up to 100 m using blade-passing-frequency changes alone.

An LLM-arbitrated variable-autonomy assistant for single-operator bimanual teleoperation claims a 240.8% success-rate increase over solo teleoperation and even beats two-human dyadic teleoperation by 69.9%.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: ByteWrist, a compact three-stage parallel anthropomorphic robotic wrist for narrow-space and dual-arm manipulation, benchmarked against Kinova-based systems — hardware for their manipulation robot stack.
Production robotic welding: real-time seam tracking via constrained ICP against a prescan prior, with field deployment lifting weld acceptance from 81% to 95-98%.
VL-DPO: using a VLM as a zero-shot preference rater to DPO-finetune ego-motion forecasting models on the Waymo Open E2E Driving Dataset.
EXOM: embedded excavator-operation monitoring (excavation counting) from factory-installed cabin camera plus hydraulic sensors, for construction productivity analytics.
AddTraX: clip-on motor-integrated driving wheel units that boost electric tractor traction 1.9x on demand.
R2S2, a real-world-ready whole-body skill space for humanoid reaching and teleoperation — commercial humanoid WBC infrastructure.
TransDiffuser: anchor-free diffusion trajectory planner for end-to-end driving, SOTA 94.9 PDMS on NAVSIM.
Corridor-constrained end-to-end driving with differentiable trajectory optimization, cutting agent collisions 66.7% on nuScenes.
Data-driven Koopman embeddings enabling whole-body safety constraints as a single QP, validated on a Kinova arm and Unitree Go2 quadruped.
EndoDDC: diffusion-based depth completion for endoscopic surgical robot navigation.

contrarian.finding: Even oracle-perfect future predictions fail to improve planning, and imitation-learning planners produce worse proposals than the rule-based PDM lane-follower; a proposal-centric classical approach sets new SOTA in out-of-distribution interactive scenarios.

Under realistic learned reactive traffic agents, nearly all planner scores drop — IDM-based closed-loop benchmarks have been overestimating learned planners, and in augmented edge cases all learned planners fail abruptly while rule-based planners retain reasonable behavior.
Classical variable-projection nonlinear least squares, adapted for gauge symmetries, runs 2-35x faster than state-of-the-art SLAM/SfM solvers — algebraic structure, not learning, delivers the gain.

A closed-form spatio-temporal-tube safety law on DMPs is orders of magnitude faster and more accurate than optimization-based CBF baselines, removing online optimization entirely from provably-safe manipulation.

Exploiting B-spline gradient sparsity and closed-form derivatives yields a 26.9x trajectory-optimization speedup on a UR5e with no learning component — near real-time manipulator planning via structure, not neural networks.

Zero-shot test-tube recognition via automatic segmentation, clustering and self-labeling removes the need for any explicit training data on new tube types — engineering around learning rather than with it.

humanoid.signal: Opt2Skill (Georgia Tech, Digit): DDP-generated contact-consistent reference trajectories tracked by RL beat human-demo/IK references, and including torque information in references improves contact-force tracking — the emerging humanoid loco-manipulation recipe is traj-opt references + RL tracking, transferred to hardware.

R2S2 (Galaxy General Robot + Tsinghua/PKU): structural skill priors built from individually sim2real-verified primitive skills, enabling large-workspace whole-body reaching and teleoperation — Chinese commercial humanoid players are publishing their WBC infrastructure.

AMPCO on Unitree G1: adaptive human-motion priors with constrained task optimization cut locomotion energy consumption ~70% versus task-focused RL baselines — energy efficiency is becoming an explicit humanoid training objective.

Ego-vision world model + sampling MPC for contact-seeking humanoid behavior (wall support after a push, blocking objects, ducking under arches) deployed in real time from proprioception and depth — humanoids moving from collision avoidance to deliberate contact exploitation.

Low-cost sim2real for the BRUCE biped: a PINN (Euler-Lagrange structure + LSTM) trained only on sim data predicts forward dynamics at 1 kHz on embedded hardware as reference states for feedforward+feedback torque control — closing the reality gap without mocap.

Sensorless torque estimation for harmonic-drive actuators via a co-designed compliant helical tube + LSTM on encoder features — a cost-down path to torque-controlled humanoid joints without external torque sensors.

ByteWrist (ByteDance): nested three-stage parallel RPY wrist with a central spherical support ball, claimed stiffer and more compact than serial wrists for confined-space and dual-arm manipulation — anthropomorphic wrist hardware is an active industrial battleground.

MARG: quadruped gap-terrain traversal using elevation maps from a single LiDAR with a drift-reducing terrain-map-generation model — perception-based legged locomotion shrinking to one-sensor deployments for zero-shot transfer.

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting becomes the default robotics map
themes.whats_new: 3DGS has fully displaced NeRF/implicit fields as the dense scene representation; the research frontier has moved from 'can we use GS' to engineering problems: memory pruning for large scale (Pocket-SLAM's rendering-area-aware pruning, 60% memory cut), label-free dynamic-instance 4D GS for street scenes (DIAL-GS), feed-forward semantic GS from sparse views without per-scene optimization (SemGS), GS-voxel hybrids unifying optics/geometry/semantics online (OmniMap), and GS for live teleoperation displays (HIL-GS).

themes.name: Sim-trained RL plus real-world residual correction for contact-rich skills
themes.whats_new: Rather than chasing better domain randomization, the recipe is now a sim-trained base policy plus a small residual learned in the real world: SPARR (NVIDIA) adds an asymmetric real-world visual residual to a sim assembly policy (+38.4% over zero-shot sim2real); the chicken-deboning work (WeI2I.292) uses residual RL with discretized force observations on a physical bone-analog testbed; IMI (ThI2I.240) iterates imitation on the policy's own rollouts to escape infeasible references.

themes.name: Making generative action policies fast and verifiable
themes.whats_new: Diffusion/flow policies are the assumed baseline; the new work attacks their latency and lack of guarantees: SeFA aligns one-step rectified flow with observations (98% latency cut), Viper wraps imitation learning in an NMPC closed loop with a stability proof, X-Diffusion trains on human videos by treating human actions as high-noise robot actions (Ambient Diffusion), and Diffusion-MPC repurposes diffusion as a dynamics prior for test-time-adaptable locomotion planning.

themes.name: LLMs/MLLMs as zero-shot hierarchical planners
themes.whats_new: Off-the-shelf LLMs slot in as the top of a planning hierarchy with no task training: LaViRA decomposes VLN into language/vision/robot action tiers using different-scale MLLMs, TZPP coordinates a Unitree G1 humanoid and Go2 quadruped with zero training/prior/simulation, GPT-PDDL converts demonstration videos into executable PDDL plans, and ActionReasoning explicitly argues continuous action spaces exceed token-based VLA capacity, using multi-agent LLM physics reasoning instead.

themes.name: Cheap, structured touch: tactile hardware and extrinsic contact estimation
themes.whats_new: A push to make force/contact sensing accessible and useful beyond raw F/T readings: ShapeForce replaces a six-axis F/T sensor with a deformable wrist plus marker tracking at near-zero cost; TacTape adds fiducial tape giving vision-based tactile sensors sub-mm localization; UVDtact decouples shape and force channels via UV-ink markers; UNIC estimates extrinsic (object-environment) contacts multimodally without camera calibration.

themes.name: CBF/CLF safety certificates bolted onto every planner
themes.whats_new: Control barrier/Lyapunov functions are now a standard composable layer rather than a control-theory niche: C-CLF-CBF-RRT embeds them in sampling-based planning with probabilistic completeness via per-iteration QCQPs; ES-HPC-MPC combines exponentially stabilizing CLFs with perception-constraint CBFs for slack-to-taut payload hybrid dynamics; FactorMPC encodes on-manifold CBF obstacle factors inside factor-graph MPC.

methods_shift: RL-in-sim with domain randomization is the unquestioned default for locomotion and dynamic skills (Kungfubot2, MUJICA, TADPO, OPEN), but the frontier has shifted from better randomization to hybrid sim-base + real-world-residual stacks (SPARR, the deboning work) and to model-based RL from pixels (DreamerV3 in Dream to Fly) displacing PPO/SAC where samples are expensive. In imitation learning, diffusion policies are the incumbent being displaced by one-step rectified flow (SeFA) and by MPC-wrapped verifiable formulations (Viper); diffusion itself is migrating from policy head to world-model/planner roles (Diffusion-MPC, VISTA's generative imagination for VLN). In mapping/SLAM, 3DGS has displaced NeRF/INR as the dense representation of record, with work now on pruning, monocular coupling, and semantics. Zero-shot MLLM hierarchies are displacing trained waypoint predictors in vision-language navigation (LaViRA, TZPP). Meanwhile classical machinery persists where guarantees matter: CBF/CLF layers, momentum observers and invariant EKFs for proprioceptive estimation (D-GVIO, the leg-wheel contact estimator), Koopman LQR for switched contact dynamics, and hard-constrained optimal control still beating GPU planners on trajectory quality (the KU Leuven picker vs cuRobo).

notable.why: Runs fully autonomous UWB relative localization on 13 drones of 33 g each (168 MHz MCU, 192 KB RAM) and reports the counterintuitive theoretical result that unobservability self-corrects because state drift induces control actions that restore observability.

First learned policy to debone a real chicken shoulder — residual RL with force feedback transfers zero-shot from a custom multi-material cutting simulator to actual poultry with 4x better bone avoidance than open-loop baselines.
Claims the first deployment of RL-based end-to-end vision policies on a full-scale off-road vehicle, with zero-shot sim-to-real at high speed on extreme slopes.

DreamerV3 world-model RL learns drone racing directly from pixels — with perception-aware camera-steering behavior emerging without any handcrafted viewing reward — and flies real quadrotors at 9 m/s.

A blockchain DAO robot that gets paid in tokens for collecting trash and autonomously re-invests its income to buy its own battery charging — doubled its income over a 3-day experiment, an unusual robot-economics demonstration at ICRA.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: SPARR — sim-trained assembly policies with real-world asymmetric residual RL, pushing near-perfect industrial two-part assembly without human supervision.
One-Policy-Fits-All cross-embodiment manipulation via geometry-aware latent action spaces spanning 11 end-effectors from grippers to dexterous hands.
Soft conical robotic hand with simulation-driven evolutionary trajectory optimization for granular scooping (food/material handling automation).
Vision-based anomaly detection (ALLO benchmark + MRAD algorithm) for Canadarm3 autonomous inspection on NASA's Lunar Gateway.
Sensorless haptic feedback for surgical robots — structured-light 3D reconstruction plus a PointNet image-to-force network for soft-tissue interaction.
Kungfubot2/VMS — a single whole-body RL controller with orthogonal mixture-of-experts for versatile, minute-long dynamic humanoid motion skills.
PA-BiCoop — single-model bimanual manipulation with dynamic primary/auxiliary arm role assignment, +48% over SOTA on RLBench2.
PPT — pretraining motion forecasting for autonomous driving on pseudo-labeled trajectories from off-the-shelf detectors/trackers, removing manual annotation.
UVDtact — fingertip-form-factor vision-based tactile sensor with UV-marker decoupling of shape reconstruction and force estimation.
EdgeGrasp — edge-perception-enhanced 7-DoF grasp pose estimation, SOTA on GraspNet-1Billion.
Acrobatic control for an Ultra-Mobility Vehicle bicycle robot — first unassisted front-flips via iterative motion imitation RL.

contrarian.finding: State-of-the-art learned visual anomaly detectors fail in the space domain; a statistical model-reference algorithm using known arm pose plus a CAD model (MRAD) beats them on the ALLO lunar-orbit benchmark.

A CPU-only hard-constrained optimal control planner produces trajectories with 0.7x the execution time of NVIDIA's GPU-accelerated cuRobo and guarantees constraint satisfaction — classical OCP beating the GPU-learning-era toolchain on solution quality.

A learning-free event-camera 6D pose tracker (optical-flow propagation + template correction) matches and sometimes beats deep-network trackers on fast-moving objects, where network update rates are the bottleneck.

Zero-shot multimodal LLMs underperform domain-specific methods at fine-grained, spatially grounded driving risk assessment — a measured negative result against the 'MLLMs solve scene understanding' narrative.
Zero-training, zero-prior, zero-simulation MLLM-guided navigation achieves human-comparable efficiency on real Unitree robots, explicitly rejecting the train-in-sim orthodoxy.
Argues the continuous action space of the physical world exceeds the representational capacity of linguistic tokens, questioning whether scaling VLA data alone yields general robot intelligence.

A climbing robot with passively conforming pin-array grippers achieves stable locomotion on steep natural rock with no complex sensing or control — mechanical compliance and redundancy instead of learning.

humanoid.signal: Single-policy whole-body humanoid control recipe: hybrid local-fidelity/global-trajectory tracking objective + Orthogonal Mixture-of-Experts + segment-level (not step-wise) tracking rewards, yielding stable minute-long dynamic motion sequences on real hardware.
Unitree G1 + Go2 as the de facto commodity research stack — a humanoid acts as task coordinator while the quadruped explores, all driven zero-shot by an MLLM.

Wheeled-legged locomotion recipe (Unitree Go2-W): jointly train omnidirectional driving, platform climbing, and fall recovery in one policy with explicit DC-motor constraint modeling, plus a proprioception-only learned skill selector for sim-to-real robustness.

Diffusion world-model MPC for legged locomotion enables test-time behavior adaptation via reward/constraint optimization at the planner, addressing the fixed-policy limitation of model-free RL controllers.

WiXus design principle: add wire-driven environmental anchoring to a wheeled-legged robot so legs can be freed from support duty and repurposed as manipulator arms — mobility and manipulation from one limb set.

Proprioception-only contact state + contact point estimation (generalized momentum observer + rim-localization optimization) for leg-wheel transformable robots, 97% contact detection accuracy at 0.4 m/s trot — fills the gap left by point-foot model assumptions.

DAPPER targets query-efficient preference-based RL for legged skill acquisition by sampling comparisons across multiple from-scratch policies — relevant for tuning locomotion styles without reward engineering.

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting becomes a general-purpose robotics representation, not a rendering trick
themes.whats_new: 3DGS is being pushed past novel-view synthesis into SLAM in dynamic scenes (GGD-SLAM), physics-informed sensor fusion (Aqua-Splat embeds sonar wave-propagation in the GS image-formation model; GS-UVCE adds Medium/Light MLPs for underwater attenuation), visual localization via render-and-match (WeI1I.381), explicit 3D memory for video segmentation (FastSAM-Splat/SAM2-Splat), real-to-sim reconstruction for RL (Phys2Real), and aggressive pruning for embedded deployment (SVR-GS, 5.6x fewer Gaussians). The representation is displacing NeRF and TSDF/point maps simultaneously.

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs wrapped in structure rather than trusted end-to-end
themes.whats_new: The pattern is no longer 'prompt the VLM for actions' but bolting formal or structured machinery around it: LLM mission plans formally verified against LLM-extracted LTL specs with self-correcting loops (TuI1I.162), LLMs querying 3D scene graphs through a Cypher graph-database interface instead of context stuffing (ThI1I.292), VLM physical-parameter priors fused with online estimates via ensemble uncertainty (Phys2Real, ThI1I.302), VLMs as data curators issuing semantic queries over robot datasets (RoboSQ filters DROID failures, lifting policy success from 1/15 to 13/15), and topology injected directly into VLM self-attention for navigation (TagaVLM).

themes.name: Force, compliance and controller parameters enter the learning loop for contact-rich manipulation
themes.whats_new: Visuomotor policies are being made contact-aware instead of position-only: flow-matching policies trained on force-informed sim data from a single human demo coupled with impedance control (WeI2I.319), policies that jointly output actions AND low-level controller gains to close the sim2real controller-dynamics gap (DexCtrl), Bayesian-Optimization fine-tuning plus GMM initialization sampling to push assembly policies from ~80% research-grade to 91.5% and 8-part chained assembly (Refinery), and grasp-independent tool-force estimation from vision-based tactile sensing (TuI2I.378).

themes.name: Formal safety layers go mainstream: CBFs and conformal prediction over any nominal controller
themes.whats_new: CBF-QPs are being extended beyond toy settings: limiting whole-robot kinetic energy on a Franka (TuI1I.14), first formally-verified contact-force safety for a soft manipulator via predicted environment deformation (ThI2I.355), and hierarchical 'safety-first' QPs with guaranteed feasibility under many CBF constraints (WeI2I.361). In perception, conformal prediction arrives for driving-risk calibration with coverage guarantees (WeI2I.271), and chance-constrained sensitivity-aware MPC handles multiple uncertainty sources at once (ThI2I.24).

themes.name: Diffusion expands from policies into model-based planning — and gets its first critical audits
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is moving upstream into trajectory optimization itself: data-free Model-Based Diffusion improved 13x in sample efficiency via importance sampling (WeI1I.402), and context-conditioned classifier-free diffusion generalizes motion planning across unseen environments without per-scene retraining (CAMPD). Simultaneously the first careful negative-leaning audits appear: MIT shows diffusion policies only coarsely approximate kinematic constraint manifolds even when task success looks fine (WeI2I.233). Diffusion also escapes robotics outright — image diffusion models finetuned to emit navigation path masks (Ventura) and diffusion policy repurposed for speech-driven facial animation (WeI1I.221).

methods_shift: Three defaults are visible. (1) For manipulation policies, diffusion/flow-matching imitation is now the assumed baseline — papers either extend it (force-aware flow matching WeI2I.319, BFA view-weighting on top of ACT-style policies WeI2I.18) or audit it (WeI2I.233) — displacing plain behavior cloning. (2) For dense scene representation, 3D Gaussian Splatting has effectively replaced NeRF and is displacing TSDF/point-cloud maps across SLAM, localization, underwater and even segmentation memory (ThI1I.98, WeI1I.334, TuI2I.355). (3) For legged locomotion, massively-parallel RL-in-sim with teacher-student privileged learning and zero-shot transfer is the unquestioned recipe (WeBT3.7, ThI1I.365), with the new twist being sample-efficiency (one-stage L2T) and hybridization with model-based controllers (MAR regularization). Meanwhile classical optimal control is not being displaced at the performance frontier — LPV-MPC runs a full-scale racecar at 160+ mph (TuBT2.5), CADMM-parallelized trajectory optimization hits O(1) per-iteration complexity (WeI2I.358), and CBF-QPs are the standard safety wrapper around whatever policy sits above (TuI1I.14, WeI2I.361). LLMs/VLMs have become the default semantic/reasoning layer but are increasingly constrained by formal verification, graph queries, or conformal calibration rather than used raw.

notable.why: First end-to-end neural controller for an industrial-grade 6D magnetic levitation system — raw sensors to coil currents on an unstable plant, trained from a proprietary controller's interaction data, suggesting learned control is reaching hard low-level industrial loops.

A linear-parameter-varying MPC kept a full-scale IAC autonomous racecar stable above 160 mph (71.5 m/s) on real hardware — a striking demonstration that classical control still owns the dynamics frontier.

MIT shows diffusion policies learn only a coarse approximation of kinematic constraint manifolds and that task success rate is a misleading indicator of constraint satisfaction — a rare rigorous audit of the field's favorite policy class.

NVIDIA's Refinery chains sim-trained contact-rich assembly policies to assemble up to 8 parts at 91.5% success without multi-step training, explicitly framing ~80% research-grade success as inadequate for industry.
Near-100% success on dual-arm dual peg-in-hole at 0.2 mm clearance using only monocular in-hand cameras and a virtual-force visual-servo scheme, robust to camera calibration error.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Sim-to-real fine-tuning and deployment-time optimization (Bayesian Optimization + GMM initialization sampling) to push robotic assembly policies to industry-grade reliability and long-horizon multi-part assembly.
Ventura — a vision-language navigation stack that finetunes internet-pretrained image diffusion models to emit visual path plans for field robots, trained without manual annotation.
Monocular 3D Gaussian Splatting SLAM that stays robust in dynamic environments via a generalizable motion model, no depth or semantic priors required.
Online 3DGS as an explicit 3D memory that upgrades FastSAM/SAM2 video segmentation consistency — perception infrastructure for consumer/warehouse robots.
Whole-body optimization-based control with adaptive-predictive visual servoing so mobile manipulators can track moving targets in unbounded workspaces.
Best-Feature-Aware multi-view fusion, a plug-and-play module that reweights camera views per task phase, claiming 22-46% success gains on fine-grained manipulation.
Controllable collision-scenario generation (collision type + time-to-accident) and the COLLIDE dataset for stress-testing and fine-tuning AV planners.
LMPOcc — crowdsourced long-term-memory global occupancy priors that boost onboard 3D semantic occupancy prediction for production autonomous driving.
Single-step 3D-printed soft proprioceptive sensors with continual-learning calibration for the neck actuator of the HARU social robot.
Model-mediated teleoperation with imperfect regolith models to keep lunar sample-collection operators trusting and performant under multi-second delays.
CFEAR-TR, radar-only teach-and-repeat localization at 29 Hz reaching 0.117 m accuracy — all-weather navigation for field/industrial vehicles.

contrarian.finding: Diffusion policies do NOT precisely learn the kinematic constraint manifolds in their training data — they learn coarse approximations, and task success masks constraint violation; a direct challenge to reading benchmark success as understanding.
Classical LPV-MPC, with no learning, achieves stable lateral control of a full-scale autonomous racecar above 160 mph — the highest-performance control result in the batch is hand-engineered.

Well-known VLMs peak at 33% accuracy (at high latency) on agent-level spatio-temporal risk reasoning for driving; even a fine-tuned 7B model only reaches 41%, undercutting claims that VLMs are ready for safety reasoning.

Lightweight, non-semantic dilated superpixels beat semantic supersegment representations (SegVLAD) for visual place recognition — semantics-driven features were excluding valuable 'meaningless' pixels.

A simple pipeline of off-the-shelf Faster R-CNN plus classical PnP on the robot's standard circular flange achieves higher hand-eye calibration accuracy than more complex learned approaches, with no manual annotation.

Black-box evolutionary adversarial attacks (dimension-weighted perturbations) significantly degrade multi-agent deep RL systems cheaply — a robustness negative result for learned multi-robot policies.

humanoid.signal: Learn-to-Teach (L2T) collapses the standard two-stage teacher-student privileged-learning pipeline into one stage by recycling simulator samples, achieving zero-shot sim-to-real on Digit over 12+ real uneven terrains without any depth/perception module — the locomotion recipe is being compressed, not just scaled.

PPF pre-trains the policy by imitating a model-based controller, then RL-fine-tunes with model-assumption-based regularization (align to the controller only where its assumptions hold) — full-size Digit hits 1.5 m/s across slippery, sloped, uneven and sandy terrain; hybrid model-based+RL is the emerging recipe for robustness without catastrophic forgetting.

LLM-generated grasp functionality and synergy for language-guided dexterous functional grasping on humanoid hands (CUHK/IIT) — the VLM-to-dexterity pipeline is being targeted specifically at humanoid manipulation.

A 10-gram, 25x10x24 mm modular hand actuator with multistage gearing, moderate backdrivability, and sensorless torque observation (Reaction Torque Observer, 0.011 N·m error) provides position/velocity/current/torque feedback — relevant datapoint for cheap proprioceptive hand/finger actuation without dedicated force sensors.

Dual rimless wheels coupled by elastic (tensegrity-style) connectors show low-stiffness coupling yields slow, low-energy-loss passive gaits and more stable real-world walking on low slopes — compliance in the body, not the controller, as a locomotion design lever.

themes.name: Foundation models slotted as components into classical robotics pipelines
themes.whats_new: Rather than end-to-end VLAs, pre-trained foundation models are dropped into specific slots of otherwise conventional stacks: MASt3R/3D foundation models for C-SLAM loop closing (TuI1I.44) and opti-acoustic fusion (WeBT1.6), DINOv2 features replacing SuperPoint-style descriptors in visual odometry (WeI2I.25), VLMs as frontier-assignment global planners for multi-robot navigation (WeI2I.411), VLM-driven automatic generation of task-ready sim environments from one RGB image (ThI1I.376), and LLMs building behavior trees online for heterogeneous teams (TuI2I.254). A second-order signal: wrappers for trust are appearing — privacy-screening edge LLMs before cloud planning (ThI2I.212) and formal-method-guided LLM fine-tuning for safety (ThI1I.260).

themes.name: Data and viewpoint engineering for imitation learning, not new policy architectures
themes.whats_new: Policy classes are frozen (diffusion policy, BC); the innovation moved to the observation and data distribution. Vantage selects camera poses via information-gain optimization to make any pre-trained policy viewpoint-agnostic (WeI1I.242); ObAct uses a second arm to build a 3DGS scene and physically move to an occlusion-free vantage, improving trajectory transfer up to 233% (ThI1I.284); MonoDuo synthesizes bimanual demonstrations from single-arm robot + human collaborator via pose estimation and inpainting (WeI1I.262); real-is-sim trains/evaluates policies entirely inside a 60Hz-synchronized Gaussian digital twin (ThI2I.56).

themes.name: Structured RL for legged robots: residuals, hybrid action spaces, and policy composition
themes.whats_new: Monolithic end-to-end locomotion policies are being decomposed: SEEC adds model-enhanced residual RL for upper-body end-effector stabilization that transfers to unseen locomotion controllers without retraining (TuI1I.251); HFPLP defines the action space as joint positions plus feedforward torques with a learned disturbance compensator (WeI2I.317); REBot composes avoidance and recovery policies in a finite-state machine for reflexive quadruped evasion (TuI1I.388); DreamTIP injects LLM-identified task-invariant properties (contact stability, terrain clearance) into a Dreamer world model, taking a real Climb task from 10% to 100% success (ThI2I.272).

themes.name: Gaussian splatting as the default 3D scene representation
themes.whats_new: 3DGS has displaced NeRF and meshes across very different jobs in one batch: active-vision camera placement (ThI1I.284), RGB-D SLAM fused with event cameras (ThI1I.377), single-view generalizable street reconstruction with LiDAR depth (TuI1I.64), language-driven 4D editing of driving scenes for data augmentation (WeI1I.162), and as the physics substrate of a real-time digital twin (ThI2I.56, Embodied Gaussians).

themes.name: Opti-acoustic fusion for turbid underwater perception
themes.whats_new: Sonar+camera fusion is graduating from heuristic alignment to principled dense methods: plane-sweep cross-modal stereo (TuI1I.188, with the first synchronized stereo+sonar dataset), MASt3R correspondences paired with acoustic 3D cues running in real time at 12+ NTU turbidity (WeBT1.6, an award-session paper), and ping-level raw-backscatter SLAM that skips image formation entirely for feature-poor seabeds (TuI2LB.5).

methods_shift: Diffusion policies are now the assumed baseline being extended rather than the novelty — extended to decentralized multi-agent coverage control (TuI2I.239) and meta-manipulation (WeI2I.116), and used as the reference policy class that viewpoint-selection work like Vantage (WeI1I.242) improves. RL-in-sim with domain randomization is the unquestioned default for legged locomotion and dexterous grasping; the frontier moved to what structure to add: residual policies (TuI1I.251), hybrid force-position action spaces (WeI2I.317), FSM-gated skill composition (TuI1I.388, TuI2I.280), and Dreamer-style world models for reward-free or sim-to-real adaptation (WeI2LB.22, ThI2I.272). In perception, pre-trained foundation features (DINOv2 in WeI2I.25, MASt3R in WeBT1.6 and TuI1I.44) are displacing bespoke trained descriptors like SuperPoint, and 3DGS is displacing NeRF/meshes as the scene representation. LLMs/VLMs are displacing hand-written task planners and behavior-tree authoring (TuI2I.254, WeI2I.411, ThI2I.212). Notably, classical control has not been displaced at the safety-critical layer — CBFs (WeI1I.128), MPC/MPPI (TuI1I.28, ThI1LB.18), VO-style reactive avoidance (TuI1I.195), and energy-shaping (WeI1I.39) persist, but almost always hybridized with a learned component (learned safe sets via hypernetworks, learned residual hysteresis models, learned parametric CBFs).

notable.why: Demonstrates sustained autonomous circular and figure-eight drifting on a production combustion sports car with mechanically coupled axles and >250 ms actuator delays — moving drift control off bespoke research platforms for the first time.

First aerial robot with closed-loop control informed by real-time flow-field measurements: event-camera smoke velocimetry feeds a recurrent disturbance estimator so a quadrotor can hover inside a narrow pipe.

NVIDIA's DextrAH-RGB claims the first robust sim-to-real transfer of an end-to-end RGB-only policy for dynamic, contact-rich multi-fingered grasping — no depth, no CAD models, distilled entirely in simulation with photorealistic tiled rendering.

Fully open (weights, code, 10k-trajectory dataset) 7B 'Action Reasoning Model' from AI2 that reports beating Pi-0 and GR00T N1.5 zero-shot on SimplerEnv — an open-source challenge to closed robot foundation models.

Reward-free continual adaptation: a frozen reward predictor inside a latent world model lets a degraded space robot retrain its policy purely on imagined rollouts, with no reward signal available at deployment.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: End-to-end RGB-based dexterous arm-hand grasping policies trained with RL plus geometric-fabric controllers and distilled via photorealistic tiled rendering — sim-first pipelines for deployable manipulation.
MAPF-HD: heuristic phased null-agent swapping for multi-agent path finding in high-density grids (700+ cells in seconds), aimed at automated warehouses and valet parking.
Flow-aware MAPF that learns motion patterns of uncontrollable agents (humans) to cut fleet conflicts up to 55% — large robot fleets in shared industrial spaces.
Low-cost, error-tolerant peg-in-hole assembly using passive flexible joints and peg rotation with simple control — no high-frequency sensors or servos.
MolmoAct, an open-weights robotic foundation model family (Action Reasoning Models) plus an open 10k-trajectory dataset, positioned as an open blueprint against Pi-0/GR00T.

real-is-sim: a 60Hz Embodied-Gaussian digital twin where policies always act on the simulated robot and the real robot mirrors it — shifting the sim-to-real burden from the policy to the twin's synchronization.
Hypernetwork-learned maximal safe sets (HJ-reachability supervised) as terminal constraints for real-time MPC local planning — production-oriented safe trajectory planning.

contrarian.finding: Crowd navigation by simply selecting and following a human leader with rule-based evaluation and a trivial base planner beats heavyweight predictive and data-driven planners in sim and real, with no learned or predictive modules at all.

Controlled comparison of six LLMs (frontier and efficient tiers) against the OntoAgent cognitive architecture in the same safety-critical robot teaming system: LLMs fail to verify their own knowledge state before acting even when given equivalent procedural knowledge — the authors argue the deficit is architectural, not fixable with knowledge or scale.

Passive mechanical compliance plus peg rotation and simple control achieves robust, error-tolerant peg-in-hole assembly — no learning, no high-frequency force control, undercutting the case for learned insertion policies in this regime.

Explicitly abandons accurate fabric dynamics modeling as practically impossible, instead predicting a time series of oriented bounding boxes (OBB-Transformer) for collision avoidance — a deliberate retreat from model fidelity that works on real hardware.

Classical nonlinear observers on SO(3) with proven exponential stability show two scalar measurements suffice for attitude estimation — full vector IMU readings (and learned estimators) are unnecessary for the task.

humanoid.signal: SEEC on the Booster T1: model-enhanced residual RL stabilizes the arm end-effector against lower-body locomotion disturbances and transfers to unseen locomotion controllers without retraining — evidence the field is modularizing humanoid whole-body control into composable upper/lower policies.

Robust Policy Gating for multi-skill transitions in humanoid fighting from Shanghai AI Lab / China Telecom / Tsinghua — dynamic, contact-adversarial multi-skill humanoid control is now a mainstream research target.

HR2-KILO: humanoid-specific high-rate kinematic-inertial-LiDAR odometry, signaling that state estimation tailored to bipedal kinematics (not just quadruped/wheeled recipes) is becoming its own subfield.

SignBot pipeline (motion retargeting from human sign-language datasets, RL tracking policy, generative interaction layer) works across multiple humanoid platforms — human-motion retargeting + RL tracking is now the standard recipe for expressive humanoid upper-body skills.

Bipedal walking dynamics model for granular terrain adds 3 DoF for foot sinkage/slip and validates GRF and cost-of-transport predictions on sand — contact modeling beyond rigid flat ground is entering bipedal locomotion design.

Quadruped (legged) sim-to-real via Dreamer world model with LLM-identified task-invariant properties: real-world Climb success from 10% to 100%, suggesting world-model adaptation may displace pure domain randomization for legged deployment.

themes.name: Diffusion policies as commodity infrastructure for manipulation
themes.whats_new: Diffusion policies are no longer the contribution — they are the substrate. Papers now build deployment tooling on top of them: free uncertainty estimates from the generative process for human-in-the-loop gating (TuBT1.2), path-consistent safety filters with reachability analysis that beat CBFs by 68% (TuI1I.191), reference-guided denoising to cut sampling steps for real-time inference (ThI2I.397), discriminator-filtered diffusion grasp generation at 53M-grasp scale (ThI1I.73), and LLM-generated demonstrations feeding 3D diffusion policies so no human demos are needed (TuI1I.415).

themes.name: Force/tactile signals fused into learned manipulation policies
themes.whats_new: Vision-only imitation learning is being explicitly called out as insufficient for contact-rich tasks. New recipes: handheld devices capturing high-frequency F/T data during human demos with frequency-aware transformer fusion reaching 83% on gear assembly/battery insertion (ThI2I.257), dual-branch tactile encoders that estimate in-hand pose and adaptively reweight modalities at contact (ThI2I.397), differentiable non-holonomic hydroelastic models of compliant tactile sensors enabling gradient-based trajectory optimization (TuI1I.169), and cheap photometric-stereo calibration for curved visuotactile sensors (TuI1I.160).

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs as runtime planners and verifiers — without fine-tuning
themes.whats_new: The shift is from fine-tuning foundation models to wrapping them: formal-verification feedback drives automatic prompt refinement via LLM-AutoDiff, lifting plan compliance from 60% to 90% with zero gradient updates (WeI2I.99); a VLM verifies at runtime that a reasoning-VLA's sampled actions actually match its own textual plan, a training-free 'embodied CoT faithfulness' check (ThI2I.128); MLLM relevancy distilled into lightweight 3D fields for 16.5s zero-shot context-aware grasping (WeI1I.92); LLM part-reasoning for task-oriented handovers at 83% zero-shot (TuI2I.427); ControlNet-style depth-conditioned fine-tuning that preserves navigation-foundation-model priors (TuI2I.145).

themes.name: Humanoid whole-body skills = retargeted human motion + massively simplified sim RL, on Unitree G1
themes.whats_new: The recipe is converging: retarget human MoCap, train proprioceptive RL in sim, deploy on a Unitree G1. The new frontier is data quality and reward minimalism — interaction-mesh retargeting that preserves human-object/terrain contacts enables 30-second parkour and loco-manipulation with only 5 reward terms and no curriculum (WeAT1.4); a single policy unifying agile dance/run tracking with extreme-balance maneuvers via heterogeneous MoCap + synthetic balance data (WeI2I.165); CLF-shaped rewards from LIP/HZD reference generators replacing hand-tuned reward soups (TuBT3.8).

themes.name: SLAM leaning on semantic and structural priors instead of more sensors
themes.whats_new: Localization papers increasingly exploit human-made structure as a free prior: navigation signs matched to OpenStreetMap/floor plans for mapping-free global localization after 1-2 sign observations (WeI1I.377), four-layer hierarchical scene graphs (keyframe/wall/room/floor) making optimization 10x faster and rejecting cross-floor false loop closures (ThI1I.356), BIM wall correspondences as back-end constraints on construction sites (WeI1LB.8), gravity-decoupled 4-DOF PnP for underwater VI-SLAM (ThI1I.274), and cross-domain USV-AUV loop closures on structures visible above and below water (ThI1I.410).

themes.name: Evaluation science and data-scaling rigor for learned policies
themes.whats_new: The field is building its own measurement instruments: a 30,000-hour study showing power-law scaling holds open-loop but breaks closed-loop in driving (ThI2I.259), a formal generalization taxonomy (STAR-Gen: visual/semantic/behavioral) with reproducible Bridge-V2 and ALOHA-2 case studies (ThI1I.435), the first RGB-D industrial-parts 6D-pose benchmark with onboarding sequences (WeI2I.208), and a 20M-grasp 500K-object cross-embodiment dataset (TuI1I.171).

methods_shift: Diffusion policies are now the unmarked default action head for manipulation — papers assume one and contribute safety filters (TuI1I.191), uncertainty gating (TuBT1.2), or faster sampling (ThI2I.397) around it, the way papers once assumed an EKF. For humanoids, the default is RL-in-sim on retargeted human MoCap deployed on a Unitree G1 (WeAT1.4, WeI2I.165, TuBT3.8, TuI2I.242), displacing hand-designed gait libraries — though notably the new trend is re-injecting model-based structure into RL (CLF rewards from LIP/HZD in TuBT3.8, Lyapunov-certified centroidal MPC in TuI2I.32). LLMs/VLMs are displacing fine-tuned task planners via training-free wrappers: prompt optimization with formal verification (WeI2I.99) and runtime VLM verification of VLA plans (ThI2I.128). Classical methods aren't dying, they're being hybridized: learned Kalman gain inside tracking-by-detection (ThI1I.12), optimization layers embedded in end-to-end planning networks (WeI1I.379), RL+MPC hierarchies for crowd navigation (ThI2I.307), and pure classical planners still posting big wins (InBi-RRT 22x faster replanning than RRTX, TuI1I.135; ellipsoidal-corridor trust regions beating SOTA corridor planners, WeI1I.266). Vision-only imitation is being displaced by visuo-tactile/force-conditioned policies (ThI2I.257, ThI2I.397).

notable.why: OmniRetarget gets a Unitree G1 to execute 30-second parkour and loco-manipulation sequences with only 5 reward terms, no curriculum, and plain domain randomization — arguing data quality (interaction-mesh retargeting) has replaced reward engineering as the bottleneck.

A 30,000-hour, 4M-demonstration study finds the imitation-learning power law holds in open-loop but breaks in closed-loop driving evaluation — directly undercutting the 'just scale data' thesis the field has imported from LLMs.

Shows diffusion policies' generative process yields a deployment-ready uncertainty metric 'for free', letting robots request human help only when needed with zero operator interaction during training (award session).

Claims Grover's algorithm plus quantum ML achieves up to 93x speedups over Nelder-Mead for manipulator kinematic optimization — an extraordinary quantum-robotics claim a skeptical reader will want to audit.

First closed-loop drone control demonstrated on the SynSense Speck neuromorphic chip — dense optical flow regression via hybrid SNN-ANN running 2x faster than an ANN baseline at identical power, sustaining real indoor flight.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: STAR-Gen, a standardized generalization taxonomy and reproducible benchmark protocol for generalist manipulation policies (Bridge V2, bimanual ALOHA 2).
GraspGen — diffusion-transformer 6-DOF grasp generation plus a released 53M-grasp simulated dataset, aimed at turnkey cross-gripper grasping.
Runtime steering/verification layers for reasoning VLA models so deployed robots' actions stay faithful to their textual plans.
End-to-end autonomous driving data engine; probing scaling laws over 30,000 hours of fleet demonstrations to decide where data money goes.
Autonomous valet parking pipeline that explicitly predicts other drivers' parking intentions from motion history.
Adaptive centroidal MPC with closed-loop stability certificates for humanoids (ergoCub) and quadrupeds (Aliengo) — production-grade legged locomotion control.
D-CLING — ControlNet-style depth-conditioned fine-tuning of navigation foundation models that preserves pretrained priors for deployable robot navigation.
PAPRLE, a plug-and-play modular teleoperation ecosystem for reconfigurable robotic limbs to scale embodied-AI data collection.
DexHand 021 — a 1 kg, 19-DoF tendon-driven commercial dexterous hand with proprioceptive force-sensing admittance control.

industry_signal.company: Amazon Robotics / Mitsubishi Electric Research Labs
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: GRAM — a robust adaptation module for deep RL that handles both in- and out-of-distribution dynamics, validated on quadruped hardware.
Constrained EM learning-from-demonstration that enforces time-sensitive via-pose constraints — closing the precision gap blocking LfD in industrial cells.
CorrectManip — closed-loop sim data generation that learns from policy failures, with sim-to-real on Unitree H1/G1 humanoids.

contrarian.finding: Data scaling laws for end-to-end driving fail to transfer from open-loop to closed-loop evaluation — targeted long-tail data and distribution matter more than raw volume, a negative result for naive scaling.
Open-source vision-language-action models often fail at semantic generalization despite internet-scale language pretraining — the headline promise of VLAs is the part that breaks first.
Human teleoperators outperformed an LLM-powered autonomous robot on tasks requiring spatial awareness and contextual reasoning; autonomy only won on rapid broad-information recall.
Explicit, structured intention prediction from motion history beats end-to-end implicit models for autonomous parking — a modular classical pipeline outperforming the learned monolith.

A purely classical incremental bidirectional RRT achieves 22x faster replanning than RRTX with 19.8% shorter paths — no learning required, classical sampling planners still posting order-of-magnitude wins.
Five-week in-the-wild HRI study: users' subjective understanding of a robot grew with exposure while their objective understanding did not — familiarity breeds confident, wrong mental models.
On a Franka Panda with depth cameras, computational energy consumption is comparable to mechanical energy — the compute stack, not the motors, is becoming the energy story for lightweight cobots.

humanoid.signal: Interaction-mesh retargeting (Laplacian deformation minimization) from OMOMO/LAFAN1 MoCap produces contact-preserving training data that lets a Unitree G1 do 30s parkour and loco-manipulation with 5 reward terms and no curriculum — data engines, not reward design, are the humanoid moat.

AMS: one policy on a real Unitree G1 spans agile skills (dancing, running) and zero-shot extreme balance ('Ip Man squat') by mixing MoCap with physically constrained synthetic balance motions and a hybrid reward scheme.

Caltech's CLF-RL: control Lyapunov function rewards built from LIP and HZD gait-library references stabilize bipedal RL training; references used only at training time, lightweight policy deployed on Unitree G1.
Honda R&D + IIT give ubiquitous centroidal MPC rigorous closed-loop stability certificates via adaptive control and CLFs, validated on the 56.7 kg ergoCub humanoid and 21 kg Aliengo quadruped.

Cross-embodiment latent space (TU Wien) trained only on human data controls single-arm, dual-arm, and legged humanoids; adding a new robot needs just a lightweight embedding layer — morphology-agnostic control is getting cheap.

DexHand 021: commercial 1 kg tendon-driven hand, 12 active + 7 passive DoF, >10 N per-finger load, <0.2 N force-estimation error, 33 GRASP-taxonomy motions — Chinese supply chain pushing industrial-grade dexterous hands.

FeLaN extends Deep Lagrangian Networks to floating-base dynamics with physically consistent inertia parameterization (branch sparsity, triangle inequality on eigenvalues), with a released multi-humanoid/quadruped dataset — grey-box system ID arriving for humanoids.
KIST whole-body control achieves human-like knee-stretch (straight-knee) walking on flat-foot humanoids, moving past the perpetually bent-knee gait standard.

Unitree H1 and G1 are now the default sim-to-real validation hardware even for general manipulation-learning frameworks (CorrectManip) — commodity humanoid platforms have become the reference testbed.

themes.name: VLAs as base models to be patched, not built
themes.whats_new: Papers no longer propose new VLA architectures; they take pi0/OpenVLA/GR00T-class models as given and bolt on missing capabilities — a pretrained depth transformer for spatial reasoning (DepthVLA), recycled ViT register tokens for spatial context in compressed models (RetoVLA), force-aware curriculum fine-tuning with a variational information bottleneck for contact-rich tasks (CRAFT), route-conditioned two-stage SFT+RFT for urban navigation (UrbanVLA), and post-training human preference optimization to fix imitation-learning ceilings (TuI1LB.21). The novelty is in the adapter, not the backbone.

themes.name: Zero-shot foundation-model navigation with latency engineering
themes.whats_new: Zero-shot VLN (no task-specific training) is now the assumed setting; the new frontier is making LLM/VLM pipelines deployable: SFCo-Nav triggers a slow LLM planner asynchronously only when graph-alignment confidence drops (3.5x faster, 50% fewer tokens), DreamNav moves from point-level to trajectory-level actions with an imagination predictor, T2-Nav adds topological graph memory and loop detection. SINGER pushes this onboard a drone via Gaussian-splatting sim training.

themes.name: Synthetic data generation expands past rigid bodies and fixed cameras
themes.whats_new: The MimicGen-style automated data-generation paradigm is being extended to previously hard regimes: deformable objects across 4 embodiments incl. humanoid and surgical (SoftMimicGen), viewpoint diversity via segmentation-conditioned sim2real image translation (MANGO, +40pp on shifted views), human-video pretraining via semantic action flow (ViSA-Flow), and progressive coarse-to-fine resolution distillation in granular-soil excavation sim (PRPD, 7x faster sampling). Real demos are now the scarce resource everyone is engineering around.

themes.name: GPU-parallel sampling optimization as a planning substrate
themes.whats_new: Massively parallel sampling (MPPI, particle optimization) compiled to CUDA/Warp kernels is replacing both hierarchical TAMP and handcrafted gait MPC: SPaSM does sequential manipulation planning in milliseconds (4000x speedup) by fusing constraint evaluation and gradients into kernels; AERO-MPPI runs anchor-guided ensembles of MPPI onboard a Jetson at 7+ m/s flight; ThI1I.109 gets emergent trotting/galloping/backflips from spline-parameterized MPPI with no reference gaits — on CPU. ThI1I.126 hybridizes flow matching with MPPI in a generation-refinement loop.

themes.name: Force/tactile data collection for contact-rich learning
themes.whats_new: Recognition that vision-only demos hide contact events: UMI-style handheld collectors now integrate ViTac sensors, F/T sensing and pose tracking (TacUMI, >90% segmentation of contact-event boundaries), homologous leader-follower teleop collects synchronized force data for VLA fine-tuning (CRAFT), and kinesthetic teaching explicitly models contact transitions with unilateral damping (ThBT1.9).

themes.name: Surgical autonomy moving from perception to acting subtasks
themes.whats_new: Medical papers in this batch attempt closed-loop physical subtasks, not just scene understanding: dual-arm nanorobotic nerve suturing with in-vivo functional recovery (TuI2I.279), VLA-learned tumor debulking in endoscopic neurosurgery (NeuroVLA), tumor boundary estimation from robotic palpation, LiDAR-based automatic probe-pose initialization for tele-echocardiography, and fast-switching phase-change variable-stiffness endoscope sheaths (16.5x stiffness change in 30s).

methods_shift: Diffusion policies are now the unmarked default for visuomotor manipulation — papers like ARRO (TuI2I.436) and HeRO (TuI2I.167) assume Diffusion Policy as the base learner and innovate on the representation fed into it, while CBS-as-protocol (ThI2I.94) casually lists diffusion alongside A*, RRT and RL as just another single-agent planner type. One level up, VLAs (pi0, OpenVLA, Octo, GR00T) have become the pretrained substrate that papers fine-tune, compress, or inject modalities into rather than train from scratch. In locomotion, RL-in-sim remains standard but the PPO monoculture is being challenged: AMP+SAC beats AMP+PPO off-policy (TuI2I.270), and residual RL over stochastic base policies (ThI1I.382) displaces full fine-tuning. Simultaneously, GPU-parallel sampling-based MPC (MPPI variants: SPaSM, AERO-MPPI, ThI1I.109) is displacing both gradient-based MPC and learned policies for planning — even producing emergent gaits with no training at all. Hybrids of generative models and sampling MPC (flow matching + MPPI, ThI1I.126) are emerging as the synthesis. Classical machinery is not retreating in state estimation: factor graphs (TuI1I.398), pose graph optimization (TuI2I.112), and handcrafted descriptors (ThI2I.402) still own SLAM/localization, and CBF-based formal safety filters (TuI2I.22, TuI1I.244, WeI1I.185) are the standard wrapper around learned or sampled motion.

notable.why: Rigorous negative result on a flagship VLA: NVIDIA GR00T N1.6 scores 90% on pick-a-can but 4.5% on a multi-step drawer task in SimplerEnv, quantifying how badly imitation learning collapses on long horizons.
SPaSM claims a 4000x speedup over existing sequential-manipulation planners with 100% success by compiling the entire sample-and-optimize loop into CUDA kernels — millisecond TAMP.
A USV with 95% thruster degradation completes harbor return in 91.25% of 320 trials (baselines: 0%) by exploiting wind and waves as virtual actuators instead of rejecting them.

Spline-parameterized MPPI produces emergent trotting, galloping, jumping and backflips on a Go2 with no reference gaits, no pre-training, and real-time on a standard CPU — undercutting both RL and GPU-MPPI orthodoxy.

Demonstrates provably 'perfectly undetectable' false-data-injection attacks on mobile-robot trajectory tracking via affine signal transformations, validated on a real Turtlebot — an unusual offensive-security result at a robotics venue.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: SoftMimicGen — automated synthetic-demonstration generation extended to deformable objects (rope, towels, tissue) across four embodiments incl. humanoid, feeding the Isaac-style sim-data flywheel.

industry_signal.company: Boston Dynamics AI Institute / Robotics and AI Institute
