industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Uncertainty-guided residual RL to cheaply adapt pretrained (incl. diffusion) base policies, with zero-shot sim-to-real deployment.
EMPERROR, a transformer-based generative perception-error model for stress-testing self-driving planners (raises planner collision rate up to 85% with realistic detector noise).
CooperDrive — V2X object-level cooperative perception feeding planning at ~90 kbps and 89 ms latency for occluded-intersection driving.
UrbanVLA — route-conditioned VLA for sidewalk delivery robots, trained with SFT on web-video trajectories then real-world RFT.

industry_signal.company: Beijing Innovation Center of Humanoid Robotics
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: CRAFT — force-aware curriculum fine-tuning plus a leader-follower force-data teleop rig to make VLAs handle contact-rich manipulation.
TacUMI — a handheld UMI-style multi-modal (ViTac + F/T + pose) demonstration-collection gripper for contact-rich task learning.
HeRO — diffusion policy conditioned on hierarchical DINOv2+Stable-Diffusion semantic fields for pose-aware bimanual manipulation.
RL-based coverage path planning for wiping/covering 3D surfaces with deformable objects, deployed on a Kinova arm.
Swarm-ReID — decentralized open-world person re-identification across a robot swarm with online gallery construction.

contrarian.finding: Imitation-learned VLAs hit a wall on multi-step tasks (90% to 4.5% success as complexity rises); authors document missing recovery behaviors and compounding distribution shift, arguing demos alone cannot teach trajectory quality.

Classical sampling-based MPC with a cubic Hermite spline parameterization achieves emergent quadruped gaits, jumps and backflips with zero learning, zero reference motions, and CPU-only compute — capabilities usually showcased as RL victories.

Benchmark of 14 panoptic-segmentation backbones on degraded automotive images finds generic image restoration does NOT consistently improve downstream perception, countering the 'just restore the image' assumption.

Realistic perception noise (modeled generatively from actual detectors) raises an imitation-learned driving planner's collision rate by up to 85%, exposing how brittle learned planners are once the ground-truth-state evaluation assumption is dropped.

Instead of collecting more demonstrations, ReSET has the robot rearrange out-of-distribution initial states back toward the training distribution — beating diffusion policies and VLAs at equal total data.
Swapping PPO for off-policy SAC in Adversarial Motion Priors yields higher imitation rewards and better sample efficiency on quadruped gaits, challenging the field-wide AMP+PPO default.

humanoid.signal: Reference-free MPPI generates real-time quadruped locomotion on Unitree Go2 and, in sim, humanoid locomotion and dynamic handstand balancing without gait libraries, offline training, or GPU — a cheap-compute path to legged control.
CBF-embedded distributed NMPC enables safe cooperative locomotion of teams of up to four Unitree A1 quadrupeds under pushes and rough terrain, with full-order whole-body tracking underneath.
RL framework with paired inverse/forward dynamics models lets quadrupeds carry active, shifting loads (dynamic mass and CoM) over complex terrain — relevant to legged logistics payloads.

Beijing Innovation Center of Humanoid Robotics is investing in force-signal curricula and force-capable teleop rigs for VLA fine-tuning, signaling that humanoid manipulation stacks are hitting the limits of vision-only demos.
NVIDIA's SoftMimicGen includes a humanoid embodiment in its deformable-object synthetic-data suite — humanoids are now a standard target embodiment in sim data pipelines, not a special case.

A 1.25 kg, 0.33 m underactuated monoped with two reaction wheels achieves rolling, aimed jumping, aerial reorientation and self-righting with only three actuators — minimal-actuation legged locomotion for low-gravity bodies.

themes.name: Foundation models (VLM/LLM/VLA) as the robot's reasoning layer
themes.whats_new: The argument has shifted from 'can a VLA control a robot' to practicality and verification: LightVLA (WeI1I.142) shrinks VLAs to consumer GPUs and unifies navigation+manipulation; VeriGraph (ThI2I.226) wraps VLM planners in scene-graph feasibility checks; OsmAG-LLM (TuI2I.398) deliberately abandons high-fidelity open-vocab maps in favor of LLM priors over coarse maps because detailed maps go stale; SIFToM (TuI2I.320) adds theory-of-mind inference so a lightweight VLM beats Gemini 2.5 Pro on noisy spoken instructions.

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting as infrastructure, not novelty
themes.whats_new: GS has moved past novel-view synthesis into being a workhorse component: GSWorld (TuI1I.48) couples Gaussian-on-Mesh assets with physics engines for closed-loop policy training and reproducible real-robot benchmarking; RealMirror (TuI1I.179) uses 3DGS reconstruction to get zero-shot sim2real VLA transfer with no fine-tuning; GaussianCaR (WeI2I.120) repurposes splatting as a universal view transformer for camera-radar BEV fusion at 3.2x faster inference.

themes.name: Hybrid stacks: learning wrapped around model-based control rather than replacing it
themes.whats_new: Residual and hierarchical compositions are the recipe of the moment: residual off-policy RL fine-tunes black-box BC policies with sparse binary rewards (TuI2I.230); KAIST bolts modular residual learners onto an MPC footstep planner on a real quadruped (ThI1I.26); Tsinghua embeds a Chambolle-Pock convex QP whole-body controller inside massively-parallel RL training (ThI2I.413); EMBRL (WeI2I.243) puts classical Kalman/particle filters inside deep model-based RL via EM.

themes.name: Diffusion/generative models for scenes, trajectories, and world models
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is now the default generative tool well beyond policies: R3DPA (WeI2I.197) transfers RGB-pretrained generative priors into LiDAR point-cloud generation; EP-Diffuser (TuI1I.29) gets SOTA traffic-scene distributions from a much smaller model via polynomial representations; UniFuture (WeI2I.65) couples RGB and depth in one 4D driving world model; guided diffusion conditions trajectory refinement on online-estimated dynamic limits for zero-shot fault adaptation (WeI1I.322).

themes.name: Safety and formal guarantees migrating into learned/latent systems
themes.whats_new: Safety machinery is being ported into learned representations: AnySafe (WeI2I.194) parameterizes Hamilton-Jacobi latent safety filters by constraint images at runtime with conformal calibration; ILCL (ThI2I.353) learns temporal-logic constraints from demonstrations via a GA + constrained-RL game; STLCG++ (TuI1I.22) makes STL robustness differentiable and parallel for gradient-based pipelines; mixed-integer QP shared control gives recursive feasibility under non-convex constraints validated on 66 users (WeI1I.102).

themes.name: Localization/SLAM hardened for degraded sensing
themes.whats_new: The frontier is degradation handling, not benchmark accuracy: AQUA-SLAM (WeAT3.9) tightly couples DVL-stereo-IMU with online extrinsic calibration for turbid water; Good Weights (WeI1I.117) adaptively reweights dead reckoning into every module of visual SLAM; implicit LiDAR SLAM adds learned SDF uncertainty and incidence-angle-aware sampling (ThI1I.104); OMCL (ThI1I.421) puts open-vocabulary vision-language features inside classic Monte Carlo Localization so language can initialize global localization.

methods_shift: In manipulation, diffusion policies and VLAs are now the unargued baseline — papers fix their weaknesses rather than justify them: MoE-DP (WeI1I.153) adds skill decomposition and failure recovery to diffusion policies, LightVLA (WeI1I.142) attacks parameter bloat and pre-training cost, MOVE (ThI1I.192) attacks their data-collection bottleneck, and residual off-policy RL (TuI2I.230) treats the BC/VLA policy as a frozen black box to be corrected. In locomotion, RL-in-sim with zero-shot sim2real is the default recipe (SURF-Loco ThI2I.144, LF2WB WeI1I.236), but pure end-to-end RL is being displaced by hybrid hierarchies that keep a model-based layer for safety and energy: RL planner over convex QP whole-body control (ThI2I.413), residual learners over MPC (ThI1I.26). 3D Gaussian Splatting is displacing NeRF and mesh pipelines as the default scene representation for simulators, sim2real, and even sensor fusion (TuI1I.48, TuI1I.179, WeI2I.120). LLMs/VLMs appear almost exclusively as runtime adaptation/verification wrappers around classical planners (VeriGraph ThI2I.226, AMPLIFY ThI2I.220, OsmAG-LLM TuI2I.398), not as end-to-end controllers. Meanwhile classical optimization is quietly counterattacking: differentiable first-order MPC in JAX beats a SOTA RL policy on balloon station-keeping (WeI1I.243), and a certifiably-optimal eigenvalue solver does category-level shape+pose in 100 microseconds (TuI2I.250).

notable.why: Claims the first successful real-world RL training on a humanoid robot with dexterous hands, using only sparse binary rewards via lightweight residual corrections on top of behavior-cloned policies.

Differentiable first-order MPC in JAX beats a state-of-the-art RL policy by 24% time-within-radius on high-altitude balloon station-keeping with zero offline training, directly refuting the field's assumption that model-based control is impractical there.

Category-level object shape and pose estimation with a global optimality certificate in roughly 100 microseconds per iteration — certifiable optimization at speeds normally reserved for learned regressors.
A fully embedded Whisper speech model controlling a soft hip exosuit at ~9 ms latency, with metabolic measurements showing a 20.9% reduction in walking energy cost on voice command.
A security paper at a robotics conference demonstrating Discord weaponized as a covert command-and-control channel for UAV hijacking — a threat model robotics venues almost never touch.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: LiDAR scene generation (R3DPA) that transfers image-pretrained generative priors to point clouds — synthetic 3D data to cover autonomous-driving data scarcity.
Parameter-efficient diffusion model for multi-modal traffic scene prediction with OoD robustness, aimed at AV safety cases.
Large-scale Gaussian Splatting reconstruction of driving scenes for high-fidelity autonomous-driving simulation.
UniFuture, a 4D driving world model jointly forecasting future RGB and depth for AV planning.
Sparse-view zero-shot 3D instance segmentation for language-grounded grasping in clutter — warehouse/factory manipulation perception.
SpikeATac, a 16-taxel PVDF+capacitive multimodal tactile finger enabling in-hand manipulation of fragile objects on dexterous hands.
GSWorld, a Gaussian-Splatting-plus-physics simulator for sim2real manipulation policy training and reproducible real-robot benchmarking.
Surfel-based RL locomotion for hexapods targeting industrial-terrain inspection deployments.
RealMirror, an open-source humanoid VLA platform with 3DGS-reconstructed environments claiming zero-shot sim2real without fine-tuning.
A2, action-prior alignment for language-conditioned pick-and-place in clutter with one learned attention layer over foundation priors.
Tool-aware learned collision avoidance for collaborative robots running in under 10 ms with ~60% less compute than GPU planners.
Astrobee free-flying robots operating on the International Space Station — a retrospective systems paper on a deployed platform.

contrarian.finding: Model-based control beats RL: gradient-based FOMPC outperforms a state-of-the-art RL station-keeping policy by 24% with no offline training, explicitly revisiting prior work's dismissal of model-based approaches under wind uncertainty.

Honest sim2real negative result: despite a real robot climbing a step 51% of wheel radius, the authors state their mass-spring-damper model's structural limitations and parameter-identification challenges hinder sim-to-real transfer.
LiDAR-only odometry pushed 'in an era dominated by multi-sensor fusion' — Onion-LO++ beats SOTA across degenerate scenes, high-speed motion, and aerial mapping without fusing additional sensors.

A classical self-consistent-field eigenvalue solver with global optimality certificates does category-level shape+pose in under a millisecond — no neural pose regressor needed past the keypoint front-end.

Benchmarking exposé: Event-LAB shows large performance swings in event-based localization come from event-collection and frame-generation parameters, implying many published cross-method comparisons were not apples-to-apples.

Modeling humility from MIT: their computational stop-rotor aircraft models account for only up to 40% of variance observed in real experiments, quantifying how far first-principles models remain from hardware.

humanoid.signal: First claimed real-world RL training on a humanoid with dexterous hands — residual off-policy RL over BC bases with sparse binary rewards, a practical recipe for improving deployed humanoid manipulation without sim.

RealMirror: open-source low-cost humanoid VLA stack (data collection, training, benchmark) with 3DGS-reconstructed scenes; claims zero-shot sim2real task execution with no real-robot fine-tuning — directly relevant to cheap humanoid dev loops.
Dexora, an open-source VLA for high-DoF bimanual dexterity, placed in an award session — open-source humanoid manipulation stacks are now award-track material.

Locomotion recipe: a Chambolle-Pock primal-dual QP whole-body controller fast enough for both massively-parallel RL training and onboard real-time deployment, yielding measured gains in energy consumption and constraint satisfaction on a real quadruped.

Residual learning modules grafted onto an MPC footstep planner and dynamic model on a real quadruped — the hybrid keeps model-based interpretability while absorbing model mismatch, and makes the nominal controller less tuning-sensitive.

LF2WB: terrain memory written during forward walking enables collision-free backward locomotion with no rear camera, with constant-size-state recurrent inference explicitly designed for onboard processors on low-cost legged robots.

Theory result unifying the Signorini contact law with the zero-moment point via conic complementarity ('extended center of pressure') — a cleaner mathematical foundation for locomotion contact control and simulation.

HYPERDYNE: a reconfigurable testbed and protocol for benchmarking actuator dynamics (backdrivability, transparency, friction, frequency response) beyond datasheet torque/speed specs, demonstrated on a quasi-direct-drive actuator — directly useful when selecting/validating humanoid actuators.
A quartic oscillator template model (linear leg stiffness + nonlinear energy regulation) that converges to a limit cycle, proposed as a control template for bipedal running.

themes.name: Diffusion/flow policies enter their optimization era
themes.whats_new: Nobody is arguing for diffusion policies anymore — papers now make them cheaper, steerable, and robust. Mini Diffuser's two-level mini-batching exploits the low-dimensional action target to cut training to 5% time/7% memory; FDP factorizes the conditioning so modalities (vision vs tactile vs proprio) can be prioritized, fixing catastrophic failure under occlusion; GRITS adds a differentiable spillage predictor as guidance at sampling time. Flow matching is appearing as the successor (Human2Nav adds training-free feasibility guidance at inference to retarget human-video trajectories to arbitrary robot kinematics).

themes.name: VLMs/LLMs as load-bearing components, increasingly fine-tuned rather than prompted
themes.whats_new: The shift is from zero-shot prompting to fine-tuning the VLM as the actual policy or supervisor: FiLM-Nav fine-tunes a VLM directly as the frontier-selection navigation policy (SOTA on HM3D ObjectNav/OVON); AutoFocus-IL uses VLMs to auto-generate saliency maps that replace human gaze annotation for IL regularization; TuAT1.2 pairs in-context VLM planning with RL-trained low-level skills for 92% success on long-horizon cable routing. For VLAs, PEFT is being rethought — LoRA-SP shows robotics transfer needs much higher, task-varying intrinsic rank (r≈128 vs r=4-8 for LLMs).

themes.name: Human video as a substitute for robot teleoperation data
themes.whats_new: Multiple papers extract robot-usable supervision from raw human video without auxiliary annotation: TuI1I.167 trains a pi0-architecture VLA from 6DoF trajectories auto-extracted from egocentric videos (EgoScaler) and matches real-robot-data pretraining; SFCrP (TuI2I.53) uses scene-flow as a cross-embodiment intermediate; CorDex (ThI2I.292) bootstraps dexterous functional grasping from a single human demo via a correspondence-based synthetic data engine.

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting as the default scene representation, now task-aware
themes.whats_new: GS has displaced NeRF and is being pushed beyond rendering into decision-making: VISTA plans exploration trajectories over an online semantic GS map with a viewpoint-semantic coverage metric; UP-SLAM adds training-free per-pixel motion-uncertainty estimation for dynamic scenes (+59.8% localization); SMS chains GS reconstruction + VLM material inference + physics simulation for physically grounded planning; GSRender replaces NeRF sampling for weakly supervised occupancy.

themes.name: Tactile sensing matures: simulation throughput and microsecond inference
themes.whats_new: Tactile is moving from sensor demos to infrastructure: ETac's data-driven elastomer deformation model runs 4,096 parallel RL environments at 869 FPS on one 4090 (FEM-comparable fidelity); WeI2I.408 does incipient slip detection in 0.42 microseconds with a 0.375 kB hyperdimensional-computing model on FPGA; Vi-TacMan codifies a vision-plans/touch-controls division of labor for articulated objects; SuckTac embeds a camera-based tactile sensor inside a suction cup.

themes.name: Legged robustness via redundancy, adversaries, and skill selection rather than bigger end-to-end policies
themes.whats_new: The recipe is shifting from monolithic end-to-end DRL toward structured robustness: RENet's dual redundant estimators keep quadrupeds walking through onboard vision collapse; VOCALoco selects among pre-trained policies by predicted viability and cost-of-transport instead of one end-to-end policy; WeI2I.144 gives the adversary privileged observations during training, more than doubling attack success and hardening the deployed policy.

methods_shift: Diffusion policies are now the unquestioned default for manipulation imitation learning — the batch contains no vanilla behavior-cloning-MLP manipulation paper, and the diffusion papers are all second-generation (efficiency: ThI1I.379; factorization: ThBT2.9; guidance: TuAT1.1), with conditional flow matching emerging as the lighter successor (WeBT2.2). RL-in-sim (mostly PPO in Isaac-style massively parallel sims) is the default for legged locomotion and is spreading to tactile manipulation via fast surrogate simulators (TuBT1.5, 4096 parallel envs). VLMs are displacing both hand-built semantic pipelines and zero-shot LLM prompting: the new pattern is fine-tune-the-VLM-as-policy (WeI2I.245) or VLM-as-automatic-annotator (ThI1I.323, TuI1I.207), with LoRA-style PEFT being re-engineered for robotics' higher intrinsic rank (WeI1I.44). 3D Gaussian Splatting has effectively replaced NeRF everywhere it appears (TuI2I.64, ThI2I.224, WeI2I.410). Meanwhile classical model-based methods are not retreating — they are being modernized: sampling-based MPC is explicitly described as resurgent with shared GPU tooling (TuI2I.199 judo, MuJoCo backend), recursive rigid-body dynamics gets new linear-complexity loop-constrained algorithms in Pinocchio (ThI1I.431), and CBF/QP safety layers wrap learned components (ThI2I.123 learns neural CBFs from demonstrations via inverse constraint learning; TuI1I.347 extends HOCBFs to whole-body soft-robot contact). The dominant architecture is hybrid: VLM/LLM for semantics and planning, diffusion/RL for control, classical QP/CBF/MPC for safety and constraints.

notable.why: Incipient slip detection in 0.42 microseconds on FPGA with a 0.375 kB hyperdimensional-computing model — a claimed 10^4x speedup over CPU learning-based detectors, at 91.78% accuracy.
Mini Diffuser reaches 95% of SOTA multi-task diffusion-policy performance using only 5% of the training time and 7% of the memory, via a simple observation that action targets are low-dimensional.

A pi0-architecture VLA pretrained purely on trajectories auto-extracted from raw egocentric human video (no teleoperation, no hand-pose annotation) is competitive with pretraining on real-robot datasets and adds 20%+ success over from-scratch.

Huawei's psychophysics-style benchmark shows visual clutter cuts VLA manipulation success by up to 34%, that different VLAs barely agree on which scenarios they succeed in, and that fine-tuning on augmented data does not fully fix it.

First fully self-supervised point-cloud single-object tracker (pseudo-labels from scene-flow clustering + forecasting pretext task) matches and sometimes beats fully supervised trackers on KITTI/nuScenes/Waymo.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: ScheduleStream — GPU-accelerated task-and-motion planning AND scheduling for bimanual/humanoid robots, extending TAMP to produce parallel multi-arm schedules.

industry_signal.company: Boston Dynamics AI Institute / Robotics and AI Institute
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: judo — open-source Python/MuJoCo tooling for sampling-based MPC, deployed on in-hand cube rotation and quadruped loco-manipulation; positioning sampling-based MPC as shared infrastructure.
A clutter-controlled evaluation protocol for VLA manipulation policies — industrial-grade benchmarking to find where foundation-model policies break.
A production auto-annotation system for delayed/false automatic-emergency-braking triggers under extreme class imbalance, cutting manual labeling 50% and feeding continuous AEB improvement.
SHIFT — semantic and terrain-aware coverage planning, validated on robotic vacuum cleaners (consumer cleaning robots).
Whole-body contact-force safety guarantees (high-order CBF/CLF on Cosserat soft-robot models) for compliant manipulators that physically interact with people.
GRITS — spillage-aware guided diffusion policy for food scooping, aimed at food-prep/service robot deployments.

contrarian.finding: Negative result for the VLA narrative: scene clutter degrades VLA success by up to 34%, policies have low agreement on which scenes they solve, and fine-tuning on enhanced data does not remedy all clutter effects.

Argues against end-to-end DRL locomotion on safety/interpretability grounds; a modular viability-and-energy-based selector over pre-trained policies beats an end-to-end DRL policy on real stair traversal.

Frames modern LiDAR odometry as brittle — failing across LiDAR types and scenarios without manual retuning — and answers with a classical adaptive geometric pipeline (no learning) that generalizes across 11 LiDARs and 8 scenarios.

RL locomotion policies hardened with domain randomization remain fragile: giving the adversary privileged observations more than doubles attack success rate, exposing how shallow current robustness is.
Vision-conditioned quadruped RL collapses under real-world depth-sensor noise/failure outdoors; robustness requires falling back to redundant proprioceptive estimators rather than better vision.

Classical model-based progress, not learning: new recursive constrained articulated-body algorithms (LCABA/proxBBO in Pinocchio) give 6x speedups over non-recursive solvers for closed-loop humanoid mechanisms — simulator-core math is still a live frontier.

humanoid.signal: Behavior Foundation Model: CVAE + masked online distillation pretrained on large-scale behavior datasets for humanoid whole-body control, claiming task-general WBC across control modes without per-task reward engineering — the 'foundation model for humanoid control' framing is now explicit (Shanghai AI Lab et al., validated on hardware).

COLA: proprioception-only RL policy combining leader and follower roles for human-humanoid collaborative carrying (boxes, desks, stretchers) — 24.7% human-effort reduction, no external sensors, real-world across terrains; human-humanoid physical collaboration is becoming a benchmark task.

Real-time fall prediction on the full-size biped Digit: 1.1 s average lead time, zero false positives, 0.97 recovery rate — fall management treated as a deployable subsystem, relevant to any walking-humanoid program.

Dynamics infrastructure is catching up with humanoid hardware trends: linear-complexity constrained ABA for internal kinematic loops, explicitly motivated by recent humanoids using parallel/closed-loop linkages (6x speedup, in Pinocchio) — directly relevant if EUH-1 uses linkage-driven joints.
Quadruped recipe note: redundant estimator networks that hot-swap when onboard depth sensing degrades — a practical pattern for keeping RL locomotion alive through perception failure in the field.
Quadruped stair locomotion via a viability + cost-of-transport policy selector over a library of pre-trained RL gaits — energy-aware skill selection rather than one end-to-end policy.

Adversarial training with privileged-observation attackers as a complement to domain randomization for legged RL, validated on real hardware — a cheap robustness addition to a standard Isaac Lab locomotion pipeline.

Hardware minimalism datapoint: an 8 g string-tensioned tape-spring truss gives wheel-to-leg transformation ratios of 3.8-5.4x and climbs obstacles over one wheel diameter — compliant deployable structures as ultra-low-cost legs.

themes.name: Diffusion models as the generic generative engine (policies, planners, priors)
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is no longer just a manipulation-policy head: it generates 2D guidance trajectories for long-horizon IL (DTP), global paths with topology/safety constraints baked into the denoising loop (ST-DiffPlanner), human-pose forecasts for assistive pHRI (HHI-Assist), HD-map keypoints (PSKDNet), and image-to-3D mesh priors that replace CAD models for 6D pose (HIPPo). The new move is constraining or repurposing a pre-trained diffusion model at inference time rather than training task-specific networks.

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs as high-level planners over structured scene representations
themes.whats_new: The pattern has converged: build a hierarchical scene graph or primitive tree (floors/rooms/objects in KeySG, viewpoint/object/room nodes in STRIVE, skill-primitive trees in AINav), then query an LLM/VLM sparingly through RAG-style retrieval instead of every step. GIFT uses LLMs to infer demonstration intent so learned rewards transfer without retraining. The novelty is engineering around context-window and query-cost limits, not the LLM itself.

themes.name: Formal safety certificates wrapped around learning
themes.whats_new: Safety machinery is getting learned but keeps hard guarantees: observation-conditioned neural CBFs from HJ reachability via hypernetworks (ORN-CBF), RL for variable impedance restricted by construction to a manifold of Lyapunov-stable gain schedules (C-GMS), friction-cone chance constraints inside convex MPC (CCMPC), runtime compliance monitors that minimally correct black-box AV outputs (M4PC), and CBFs converted into safe goal sets (Control Barrier Corridors). The shift is from reward-penalty safety to safety-by-construction around arbitrary policies.

themes.name: Data-efficient imitation: one-shot, play data, and cheap collection hardware
themes.whats_new: Instead of scaling demos, papers attack the demo cost itself: one human video retargeted then refined by a pre-trained generalist diffusion policy (DemoDiffusion, 83.8% vs 13.8% for the pretrained policy alone), symbols+skills co-invented from 5 minutes of unsegmented play (SymSkill), handheld-gripper data made viewpoint-robust (VIL), exoskeleton-VR teleop with force feedback (MIMO), and an explicit scaling law showing why brute-force demos won't work for precision (ThI2I.162).

themes.name: Gaussian splatting and Mamba/SSM as new default backbones in SLAM and perception
themes.whats_new: 3DGS has displaced NeRF/TSDF as the dense-mapping representation (ED-SLAM fuses events+depth into GS tracking; MANG-SLAM does multi-agent neural submaps; UnsOcc uses GS rendering as auxiliary supervision for occupancy), while Mamba state-space models displace transformers where sequence length hurts (OverlapMamba for LiDAR place recognition, LH-DETR's Wavelet-Mamba backbone for UAV detection).

themes.name: Multimodal tactile/proximity skins and tactile-conditioned manipulation
themes.whats_new: Tactile sensing is going whole-body, multi-physics and cheap: moiré-pattern amplification gives interpretable 6-axis F/T in a transparent visuotactile sensor (MoiréTac), fully 3D-printed skins fuse touch with 18cm-range proximity for contact anticipation (GenTact-Prox), audio+internal-camera fusion recovers soft-finger shape under occlusion (DeepCoFi), and tactile streams change ACT-style policy outputs online (TactileAloha).

methods_shift: In manipulation, diffusion policies and ACT-style transformer policies with action chunking are now the unremarked default (DemoDiffusion, TactileAloha, VIL, DTP); the active frontier is wrapping them — trajectory guidance, world-model verification (ReOI ThI1I.314), or symbolic planners (SymSkill) — rather than replacing them. VLAs are referenced as the baseline to beat on cost/generalization grounds, not as the novelty. In navigation, LLM/VLM reasoning over hierarchical scene graphs is displacing end-to-end visual navigation policies (KeySG, STRIVE, AINav). In SLAM/dense mapping, Gaussian splatting is displacing NeRF and voxel/ESDF representations (ED-SLAM, MANG-SLAM, Eva-Tracker explicitly eliminates ESDF updates), and Mamba/SSMs are displacing transformers for long-sequence perception (OverlapMamba, LH-DETR). Notably, classical optimal control is not retreating: convex/chance-constrained MPC (CCMPC), contact-implicit MPC with global sampling (TuI1I.375), factor-graph interior-point MPC (WeI2I.419), and GPU-parallelized RRT-Connect (pRRTC, 10x speedup) all show classical formulations being supercharged by GPUs and stochastic reformulation rather than replaced by RL. Where RL appears, it is increasingly certified or constrained by construction (C-GMS) instead of free exploration.

notable.why: Derives a data scaling law for high-precision manipulation — demonstrations needed grow super-exponentially, log(N) proportional to 1/(P-c), with the precision limit c an emergent property of the whole sensor+expert system — a quantitative argument that scaling demos cannot buy assembly-grade precision.

One human video plus a frozen generalist diffusion policy hits 83.8% success across 8 real tasks where the pretrained policy alone gets 13.8% and pure kinematic retargeting 52.5% — no paired human-robot data or fine-tuning.

Award-session paper that co-invents symbolic predicates, operators, AND low-level skills from 5 minutes of unsegmented play data, then executes 12-step tasks on a real Franka with real-time symbolic-level failure recovery — a genuine IL+TAMP unification.
KUKA builds a collision-checkable model of an entire robot cell using only joint encoders and swept volumes from 3 minutes of exploratory motion — no cameras, no CAD, no calibration.
Chance-constrained convex MPC lets a Unitree Go1 carry unknown payloads exceeding 50% of body weight across indoor/outdoor terrain with zero parameter retuning.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Sensor-free, encoder-only robot cell modeling for fast redeployment of collaborative robots in flexible production lines (validated on LBR iisy).
Real-time contact-implicit MPC combining global end-effector sampling with local complementarity models for non-prehensile dexterous manipulation.
Model-free zero-shot 6D pose estimation/tracking using image-to-3D diffusion priors, removing the CAD-model requirement for warehouse/robotics perception.
Meta-learned test-time-adapting trajectory prediction for autonomous trucking, robust to cross-dataset distribution shift (nuScenes/Lyft/Waymo).
Confidence-weighted multi-RGB-D inertial odometry that stays real-time with four cameras, aimed at production vehicle/robot localization.
Observation-conditioned neural control barrier functions via HJ reachability for automotive-grade safety filters on ground robots and quadcopters.
Robust quadruped locomotion control under payload/terrain uncertainty; TRI also funds the HHI-Assist human-assistance dataset (TuI2I.42) for caregiving robots.
4D-radar-only perception (DRIFT dual point/pillar transformer) for low-cost, weather-robust automated driving.
Mixture-of-experts accelerated coordinate encoding for large-scale visual localization and rendering, presumably for XR headsets.
Shape-changing isoperimetric truss robots built from tape springs instead of inflatable tubes.
Diffusion trajectory-guided policies to cut compounding errors in long-horizon humanoid manipulation (+25% on CALVIN).
Open-source MuJoCo+ROS2 simulator for intravehicular manipulation robots on future lunar vehicles, with demonstrated sub-day sim-to-real transfer.
Responsibility/Engagement metrics for benchmarking social robot navigation behavior quality.
Latent-space co-optimization of humanoid kinematic designs learned from existing mechanisms and human motion data.

contrarian.finding: Overturns the long-held assumption that reactive replanning requires reusing/updating existing plans: solving each replan from scratch with fast asymptotically-optimal planners (EIT*) yields shorter median paths than dedicated incremental/reactive planners in changing environments.

A training-free compressed-sensing rescaling (DCT ratio matrix) of off-the-shelf monocular depth estimates beats state-of-the-art deep depth-completion networks at sampling ratios above 50%, with >15x RMSE reduction and far less VRAM.

Negative result for the LLM-as-robot-brain narrative: an agentic-LLM cognitive architecture controlling a simulated mobile manipulator hallucinates task success and refuses/fails to follow sequential instructions, despite completing structured tasks.

Against 'just scale the data': demonstration count required for precision manipulation grows super-exponentially near a system-dependent precision wall, so hardware/expert improvements (e.g., adding a wrist camera) matter more than more demos.

A deliberately simple stateless hydrodynamics model in MuJoCo, fit from just two real swimming trajectories, outperforms classical elongated-body theory and suffices as a digital twin for RL (93% target-tracking success) — complexity not required.

More sensors is not better by default: naively adding cameras to visual-inertial odometry degrades accuracy via misaligned views; confidence-based down-weighting is needed to make four cameras pay off.

humanoid.signal: Locomotion recipe: chance-constrained convex MPC (friction cones as chance constraints, solved as a QP) gives a Unitree Go1 robust walking with unknown payloads >50% body weight and zero retuning — a classical-control alternative to RL for payload robustness.

Actuator design: open-source framework (COMPAct) that optimizes planetary gearbox parameters across 4 topologies and auto-generates 3D-printable actuator CAD; measured numbers for the builds: SSPG 60-80% efficiency, 0.59 deg backlash, 242.7 Nm/rad stiffness — directly relevant to low-cost legged actuator sourcing.

Humanoid kinematic design is being automated: NAVER LABS/MIT learn a latent space from existing mechanical designs and optimize morphology against human motion retargeting loss (screw-theory joint representation + gradient-free search), replacing hand-crafted design spaces.

X-Humanoid (Beijing humanoid robotics center) is attacking long-horizon manipulation compounding error with diffusion-generated 2D guidance trajectories rather than scaling VLA pretraining — industry humanoid labs publishing data-efficiency tricks.
Legged hardware datapoint: 10 kg quadruped (LIMBERO) climbs steep rocky cliffs at 1G using single-motor spine grippers delivering >150 N grasp at only 525 g per gripper.
Humanoid head hardware: human-eye-sized active binocular vision system built on a spherical parallel mechanism (Eyevolution/CAS) — commercial actuated-eye modules for humanoids are emerging.

Hydraulic humanoid/legged control: virtual decomposition control treating closed kinematic chains as equivalent open chains, combined with adaptive RBF neural networks, for hydraulic robot legs (Zhejiang Univ).

Novel actuation component: stick-slip-free electrostatic clutch (PVC-gel friction layer, 29 N/cm2 at 100 V) used as a thin-film programmable damper for a robotic leg — lightweight motor-free impedance modulation.

themes.name: LLMs demoted from planner to one-shot semantic parser feeding structured representations
themes.whats_new: Instead of iterative LLM-in-the-loop planning, papers call the LLM once to emit a structured artifact that classical machinery then executes: DAG-Plan parses an instruction into a directed acyclic dependency graph for dual-arm parallelism (48% higher success, 84% faster than iterative querying), EmbodiedCoder uses a coding model to emit parameterized executable trajectories training-free, CoCo-TAMP uses LLM common-sense to shape POMDP beliefs (62-73% planning-time reduction), and KTH grounds LLM failure explanations in behavior trees.

themes.name: Diffusion/flow models spreading beyond policies into planning infrastructure
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is no longer just the policy head: DiffRP uses biased-noise diffusion to predict promising sampling regions for RRT-style planners (+30% region accuracy), WeI1I.244 replaces the Gaussian prior with multi-modal priors coupled to data modes for controllable motion generation, ACG adds training-free test-time guidance to flow-matching VLAs to fix demonstration jitter, and Meta's WorldPlanner uses a diffusion action sampler to keep MCTS rollouts on-manifold inside a visual world model.

themes.name: Whole-body loco-manipulation on legged platforms, split between full-order MPC and motion-prior RL
themes.whats_new: Two recipes maturing in parallel: full-order inverse-dynamics MPC built entirely from open tooling (Pinocchio+CasADi+Fatrop) hitting 80 Hz real-time on a Unitree B2+Z1 for force-controlled pulling/wiping; and RL with distilled human-motion priors plus neural SDF contact awareness for humanoid multi-contact 'embracing' of bulky objects, with working sim-to-real on commodity humanoids like the Booster T1.

themes.name: Real-to-sim data engines and certification replacing naive domain randomization
themes.whats_new: The sim2real pipeline is being industrialized from both ends: Re^3Sim rebuilds real scenes with photorealistic 3D reconstruction inside a physics simulator and gets 58% zero-shot real-world success from sim-only data; P2L (Amazon/ETH) computes probabilistic safety certificates that distinguish policies indistinguishable under domain randomization; Honda transfers offline-RL policies trained on hybrid real+sim datasets via progressive neural networks.

themes.name: 3D vision foundation models and Gaussian splatting pushed into robotics-grade regimes
themes.whats_new: Rather than retraining, papers wrap or extend foundation 3D models: VGGT-Long chunks and loop-closes the VGGT feed-forward model to kilometer-scale uncalibrated monocular reconstruction; TI-3DGS does RGB-free pure-thermal 3DGS reconstruction; ROI-GSurFisher computes Fisher information on Gaussian surfels for next-best-view; Adobe's UniVideo unifies flow, depth, and panoptic segmentation in one correspondence-matching architecture.

themes.name: Multi-robot coordination with formal uncertainty quantification
themes.whats_new: The new ingredient is statistical or formal guarantees layered onto coordination: UMBRELLA combines conformal prediction of target motion with LTL constraints and CVaR-minimizing MCTS; real-time spatiotemporal tubes give approximation-free reach-avoid-stay guarantees with unknown dynamics; precedence specs get exact solutions via augmented graphs of convex sets, orders of magnitude faster than general STL tools; DANCeRS unifies discrete and continuous swarm consensus in Gaussian belief propagation.

methods_shift: RL-in-sim with teacher-student distillation is now the unmarked default for anything legged or agile (PACE table tennis TuI2I.206, TIN wheeled-legged ThI1I.201, vision racing ThI2I.426-session paper ThI2I.426/ThI2I.288-style teacher-student distillation in WeI2I.288's vision racing), with the research effort shifting to fixing its pain points: gradient conflict between reward terms (GCR-PPO WeI1I.149), multimodal-action confusion in distillation (ThI1I.201), and certification beyond domain randomization (WeI2LB.7). Classical optimization is not being displaced at the control layer — it is resurging via better solvers: full-order inverse-dynamics MPC at 80 Hz with Fatrop (WeI2I.426), distributed Box-iLQR+ADMM (TuI2I.333), graphs-of-convex-sets planning beating temporal-logic tools by orders of magnitude (TuI1I.217), and GPU-vectorized POMDP solving (WeI2I.141). Diffusion/flow matching has displaced GANs/VAEs as the default generative backbone and is leaking out of policies into samplers, priors, and world models. LLMs are being displaced from iterative planning loops into one-shot compilation targets (DAGs, code, behavior trees, belief priors). Mamba/state-space models are starting to replace RNNs/transformers for long-horizon policy memory (M2GRPO ThI2I.157, TuI1LB.18). In perception, 3DGS has effectively replaced NeRF, conformal prediction is becoming the default uncertainty wrapper, and feed-forward 3D foundation models (VGGT) are being scaled by systems engineering rather than retraining.

notable.why: ROGIBOT, a dual-arm container unloader co-developed with CJ Logistics, claims 2,030 packages/hour across boxes, pouches, and sacks — explicitly surpassing typical human throughput, a rare deployed-logistics number at ICRA.

End-to-end RL maps raw ball positions directly to whole-body joint commands for humanoid table tennis and deploys zero-shot on a 23-DoF Booster T1, with coordinated footwork plus striking from a single policy.

VOPP vectorizes online POMDP planning into pure tensor ops, claiming 20x efficiency over the best parallel solver while using a 1000x smaller planning budget — a step-change for planning under uncertainty.

Winner of the SPRIN-D 9 km GNSS-denied flight challenge using no learning at all: LiDAR heightmap gradient-template matching against prior geodata fused in a particle filter, real-time on CPU-only hardware.

A single event camera plus wavelength-modulated structured light yields simultaneous multispectral imaging and depth (60% RMSE improvement over commercial depth sensors), validated in a real Madagascar rainforest.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: TakeAD: preference-based post-optimization of end-to-end autonomous driving stacks using expert takeover (disengagement) data from fleet operations.

industry_signal.company: CJ Logistics (with STC Engineering, IIT, SKKU)
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: ROGIBOT, a production-oriented dual-arm autonomous container unloading robot with vacuum array, gripper, and conveyor-surface end effectors, at 2,030 pieces/hour.
WorldPlanner: action-conditioned visual world models trained on unstructured play data, used with MCTS plus zeroth-order MPC to plan real-robot manipulation without task-specific demonstrations.
UniVideo: a single architecture for video optical flow, depth, and panoptic segmentation aimed at general robotic/vision perception.
SDRS: a shape-differentiable robot simulator (convex-polyhedron contact with separating-plane formulation) targeting robot co-design.
Offline RL from hybrid real+sim datasets transferred to physical arms via progressive neural networks for manipulation.
Zero-shot sim2real RL for occlusion-aware plant manipulation toward autonomous harvesting (86.7% fruit-exposure success).
Bidirectional teacher-student RL locomotion for their MagicDog-W wheeled-legged robot (45 cm obstacles, 45-degree slopes).

industry_signal.company: Robotics and AI Institute (with ETH Zurich)
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Open-tooling whole-body inverse-dynamics MPC (Pinocchio/CasADi/Fatrop) for quadruped loco-manipulation at 80 Hz.

contrarian.finding: A learning-free classical stack (LiDAR heightmap gradient matching + clustered particle filter) won a national autonomous-flight challenge over 9 km GNSS-denied terrain, running real-time on CPU-only hardware.

Explicitly reports a negative interaction: combining learned distortion-adaptive modules with explicit geometric re-parameterization (polar PETR) degrades fisheye 3D detection — the two mainstream fixes conflict rather than compose.

Honest numbers on EEG-driven robot grasping: 40.2%/62.6% online decoding accuracy and only 20.9% end-to-end task success, underscoring how far purely imagery-based BCI robot control remains from practicality.

Domain randomization alone is insufficient for safety: policies that appear equivalent under DR are shown to differ substantially once probabilistic safety certificates (Pick-to-Learn bounds) are computed.

A nonparametric, non-deep imitation learning method matches parametric/neural LfD performance while enabling instant adaptation, by cutting complexity from cubic to quadratic — classical kernels still competitive.

A lessons-learned paper on 3-robot decentralized C-SLAM in a Mars-analogue site documents how intermittent ad hoc communication degrades collaborative SLAM in practice — field reality versus benchmark assumptions.

humanoid.signal: Booster T1 (23 revolute joints) is emerging as a cheap commodity humanoid research platform; zero-shot sim-to-real of a single end-to-end RL policy doing whole-body table tennis (>=96% hit rate in sim) with physics-guided dense rewards.

Humanoid whole-body 'embracing' of bulky objects: RL with a distilled human-motion prior plus neural signed distance field for contact awareness beats end-effector-only grasping on payload and stability, with real-robot transfer.

Full-order whole-body inverse-dynamics MPC built entirely on open-source stack (Pinocchio + CasADi + Fatrop) runs at 80 Hz on Unitree B2 + Z1 — the open tooling path to torque-level whole-body control is now real-time, directly relevant to a from-scratch humanoid stack.

Teacher-student distillation breaks down on wheeled-legged platforms (multimodal teacher actions average out); fix is a mixture-density student head plus an imitation-aware reward that penalizes non-reproducible teacher actions — MagicDog-W climbs 45 cm obstacles and 45-degree slopes.

Leg kinematics as a first-class odometry sensor: foot-contact preintegration fused with LiDAR-visual-inertial in alternating optimization/ESIKF gives drift-robust state estimation for quadrupeds — joint encoders are free sensing on any legged build.

Reward-tuning pain in massively-parallel PPO locomotion is being attacked head-on: GCR-PPO resolves per-objective gradient conflicts via a multi-headed critic, +9.5% over large-scale PPO on IsaacLab locomotion/manipulation — relevant to any Isaac Lab walking pipeline.
OTTO: dynamics and control for a wheeled bipedal jumping robot, continuing the wheeled-biped form factor as a lower-cost alternative to full humanoids.

themes.name: VLMs/LLMs as orchestration layer, with classical solvers doing the geometry
themes.whats_new: VLMs are no longer pitched as end-to-end policies but as critics, labelers, and high-level planners explicitly firewalled from metric reasoning: TuI2I.138 delegates path planning to classical graph search because of LLM hallucination; ThAT1.2 has LLM agents emit PDDL solved by a classical planner with TextGrad-style prompt optimization; WeI1I.247 (CRISP) uses a VLM as a 'social critic' to iteratively refine robot motions from the MJCF file alone; TuI2I.312 (MERGE) gates VLM calls behind a lightweight change-detector to cut cost/latency 4x; TuI2I.191 uses VLM pseudo-labels to train lightweight spacecraft segmenters annotation-free; WeAT2.3 fine-tunes Qwen-VL into a surgical action policy.

themes.name: Learned components slotted INSIDE classical control loops, not replacing them
themes.whats_new: The modal recipe is a hybrid: learned value function as an MPC cost term (WeI2I.95 Grasp-MPC), Lagrangian NN inverse dynamics embedded in computed-torque control (WeI2I.422), neural Koopman operators for soft catheter MPC (WeI2I.191) and trajectory forecasting (TuI1I.313 KoopCast), PINN surrogates enabling 47 Hz nonlinear MPC of soft robots at 467x first-principles speed (WeI2I.400), actor-critic learning time-varying MPC weights online (TuI2LB.20), RL feedforward tracked by INDI/PID (WeI1I.147), RL legs + model-based admittance arm with reference governor (TuI2I.117). Physics structure is the regularizer; end-to-end is the baseline being beaten.

themes.name: Data-centric imitation learning: curate, synthesize, or generate demos instead of collecting them
themes.whats_new: The bottleneck has shifted from policy architecture to data supply chain. SCIZOR (ThBT2.3, NVIDIA) does self-supervised transition-level curation (+15.4% with less data); AnchorDream (TuI2I.274, Toyota) repurposes video diffusion conditioned on robot-motion renderings to synthesize embodiment-consistent training data (near 2x real-world gains from a handful of teleop demos); LIDE (ThI1I.71, BD AI Institute) generates demos with contact-aware model-based planners in sim; NavDP (TuI2I.160) trains a navigation diffusion policy purely in sim on 1M+ meters with privileged critic supervision and transfers zero-shot; ThI2I.288 evaluates policies in Gaussian-splat soft-body digital twins instead of on hardware.

themes.name: Gaussian Splatting becomes a functional scene representation, not a renderer
themes.whats_new: 3DGS is displacing NeRF and voxel grids across the stack: kilometer-scale monocular-inertial SLAM with 50M Gaussians (ThI2I.30 VINGS-Mono), semantic occupancy prediction (TuI1I.107 ST-GS, TuI2I.100 GaussianFormer3D), signed-distance functions derived from Gaussians feeding control barrier functions for reactive arm control (TuI1I.224 SplatCtrl), targetless LiDAR-camera calibration (TuI1I.436), and physics-coupled soft-body policy evaluation (ThI2I.288). The novelty is GS being differentiated, queried for distance, and fused with LiDAR — used as state, not pictures.

themes.name: Vision-based tactile sensing graduates from contact geometry to physical-property estimation
themes.whats_new: Tactile papers now extract material and dynamic quantities: 3D interior elasticity distribution from tactile maps via inverse physics (WeI1I.4 'tactile elastography'), dynamic force estimation under slip with frame-stacked tactile flow on TacTip (ThI1I.256, MAPE 12.54%, closed into impedance control), octopus-sucker self-adhering sensor that conforms to 0-90 degree inclines (WeI2I.114 OcTac), and force-based early slip detection at 24 mm displacement on a quadruped foot (ThI1I.150 SlipSense).

themes.name: Human gaze as a first-class robot input
themes.whats_new: Three papers use eye-tracking not for HRI studies but as a control/efficiency signal: gaze steers a robotic ultrasound probe at vessel bifurcations (ThAT2.3), foveated ViT patch tokenization driven by operator gaze cuts tokens and improves robustness in imitation learning (ThI1I.303 GIAVA on AV-ALOHA), and MoE-based gaze-target estimation from frozen foundation models (WeI2I.195).

methods_shift: Diffusion policies are now the assumed default policy class for manipulation and navigation — they appear as the thing being trained (TuI2I.160 NavDP, ThI1I.71 LIDE), the exploration prior inside off-policy RL (ThI2I.377, Bosch), the recovery mechanism via OOD detection and in-distribution projection (TuI2I.218, Honda), and notably as the baseline being beaten (WeI2I.95 Grasp-MPC outperforms diffusion and transformer policies by ~33% with a learned value function inside classical MPC). PPO-trained RL-in-sim remains the unquestioned default for locomotion and aerial control (WeI1I.147, TuI1I.90, WeBT3.2). VLAs/VLMs have displaced handcrafted task planners for semantic reasoning, but almost every paper pairs them with a classical solver — graph search (TuI2I.138), PDDL (ThAT1.2), trajectory optimization (TuI2I.218) — explicitly citing hallucination in geometric reasoning. Gaussian Splatting is displacing NeRF and voxel grids as the 3D representation (6 papers, zero new NeRF systems beyond one RL-representation use in WeI2I.328). Inside control loops, physics-structured learned models (Koopman operators, Lagrangian NNs, PINNs) are displacing generic black-box dynamics networks. Mixture-of-Experts gating is an emerging pattern on both perception (WeI2I.195) and control (WeBT3.2 fall recovery). Counter-current: TuI1I.345 argues the robotics community's DDP-lineage solvers are matched by properly implemented off-the-shelf sparse SQP.

notable.why: A non-motorized magnetorheological hand exoskeleton holds 1046 N on 5 W input (an order of magnitude better force-to-power than conventional actuation) and boosts human grip strength 41.8% with no external power — hardware beating motorized state of the art by changing the actuation principle.

A contact microphone (~200x cheaper than a force/torque sensor) estimates grinding force from sound in real time and closes a force-control loop with 4x better consistency across disc conditions — a whole sensing modality swap, not an incremental gain.

Claims the first demonstration of closed-loop constrained nonlinear MPC on a real robot, achieved not with a novel solver but with a sparsity-exploiting implementation of standard sequential quadratic programming.

Honda's diffusion-based recovery framework (OOD detection plus diffusing the full trajectory-optimization problem parameterization) improves a hardware screwdriver-turning task by 96% and is the only evaluated method that can attempt recovery without causing catastrophic task failure.

A 91-participant study of a 'Turkish ice cream' trick-playing robot quantifies a structured trade-off rare in HRI: playful deception significantly raises enjoyment and engagement while measurably lowering trust and perceived safety.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: SCIZOR, self-supervised transition-level data curation to make large-scale imitation-learning/VLA training data cheaper and better (+15.4% across benchmarks)
Grasp-MPC: closed-loop 6-DoF grasping via a value function trained on 2M synthetic grasp trajectories deployed inside MPC — productionizable grasping in clutter

industry_signal.company: Google DeepMind / Toyota Motor North America (with UC Berkeley)
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: OmniVLA, an omni-modal (pose/image/language goal) VLA foundation model for robot navigation
AnchorDream: video-diffusion world model that synthesizes embodiment-consistent robot training data from a handful of teleop demos
LIDE: contact-rich bimanual manipulation policies trained on demos generated by model-based contact planners in sim
Autonomous failure recovery for multi-fingered dexterous hands (tool use, screwdriver turning) via diffusion models
MERGE: efficient VLM-based situational grounding for multi-person human-robot group interaction, plus the GROUND dataset
Diffusion policies as exploration mechanism in hybrid off-policy RL for non-prehensile industrial manipulation
SplatCtrl: real-time Gaussian-splat scene reconstruction feeding control barrier functions for safe reactive arm control in shared human workspaces
Hierarchical LLM multi-agent planner (LLM decomposition + classical PDDL solving + automated prompt optimization) for heterogeneous multi-robot task planning
REPRESENT: inverse RL that decomposes task reward from strategy-specific reward in suboptimal, heterogeneous human demonstrations
Single mixture-of-experts fall-recovery policy for bipedal robots, zero-shot sim-to-real on hardware
Real-to-sim policy evaluation: Gaussian-splat soft-body digital twins from real videos so manipulation policies can be benchmarked without hardware
DepthMesh: online coupled depth estimation and TSDF mesh reconstruction (<3 cm) running on mobile devices for navigation and mixed reality

contrarian.finding: Argues the robotics community's enthusiasm for DDP-lineage solvers obscured that standard sparse SQP achieves state-of-the-art MPC, handles constraints naturally, and delivers the first closed-loop constrained nonlinear MPC on a real robot — classical optimization, properly implemented, beats bespoke methods.

Classical MPC with a learned value cost term outperforms end-to-end diffusion policy, transformer policy, and IQL baselines by up to 33.3% on real-world cluttered grasping — the structured controller beats the fashionable end-to-end policies.

Explicitly walls the LLM off from low-level path planning because of hallucination and weak mathematical reasoning, delegating node-level paths to classical graph search — a negative verdict on LLM geometric competence embedded in the system design.

First benchmark (ActiSeg-NL) of action-prompted video segmentation under label noise documents systematic fragility — boundary leakage, mislocalization, identity substitution — showing embodied-perception models degrade in characteristic ways under the imperfect annotations real datasets actually have.

A passive, human-powered magnetorheological exoskeleton outperforms motorized designs on holding force per watt by an order of magnitude — cutting against the assumption that more actuation/learning is the path to wearable performance.

humanoid.signal: LAAS-CNRS provides a compact, second-order-differentiable analytical model of the Cassie-style parallel knee/ankle transmissions now common in commercial humanoids, cheap enough to use inside both trajectory optimization and RL training — hardware experiments show the usual constant-reduction-ratio approximation leaves accuracy and robustness on the table. Directly relevant to any humanoid using displaced motors with linkage transmissions.

A single mixture-of-experts RL policy (gated on base-height estimate plus proprioceptive history) handles fall recovery from diverse postures on a real biped, zero-shot from sim, including on inclined slopes — fall recovery is converging on one unified policy rather than posture-specific scripts.

Probabilistically-safe bipedal rough-terrain navigation recipe: GP terrain elevation + conformal prediction confidence intervals + contraction-based reachable tubes + flywheel torque law on the LIPM, validated on Digit in MuJoCo — formal safety certificates entering legged locomotion planning.

A custom low-cost sensorized foot + LSTM on a Unitree Go1 detects early-stage slips at 24.1 mm average displacement (3.3x finer than kinematic state-estimation baselines) — foot force sensing is becoming the cheap upgrade path for terrain-aware legged control.

GIMloco replaces TCN history encoders with a control-theoretic Generic Internal Model (predesigned first-order integral system) for proprioceptive quadruped locomotion, cutting policy training time by two orders of magnitude — locomotion training cost is collapsing via better inductive biases, not more compute.

Unitree Go2 + 6-DoF arm whole-body loco-manipulation splits the stack: RL policy for legs, model-based admittance control with reference-governor safety guarantees for the arm — the emerging pattern for safe legged manipulation is hybrid, not end-to-end RL.

Quadruped-specific LiDAR-inertial odometry that explicitly estimates and compensates gait-induced vibration disturbances in the ESKF — leg-induced sensor degradation is now treated as a first-class problem for legged autonomy.

themes.name: Tactile-first dexterous manipulation
themes.whats_new: Tactile is moving from auxiliary signal to the core policy representation, with explicit fixes for its two weaknesses: spatial grounding and sensor-specificity. SaTA (TuAT3.1) anchors tactile features in the hand's kinematic frame via forward kinematics to get sub-millimeter precision (bimanual USB-C mating); TacViT (ThI2I.196) uses ViT self-attention to generalize across unseen vision-based tactile sensors, killing per-sensor CNN retraining; TranTac (WeI1I.169) gets micrometer-scale contact sensing from a single cheap 6-axis IMU in the gripper tip plus a diffusion policy, beating 6D F/T sensing. Also factor-graph fusion of tactile with quasi-static physics constraints for simultaneous in-hand pose + extrinsic contact (TuI2I.425, MERL) and RL pellet-separation purely from a 4x4 tactile skin (WeI2I.125).

themes.name: Diffusion as a component inside other stacks, not just a policy head
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is escaping the imitation-learning policy slot and being used as: a human-motion prior that guides RL to solutions direct RL cannot find (DreamControl, WeBT3.6); a 'memory of safe reactions' fused with RL for fall safety (WeI1I.114); a model-based trajectory optimizer with interior-point-style emerging barriers (EB-MBD, TuI2I.266); and a joint flow+depth estimator inside visual odometry (JFD-VO, TuI2I.245). Standard diffusion policies persist but now with adaptive multimodal fusion (ADM-DP, WeI1I.211).

themes.name: Humanoid whole-body skills, with falling treated as a first-class skill
themes.whats_new: The recipe has standardized (RL whole-body tracking policy in sim, sim-to-real on a Unitree G1), so the frontier has moved to runtime composition and failure handling: Switch (ThI2I.303) builds a kinematic-similarity skill graph with an online scheduler for any-moment skill transitions; WeI1I.114 unifies fall prevention, impact mitigation, and get-up in a single policy; Disney (TuI1I.218) learns soft, stylized falls with user-specified end poses. Two papers explicitly assume real humanoids WILL fall and engineer for it, rather than only preventing it.

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs as outer-loop planner or teacher wrapped around lower-level learners
themes.whats_new: Rather than end-to-end language-conditioned policies, LLMs steer other learners: LLM-TALE (TuI2I.247) uses LLM task- and affordance-level plans to direct RL exploration while correcting LLM suboptimality online; LITEN (ThI1I.271) has a VLM learn a low-level VLA's affordances in-context at inference time from its own failed rollouts; PCASim (ThI2I.53) uses an LLM to generate adversarial driving scenarios co-evolved with RL safety agents; One Prompt Many Rooms (WeI1I.164) has an LLM emit one declarative plan that a force-directed physics sim expands into many training layouts.

themes.name: Neural rendering and high-fidelity sim as autonomy-testing infrastructure
themes.whats_new: 3D Gaussian Splatting and successors are displacing NeRF for sensor simulation, with the new axis being multi-sensor support and speed: Waabi's SaLF (TuI2I.170) is a sparse-voxel local-field representation supporting both rasterization and raytracing (600+ FPS LiDAR, non-pinhole cameras); Huawei's ArmGS (WeI2I.213) adds multi-granularity appearance refinement for dynamic urban scenes; Unreal Robotics Lab (WeI2I.96) couples Unreal rendering with MuJoCo physics including smoke/fire/water; UDON (TuBT4.5, w/ NVIDIA) does multi-robot neural implicit mapping that survives 1% communication success.

themes.name: Classical optimization getting certificates, feasibility, and real-time speed instead of being replaced
themes.whats_new: The model-based community's answer to learning is guarantees: globally optimal data-association-free localization via semidefinite relaxation (WeI2I.29); Dynamic Parabolic CBFs that keep the safety QP feasible among 100 dynamic obstacles where collision cones fail (TuBT2.3); CASSR's continuous-reachability A* planning 30 footsteps in <125 ms, 100x faster than discretized A* and beating a commercial MIP solver (WeI2I.219); analytically derived Koopman observables giving NMPC-grade quadrotor tracking from a linear MPC with zero training data (ThI1I.370); JPL's CRESCENT flying nonlinear trajectory optimization to the Moon (TuI2I.416).

methods_shift: Diffusion models are now default generative machinery for manipulation IL, but the notable shift is their migration into other roles: priors guiding RL (DreamControl WeBT3.6), model-based trajectory optimization (EB-MBD TuI2I.266), and odometry front-ends (JFD-VO TuI2I.245). For legged/humanoid control, massively-parallel RL-in-sim with teacher-student distillation and sim-to-real onto Unitree hardware (G1, A1) is the unquestioned recipe (ThI2I.303, WeI1I.114, ThI2I.148) — the G1 is becoming what the Franka arm was for manipulation. MPPI has quietly become the default sampling-based controller for contact-rich model-based control (C-GVMM pushing TuI1I.198, DYMO-Hair TuAT3.4), and CBF-QPs the default safety layer (TuBT2.3, WeI2LB.19, TuI2I.384). In perception, pretrained foundation models are displacing per-task CNNs: TacViT replaces sensor-specific tactile CNNs (ThI2I.196), CDV-SLAM unifies geometry and semantics on a shared visual foundation model (TuI1I.92), MASTD3R-SLAM extends MAST3R two-view pointmap prediction to dynamic scenes (ThI1I.87), and SceneComplete composes pretrained VLM/segmentation/inpainting/image-to-3D modules with no task-specific training (TuAT3.5). 3DGS-derived representations are displacing NeRF for sensor simulation on speed grounds (SaLF TuI2I.170, ArmGS WeI2I.213). Meanwhile classical optimal control is not being displaced — it is getting certified (semidefinite relaxations WeI2I.29) and cheaper (Koopman LMPC replacing NMPC without training data, ThI1I.370), and hybrid mixture-of-experts schemes explicitly route between model-based and learned modules rather than choosing a side (NavMoE ThI1I.315).

notable.why: Sony AI demonstrates the first vision-only (ego camera + onboard sensors, no external localization) RL agent to reach champion-level competitive racing in Gran Turismo 7, consistently beating MPC drivers.
A 220 g hopping robot carries up to 2 kg — 9x its own weight — using bidirectional thrusters, a payload ratio far beyond both legged and aerial platforms.
JPL's CRESCENT will be the first nonlinear-optimization-based trajectory planner ever run on another celestial body, flying on the CADRE multi-rover lunar mission.

A $300 self-assembled 6-DOF arm running a unified VLA matches state-of-the-art LIBERO success rates and beats prior methods by 49% in real-world tests — foundation-model manipulation at hobbyist hardware cost.

A learned visuo-tactile policy achieves sub-millimeter bimanual USB-C mating in free space by anchoring tactile features to the hand's kinematic frame — precision territory previously conceded to model-based methods.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: RL policies for soft, stylized, user-directable falling in bipedal entertainment robots — damage mitigation with controllable end poses.
Vision-based champion-level racing agents in Gran Turismo 7, moving toward real-world-transferable autonomous racing without external localization.
SaLF, a sparse local-field neural rendering representation for fast multi-sensor (camera + spinning LiDAR) self-driving simulation.
Gaussian-splatting reconstruction of dynamic urban scenes for autonomous driving simulation.
Factor-graph tactile estimation of in-hand object pose and extrinsic contacts for precision assembly and insertion.
DreamControl: diffusion-prior-guided RL for autonomous whole-body humanoid manipulation skills on the Unitree G1.
Real-time localization confidence scoring for AMR fleets, deployed in an operating automotive factory.
Curriculum sim-to-real domain adaptation for semantic segmentation on autonomous warehouse forklifts.
Sally, a magnetic-wheeled robot with X-ray fluorescence sensing for steel-structure inspection, field-tested at an industrial site.
Multi-task co-design tooling that selects motors and gear ratios from real component catalogues via trajectory optimization.
SLoFT: end-to-end semantic localization matching camera images to 2D floorplans, trained on 1.2M images / 740K floorplans, replacing pre-built 3D maps for AR and robot localization.
Sample-efficient RL with online expert correction for autonomous catheter steering in robot-assisted endovascular intervention.

contrarian.finding: At the DARPA Triage Challenge, an expert-defined rule-based Bayesian network fused over vision outputs nearly quadrupled triage accuracy (14% to 53%) versus a vision-only learned baseline — hand-coded probabilistic reasoning beating end-to-end perception in a flagship field trial.

Pure classical search (A* with continuous convex reachability propagation) plans 30 humanoid footsteps in <125 ms, beating discretized A* by up to 100x and outperforming a commercial MIP solver — no learning anywhere in the pipeline.
Analytically derived Koopman observables give a linear MPC with NMPC-comparable quadrotor tracking, explicitly requiring zero training data — an analytical answer to data-driven Koopman methods.

Negative result on a trendy method: adding constraints to model-based diffusion causes catastrophic performance degradation even on simple 2D systems due to Monte Carlo score-estimation sample inefficiency, requiring a barrier-scheduling fix.

A mixture-of-experts that routes between classical model-based and learned traversability estimators generalizes better than any pure learned model and cuts compute 81% via training-free lazy gating — hybrid beats end-to-end.

Opens by conceding that existing visuo-tactile learning methods fail at sub-millimeter tasks that are 'routine for traditional model-based approaches' — a rare explicit admission of the learning-methods precision gap.

humanoid.signal: Anti-backlash mechanisms for cycloidal-drive quasi-direct-drive actuators: two designs cut backlash 2-3x at the cost of <2x friction, evaluated across preload on three physical prototypes (Univ. of Twente) — directly actionable for low-cost humanoid actuator design.

Maxon's multi-task co-design picks motor sizes and gear ratios per joint via trajectory optimization, then closes the loop to discrete catalogue component selection — one design serves a whole task set at near per-task-optimal efficiency.

Locomotion recipe: build a skill graph from kinematic similarity over multi-skill motion data, train one RL whole-body tracking policy on it, then use an online graph-search scheduler for safe any-moment skill transitions.

A diffusion prior trained on human motion data guides RL to whole-body loco-manipulation solutions (drawer opening, object pickup) that direct RL cannot discover, and the natural-looking motions transfer better sim-to-real on a Unitree G1.

Single unified policy covering fall prevention, impact mitigation, and rapid stand-up — sparse human demos + RL + diffusion memory — with robust sim-to-real on a Unitree G1; falling is now engineered, not just avoided.

Disney's robot-agnostic fall reward (end-pose achievement vs impact minimization vs critical-part protection) yields controlled soft falls on real bipeds with arbitrary unseen end poses specifiable at inference.

Real-time footstep planning is solved enough for deployment: 30-step plans with rotations in under 125 ms via continuous-reachability A*, beating MIP — closes the gap between footstep planning and reactive control rates.

HoMeR: a kinematics-based whole-body controller plus hybrid absolute/relative end-effector actions gets 79% success on real household mobile manipulation from only 20 demos per task — whole-body control as the substrate, learning only task-level decisions.

themes.name: LLM/VLM moves from open-loop planner to closed-loop verifier and judge
themes.whats_new: The novelty is no longer 'use an LLM to plan' but wrapping it in verification loops: scene-graph-based failure detection feeding replanning (ThAT1.6), LLM-plan + VLM-execution-assessment iteration without a world model (TuI1I.231 IDfRA), graph-based complexity routing to avoid redundant reflection (ThI2I.80 DyRef), VLM cross-modal attention as an explicit signal for when to ask clarification (TuI2I.192 CLUE), and VLMs as human-aligned evaluators of other planners (ThI2I.58 DriveCritic). Disjunctive-graph + deterministic solvers handle the hard combinatorics while the LLM only does semantics (TuBT1.4 IMR-LLM).

themes.name: Flow matching displacing diffusion as the generative policy class, plus RL fine-tuning of VLAs
themes.whats_new: Flow-matching policies are becoming the substrate: FPO (WeI1I.160) solves the intractable importance-sampling problem to do online RL fine-tuning of the pi0 flow-matching VLA; Shifted Flow Policy (WeBT2.4) replaces action chunking entirely with a time-reparameterized flow conditioned on fresh observations; SafeFlowMPC (ThI2I.71) wraps flow matching in suboptimal MPC for hard safety guarantees. Diffusion still appears for planning (WeI2I.108 M-DP with DDIM) and dual-arm flows (TuI2I.365), but the frontier work is flow-based.

themes.name: Statistical safety wrappers around learned autonomy stacks
themes.whats_new: Conformal prediction is emerging as the default distribution-free UQ tool: calibrating trajectory-predictor-derived forward reachable sets with a Bayesian OOD fallback (TuI2I.434), and learnable nonconformity scores that adapt interval width to context (WeI2I.258). Alongside: evidential deep learning inside distributionally-robust MPC (ThI1I.418), CBFs fused with energy tanks for passivity (ThI2I.407), and safety filters that transfer across tasks from a single policy (ThI1I.148). The pattern: accept the black-box policy, bolt certified safety on the outside.

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting as the default scene representation for SLAM, simulation, and language grounding
themes.whats_new: 3DGS has moved past novel-view synthesis into infrastructure: alias-free GS-SLAM with spectral pose-graph optimization (TuI2I.324 MipSLAM), radar-vision GS-SLAM outdoors (ThI2I.349), a hierarchical GS simulator spanning whole floors used to train a navigation VLA that transfers to real (TuI2I.332 NavGSim), diffusion-generated underwater terrain fused into 3DGS for sim (TuI1I.297 DreamSea), and point-level open-vocabulary querying of language-embedded Gaussians (TuI2I.115).

themes.name: Learned components injected inside classical optimal control rather than replacing it
themes.whats_new: MPC is not being displaced, it is being hybridized: Transformer-predicted repulsive potentials injected into SQP-MPC via L4CasADi (WeI1I.323), MPPI over a lightweight NN actuator-joint map for parallel humanoid ankles (TuI1I.258), Koopman embeddings with a momentum-based structure that makes linear models beat bilinear ones (ThI1I.151), certifiably-correct differentiable SDP layers (TuBT2.8), and code-generated conic ADMM MPC running on a 27g quadrotor microcontroller (TuI1I.186).

themes.name: Hysteresis- and history-aware physics modeling for soft/continuum robots
themes.whats_new: Soft robotics is converging on explicitly modeling hysteresis, creep, and snapping rather than fitting generic black boxes: Modified Prandtl-Ishlinskii feedforward giving 71% accuracy gains on ballooning actuators (ThI1I.231), a viscoelasticity-based mechanistic model with sliding-window long-term prediction for bending pneumatic muscles (WeI2I.20), locally linearized Cosserat rods for real-time contact force estimation in medical instruments (WeI1LB.14), and an LSTM-MLP capturing CTR snapping with sub-ms inference (ThI2I.6).

methods_shift: Flow matching is visibly displacing diffusion as the policy parameterization of record: FPO does online RL on the pi0 flow-matching VLA (WeI1I.160), Shifted Flow Policy kills action chunking with a time-reparameterized flow (WeBT2.4), and SafeFlowMPC embeds flow policies in MPC (ThI2I.71). VLAs (OpenVLA, Octo, pi0) are now the named baseline class to beat, and the new frontier is post-training them with RL rather than collecting more demos. VLMs/LLMs are the default semantic layer — but the role shifted from planner to verifier/judge/failure-detector (ThAT1.6, TuI1I.231, ThI2I.58), and fine-tuned open models (Qwen2.5VL in TuBT1.3) now beat GPT-4o/Claude/Gemini on domain tasks. For legged robots, RL-in-sim with an explicit actuator model remains the unchallenged recipe, extended to a 300kg hydraulic quadruped via an analytical (not neural) actuator model (ThI2I.378) and to fault-tolerant bipeds (WeBT3.4). Classical MPC is not dying — it absorbs learned terms (L4CasADi potentials in WeI1I.323, MPPI + NN joint maps in TuI1I.258) and goes embedded (Conic-TinyMPC, 10-142x faster on MCUs). Conformal prediction is quietly becoming the default uncertainty tool (TuI2I.434, WeI2I.258), and 3DGS is replacing NeRF/meshes as the scene representation for SLAM and photoreal simulators (TuI2I.324, TuI2I.332).

notable.why: First successful RL sim-to-real locomotion transfer on a 300+ kg hydraulic quadruped, using a sub-microsecond analytical actuator model that beats neural actuator nets in data-limited regimes.

Claims the first learning-based fault-tolerant bipedal locomotion framework — joint locking and power-loss faults injected in sim, with an online joint-status classifier, deployed on the real TOCABI humanoid.
Autonomous surgical suturing (STITCH 2.0 on dVRK) reaches 100% wound closure with only two human interventions — 66% more sutures in 38% less time than its predecessor.
A fine-tuned open Qwen2.5VL for guide-dog-robot walking guidance outperforms Claude 3.5 Sonnet, Gemini 2.5, and GPT-4o on holistic and factor-wise evaluations — domain data beats frontier scale.
Conic-TinyMPC runs second-order-cone MPC on a 27g Crazyflie microcontroller with 10.6x-142.7x speedups over state-of-the-art embedded solvers via cached ADMM and code generation.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Shared-autonomy teleoperation (human drives the arm, a DexGrasp-VLA copilot drives the five-finger tactile hand) to cheaply harvest high-quality demos, then an end-to-end arm-hand VLA hitting ~90% success on 50+ objects — a dexterous-manipulation data engine.

NavGSim, a hierarchical Gaussian-Splatting simulator for floor-scale photoreal navigation, used to train navigation VLA models that transfer to real robots — sim infrastructure for their humanoid/mobile-manipulation stack.
A sidewalk-delivery-robot autopilot trained by multi-scale imitation learning with corrective behavior expansion on teleoperation fleet data.
DriveCritic, a VLM-based human-aligned evaluator/judge for autonomous-driving planners, fine-tuned with SFT+RL on pairwise human preferences.
A runtime safety monitor for end-to-end AV stacks: conformal-calibrated forward reachable sets extracted from trajectory predictors, with a Bayesian OOD fallback.
BridgeTA knowledge distillation so camera-only BEV segmentation approaches LiDAR-camera fusion quality without extra inference cost — cost-down perception for production cars.
A heavy (300+ kg) hydraulic quadruped with RL locomotion — defense/industrial-scale legged platforms.
Cross-modal transformer visuo-tactile fusion with bilateral-force physics regularization for precision insertion (96.6% on TacSL) — tactile sensing silicon/application stack.
Automated retinal laser photocoagulation using instrument-integrated OCT, hitting 29-44 micrometer accuracy in ex vivo porcine eyes.

industry_signal.company: Goertek Alpha Labs / OPPO US Research
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Language-embedded Gaussian Splatting for point-level open-vocabulary 3D querying, aimed at consumer-device spatial AI.
