Grasp-pose-driven next-best-view active perception for occluded clutter, +30% grasp success in dense scenes.

contrarian.finding: Pure nonlinear MPC with no learning outperforms state-of-the-art linear MPC for nonprehensile pushing of generic planar objects — classical control still winning on contact-rich manipulation.
A momentum-based structural reformulation lets plain linear Koopman models beat more expressive bilinear ones with fewer parameters — structure beats model capacity.
An analytical hydraulic actuator model beats neural-network actuator models in data-limited regimes, and is what made sim-to-real work on a 300kg quadruped.
Explicitly reports that naive visuo-tactile fusion often fails to improve over vision alone; gains only appear with structured cross-attention plus physics-informed bilateral-force regularization.

Action chunking — the mainstream fix for compounding errors in imitation learning — is framed as the problem: it conditions on stale observations, and a simple time-shifted flow beats SOTA chunking methods.
Honest negative result: meter-scale acoustic-waveguide tactile sleeves localize contact well, but the authors concede force estimation remains insensitive and unreliable.
Snow-removal robot navigation learned directly in the real environment with reservoir computing, explicitly rejecting simulation because snowy terrain defies sim2real.

Demonstrates that differentiable-optimization learning pipelines silently fail via spurious local minima, and fixes it with certifiable convex SDP relaxations — a correctness critique of a fashionable technique.

humanoid.signal: Humanoid-Union: 260+ hours of semantically annotated humanoid motion auto-extracted from human videos, with SCHUR showing clean data/model scaling laws for high-level humanoid control (37% MPJPE improvement) — human video as the free data source is being industrialized.

TOLEBI: fault-tolerant bipedal RL (joint locking, power loss, disturbances injected in sim) with online joint-status estimation, transferred to the real TOCABI humanoid — reliability engineering arriving in locomotion learning.

Parallel-mechanism humanoid ankles (nonlinear coupled 2-DOF joints) controlled in real time via MPPI over a lightweight NN actuator-joint map with hard joint limits — the closed-kinematics actuation trend in modern humanoids needs sampling-based MPC, not analytic inverses.

Heavy legged platforms are viable RL targets: 300+ kg hydraulic quadruped (Hyundai-Rotem/KITECH) walks with an RL policy thanks to a sub-microsecond analytical hydraulic actuator model — relevant precedent that actuator-model fidelity, not simulator fidelity, is the sim2real bottleneck.
VividFace: real-time (50ms) facial expression shadowing for humanoid robot faces with human-to-humanoid motion transfer — expressive heads becoming a productized subsystem.

Virtual Humanoid Twin: personalized whole-body humanoid models solving ergonomic optimal control to generate patient-specific reference motions — humanoid simulation tooling leaking into rehab medicine.

themes.name: Foundation models as robotic infrastructure, not just policies
themes.whats_new: VLMs/LLMs/3D foundation models are being used for plumbing tasks previously done by hand: DUSt3R-style 3D foundation models replace marker-based camera calibration (WeI1I.159 Bi-JCR), LLMs autonomously author entire RL reward/curriculum/domain-randomization YAML pipelines (TuI2I.284 AURA), NVIDIA Cosmos tokenizer latents power conformal failure monitors (ThI2I.268), and an LLM acts as the feature-correspondence engine inside a classical stereo visual-servoing loop (ThI1I.270). Meanwhile VLAs proper move to 3D inputs (TuAT1.3 FP3, point-cloud diffusion transformer pre-trained on 60k trajectories) and intention-level reasoning (WeI2I.178 IntentionVLA).

themes.name: Generative policies engineered around their own weaknesses (latency, demos, mode collapse)
themes.whats_new: Diffusion/flow-matching is now the assumed policy class, so papers attack its failure modes: Generative Predictive Control (ThI1I.55) trains flow-matching policies from sampling-based MPC rollouts instead of demonstrations for fast dynamic tasks; WeAT2.2 collapses iterative denoising into a single-step flow policy to close an ultrasound servo loop at 60+ Hz; CAPE (ThI2I.63) expands trajectory modes at inference with context-aware guided refinement; diffusion is also used as a data engine for multi-sensor tactile image synthesis (ThI2I.133).

themes.name: Formal safety machinery (HJ reachability, CBFs) wrapped around learned components
themes.whats_new: Hamilton-Jacobi reachability is migrating into sampling-based MPC and latent spaces: DualGuard-MPPI (ThI2I.327) filters every MPPI sample through an HJ-safe set; latent safety filters are built on frozen/fine-tuned pre-trained vision representations, cutting violations 73.7% (ThI1I.279); HJR is solved under manifold constraints for decentralized multi-arm planning (WeI2I.192); dual-quaternion CBFs encode time-varying surgical constraints on the dVRK (ThI2I.194).

themes.name: Degradation-aware multi-modal odometry/SLAM with exotic sensor pairs
themes.whats_new: The frontier is no longer LiDAR-inertial accuracy but explicit modal-quality estimation and unusual modality pairs for degraded settings: LiDAR-thermal-inertial fusion with adaptive degradation weighting for fire/gas scenes (WeI1I.426 DaLiTI), multi-FMCW-radar odometry with online IMU bias correction underground (WeI2I.221), LiDAR-to-4D-radar knowledge distillation for all-weather place recognition (ThI2I.163), ToF-speckle+sonar fusion to map glass on a sub-300g quadrotor (WeI2I.318). 3D Gaussian Splatting is becoming the default map representation (WeI1I.12, TuI2I.316).

themes.name: Zero-shot sim-to-real via custom GPU-parallel simulators, now for deformables and multi-agent dynamics
themes.whats_new: Sim-to-real is being declared solved for harder task classes by building task-specific high-fidelity simulators: a GPU-parallel MPM thin-shell simulator enables 81.3% real-world success on turning garments right-side out with zero real demos (ThI2I.118); decentralized RL handles slack-taut cable transitions for multi-UAV payload transport and transfers zero-shot (TuI1I.254); a controlled study finds sim-trained visual navigation beats real-data-trained policies by 31 points (WeI1I.74).

themes.name: Hybrid learning + model-based control instead of end-to-end replacement
themes.whats_new: Learning parametrizes classical controllers rather than replacing them: RL tunes the SRBD dynamics, swing-leg controller and gait frequency inside a bipedal MPC (ThI1I.241); Koopman operator theory provides linear latent structure for residual policy refinement (WeI1I.134 KORR) and for MPC-based dynamic cloth folding (TuAT3.3); a SAC agent gates an NMPC in a neuro-inspired dual-pathway architecture (ThI1I.396).

methods_shift: Diffusion/flow-matching policies are now the unmarked default for manipulation imitation (ThI1I.55, ThI2I.63, WeAT2.2, ThI2I.34), with VLAs (pi_0, ECoT cited as the baselines to beat in WeI2I.178) becoming the standard backbone for instruction-following manipulation — the research energy has shifted from proposing these methods to fixing their latency, demo-hunger, and mode collapse. RL-in-sim with zero-shot transfer (IsaacLab, custom MPM/GPU simulators) is the default recipe for locomotion, aerial control, and now deformables, displacing both demonstrations and classical controllers (TuI1I.254 explicitly beats classical decentralized controllers 80% vs 44% recovery). But pure end-to-end RL is itself being displaced on bipeds by RL-augmented MPC hybrids (ThI1I.241) and Koopman-structured residual learning (WeI1I.134). In perception, DUSt3R-style 3D foundation models are displacing calibration targets and depth sensors (WeI1I.159, TuI2I.316), and 3D Gaussian Splatting is displacing TSDF/point-cloud maps in SLAM (WeI1I.12). LLMs are displacing manual reward/curriculum engineering (TuI2I.284). Counter-current: classical tools — analytical IK via screw theory (ThI2I.300), globally-stable IBVS (WeI1I.340), HJ reachability and CBFs — are returning as the verification/safety layer around learned cores rather than disappearing.

notable.why: A controlled comparison finds a simulator-trained visual navigation policy beats its real-world-data-trained counterpart by 31 points in success rate, inverting the usual sim-to-real-gap assumption.

Turning garments right-side out — highly dynamic, severely occluded deformable manipulation — solved zero-shot from a custom GPU-parallel MPM simulator at 81.3% real-hardware success with no human demonstrations.

AURA has LLM agents autonomously design, execute, and iteratively refine complete RL curricula (rewards, domain randomization, training configs) that deploy zero-shot on a custom humanoid, replacing capabilities that previously required hand-designed controllers.

Boston Dynamics AI Institute award-session paper achieves sliding, pivoting, and multi-object dexterous manipulation in a parallel-jaw gripper purely via passive brakable rollers that modulate fingertip friction — mechanism design, zero learning.

Fully automated robotic dental crown preparation inside a phantom oral cavity at 0.17 mm RMS shape error with 74% faster cutting than prior systems — a concrete clinical-translation claim for an in-mouth cutting robot.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: GeoDrive, a 3D-geometry-conditioned driving world model with precise ego-trajectory action control and scene editing for autonomous driving simulation and planning.
SNG-VLA, an end-to-end driving model that properly fuses turn-by-turn global navigation with local scene understanding, after showing existing e2e systems ignore navigation input.
HMSim, a hierarchical multi-agent urban-driving simulator (intention level + RL-refined policy level) for realistic closed-loop AV behavior training and evaluation.
CAPE, inference-time mode expansion for diffusion policies to improve collision avoidance and replanning in cluttered manipulation.
Passive-roller fingertips with brakable/pivotable anisotropic friction for dexterous in-grasp manipulation on simple grippers.
IntentionVLA, a curriculum-trained VLA that infers implicit human intentions for human-robot interaction, beating pi_0 by 18-28% success.
Runtime failure monitors for bimanual manipulators using world models in Cosmos-tokenizer latent space plus conformal prediction, targeting data-center maintenance tasks.
MIND implicit neural descriptors for one-shot transfer of robotic surface-processing trajectories across geometry-varying workpieces in high-mix low-volume manufacturing.
Zero-shot sim-to-real garment manipulation pipelines built on a custom GPU-parallel MPM cloth simulator.

contrarian.finding: Explicitly abandons the learning framework for unknown-object grasping, arguing GraspNet-style learned models are brittle to sensor noise; a similarity-matching pipeline (C-FPFH descriptors + database retrieval + grasp fine-tuning) is more robust from a single view.

BRRP shows deep-learning-only scene reconstruction is brittle to noisy real-world observations and out-of-dataset objects; a Bayesian method with retrieval-augmented priors is both more robust and better calibrated.

Negative result on end-to-end autonomous driving: many e2e systems exhibit near-zero correlation between planning output and the global navigation input they are given, failing basic navigation-following in complex scenes.
Dexterous behaviors that learned policies struggle with (in-grasp sliding, pivoting, multi-object handling) are achieved by a purely mechanical passive-roller fingertip with no learning at all.

GeoFIK, a closed-form screw-theory analytical IK for the Franka, argues none of the existing (largely numerical) solvers are satisfactory in real applications and beats three SOTA solvers on speed and reliability.

humanoid.signal: TrajBooster: end-effector trajectories as a morphology-agnostic interface let abundant wheeled-humanoid data bootstrap a bipedal VLA on Unitree G1 with only 10 minutes of target-robot teleoperation — Unitree G1 is solidifying as the default research humanoid, and cross-embodiment data recycling is the cost-cutter.

Bipedal locomotion recipe shift: instead of pure RL or pure MPC, RL parametrizes the single-rigid-body MPC's dynamics, swing-leg controller, and gait frequency, validated in NVIDIA IsaacLab on stairs, stepping stones, and low-friction surfaces.

AURA deploys LLM-generated locomotion curricula zero-shot on a custom humanoid, achieving terrain robustness and push recovery that the team's manually designed controllers never reached — curriculum/reward engineering for humanoid RL is being automated away.

Physics-informed neural networks (torque/muscle-tension physics priors) make musculoskeletal-humanoid body-schema learning feasible from small real-robot datasets, addressing the data-collection bottleneck for tendon-driven humanoids.

VB-Com (multi-lab effort incl. Shanghai AI Lab, Stanford, Berkeley) targets humanoid locomotion under deficient/blind perception via composite vision-blind RL policies — robustness to perception dropout is an active humanoid locomotion frontier.

themes.name: VLMs/LLMs as modular subsystems with verification loops, not end-to-end VLAs
themes.whats_new: Instead of monolithic vision-language-action policies, foundation models are slotted into one stage of a classical pipeline and wrapped in a closed verification loop: Goal-VLA (WeI1I.296) uses an image-generative VLM only to synthesize goal states (with a Reflection-through-Synthesis check) and argues VLA zero-shot lags the base VLMs; PerceptTwin (WeI1I.223) auto-builds an interactive sim from the perception stack plus an LLM judge to verify plans (+39% plan success for GPT5-family planners); WeI2I.206 uses an LLM itself as a static text-based simulator for corrective code generation; WTRP-Searcher (ThAT1.5) uses a VLM only to score viewpoints inside a classical Weighted Traveling Repairman formulation; OMRON's WeI2I.113 uses a VLM purely for failure diagnosis and recovery-skill selection.

themes.name: Diffusion/flow matching escaping the manipulation-policy niche
themes.whats_new: Diffusion models are now the default generative tool well outside imitation learning: Diff-VIO (TuI2I.185) is claimed as the first diffusion-based visual-inertial odometry optimizer; DOSE3 (WeI2I.365) builds a retraining-free OOD detector from a diffusion noise estimator on SE(3) trajectories; COLSON (TuI2I.262) replaces Gaussian policies in RL social navigation with diffusion policies that adapt to unseen scenarios without retraining; DynaFlow (ThI1I.63) embeds a differentiable simulator inside flow matching so all generated trajectories are physically consistent by construction.

themes.name: Learning from cheap, imperfect, or action-free data
themes.whats_new: The bottleneck being attacked is action labels, not architectures: AMPLIFY (TuI2I.286) tokenizes keypoint-track motion from action-free video and gets the first LIBERO generalization with zero in-distribution action data; DynaFlow (ThI1I.63) trains on state-only demonstrations and infers the actions via a differentiable simulator; Diffusion Stabilizer Policy (ThI2I.311) deliberately trains surgical policies on perturbed and failed trajectories with error-based filtering; WeI2I.291 extracts expert-level behavior from naive mixed-skill demos via DMP canonical shapes; Sony's WeI2I.47 transfers tool-use from 10s of demos by pretraining contact states in sim.

themes.name: Classical optimization renaissance via GPU and solver engineering
themes.whats_new: Rather than being displaced by learning, model-based control is being made real-time through solver craftsmanship: ci-SCvx (WeI2I.300, MERL) runs GPU-accelerated contact-implicit successive convexification in JAX over an order of magnitude faster than SOTA SCP toolboxes; WeI1I.303 (TRI) solves reference-free 260m-horizon full-dynamics OCPs in 55ms by choosing A-stable integrators and dynamic-equilibrium warm starts; PRED-MPPI (TuI1I.45) adds disturbance preview via a high-order extended state observer; TAG-K (WeI1I.307) revives the Kaczmarz method for embedded inertial parameter estimation, 4.8-20.7x faster on microcontrollers than RLS/KF. CBFs are the default safety layer throughout (TuI2I.238, TuI1I.422, WeI1I.336).

themes.name: Non-RGB sensing (UWB, radar, event cameras) for degraded environments
themes.whats_new: RF and event sensing moving from curiosity to primary sensor: All-UWB SLAM (TuI2I.69) does SLAM with UWB radar plus dynamically deployed UWB AOA anchor-tags in smoke/dust where LiDAR fails; ThI1I.182 fuses UWB and radar for UGV-UAV relative localization; RadarEye (TuI2LB.16) tracks liquid level during pouring with mmWave radar at 0.35cm error and 0.62ms latency, beating vision and ultrasound; ThI2I.362 ensembles event-camera reconstructions for place recognition with 77% Recall@1 gains across day-night; SpaRC (TuI2I.330) makes sparse radar-camera fusion beat dense BEV detectors (67.1 NDS on nuScenes).

themes.name: Sim2real by decoupling and by editable photorealistic sim
themes.whats_new: Two convergent recipes replace brute-force domain randomization: WeI2I.101 trains control in sim with privileged state and adapts only perception at deployment, needing 10-20 real demos; FalconGym 2.0 (WeI2I.128, Toyota NA) builds an editable Gaussian-splatting simulator with a programmatic Edit API and performance-guided refinement, achieving 98.6% zero-shot sim2real gate success on quadrotors; MOGS (TuI2I.298) makes large-scene Gaussian splatting work from monocular VI-SfM instead of LiDAR.

methods_shift: Diffusion/flow matching has become the default generative backbone and is colonizing territories far from manipulation imitation: VIO pose refinement (Diff-VIO, TuI2I.185), SE(3) OOD detection (DOSE3, WeI2I.365), RL action distributions for social navigation (COLSON, TuI2I.262), and physically-grounded motion generation (DynaFlow, ThI1I.63). VLMs/LLMs are displacing hand-built semantics everywhere, but notably NOT as end-to-end VLAs: the dominant pattern is VLM-as-component (goal-image generation in Goal-VLA, viewpoint scoring in WTRP-Searcher, failure recovery in OMRON's soft insertion) with explicit verification loops — and Goal-VLA plus the BOSS benchmark (ThI1I.17, OpenVLA dropping 54% under observation shift) openly document VLA fragility. What is NOT being displaced is classical optimal control: MPPI, successive convexification, QPs, and CBFs are thriving via GPU acceleration (JAX-based ci-SCvx) and solver engineering (55ms full-dynamics driving OCPs), and the emerging default architecture is hybrid — learned high level feeding a classical low-level executor (M4Diffuser's diffusion policy + manipulability-aware QP, TravFormer traversability + RRT*+MPC in STATE-NAV, GP-implicit-surface CBFs). RL-in-sim with privileged information / teacher-student remains the standard recipe for legged and aerial platforms (OpenHEART TuBT3.4, ThI1I.61). Factor graphs and iESKF remain the unchallenged default for multi-sensor localization.

notable.why: BOSS benchmark shows mere observation-space shift from executing preceding skills collapses imitation policies (67%/54% drops for BC and OpenVLA) and that frozen robotics encoders, point-cloud inputs, and augmentation all fail to fix it — a rigorous negative result for hierarchical skill chaining.

A single visual policy trained in an editable Gaussian-splatting sim transfers zero-shot to real quadrotor hardware at 98.6% gate success (69/70) including a moving-gate track — unusually strong sim2real evidence.
AMPLIFY achieves the first generalization to LIBERO tasks with zero in-distribution action data by learning motion tokens from action-free video, with 1.4x average gains from human videos alone.

Solves reference-free 260m-horizon full-vehicle-dynamics trajectory optimization in 55ms, with drifting emerging spontaneously as a necessary component of feasible obstacle avoidance — no learning involved.

Validated on 3,575 hours of operational data from 35 intelligent guided vehicles in a one-million-square-meter automated port — an industrial deployment scale almost never seen in academic localization papers.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: GPU-accelerated contact-implicit trajectory optimization (ci-SCvx in JAX) for legged locomotion, an order of magnitude faster than existing SCP toolboxes.
Millisecond-scale reference-free trajectory optimization for aggressive autonomous driving at the limits of handling, including emergent drifting.
FalconGym 2.0, an editable Gaussian-splatting flight simulator with programmatic track generation for training generalizable aerial visual policies.
Keypoint-EKF perception-control coupled visual servoing for textureless industrial parts, targeting precision assembly/grasping.
Pneumatically reconfigurable bistable-metasheet end-effectors that adapt curvature to different car parts, eliminating bespoke tooling on assembly lines.
Sensorless compliant-wrist object insertion with VLM-driven failure diagnosis and recovery for industrial assembly.
Few-shot tool-use skill transfer using proximity plus tactile sensing, sim-pretrained contact policies fine-tuned from human demos.
M4Diffuser: multi-view diffusion policy plus manipulability-aware QP controller for whole-body mobile manipulation.
Self-closing form-flexible suction grippers for industrial grasping.
Sample-efficient RL for legged manipulators opening heterogeneous doors, drawers, and cabinets.
Semantic-LiDAR-inertial-wheel odometry for intelligent guided vehicle fleets in automated ports, validated over 3,575 operating hours.

contrarian.finding: Negative result: observation-space shift breaks skill-chained imitation policies (OpenVLA -54%, BC methods -34 to -67%), and all three standard remedies tested — frozen robotics vision encoders, 3D point-cloud inputs, data augmentation — fail to resolve it.

Pure classical OCP solver engineering (A-stable integrators, barrier-parameter strategies, equilibrium-based initial guesses) achieves real-time aggressive driving with full dynamics, with solve-time differences of two orders of magnitude from modeling choices alone — no learned policy needed.

Argues end-to-end visuomotor learning is the wrong abstraction: decoupling control (learned in sim) from perception (aligned in real) beats end-to-end baselines on data efficiency and OOD generalization with only 10-20 real demos.
States plainly that VLA models' zero-shot capability lags significantly behind their base VLMs because instruction-vision-action data is too limited — cutting against the scale-up-VLA narrative.
In human-robot handshakes, the sophisticated adaptive synchronization had minimal effect on perceived naturalness; cheap foundational properties (weak grip, arm compliance) dominated user experience.
A tail-averaged greedy variant of the 1937 Kaczmarz method beats RLS and Kalman filtering for online inertial parameter estimation, 4.8-20.7x faster on embedded microcontrollers with 25% lower error.

humanoid.signal: STATE-NAV: first learning-based traversability estimation for bipeds — a transformer (TravFormer) predicts instability with uncertainty, defining traversability as the fastest command velocity keeping instability below a risk limit, fed into RRT*+MPC; validated on real hardware on rough terrain.

Contact-implicit locomotion planning without pre-specified contact sequences is becoming tractable in real time: GPU-accelerated successive convexification with integral cross-complementarity constraints handles stick-slip friction and impacts, beating MuJoCo MPC on energy.

OpenHEART (KAIST/KRAFTON): legged manipulator opens heterogeneous doors/drawers/cabinets via RL made sample-efficient by compressing handle/panel geometry into a compact low-dim representation (SAFE) plus an online articulation estimator — the loco-manipulation recipe is low-dim abstraction over raw sensory RL.

DynaFlow deploys flow-matching motions on a real Go1 quadruped trained only from state-only (action-free) demonstrations, with a differentiable simulator guaranteeing dynamic feasibility — a path to learning gaits from mocap/kinematic data without torque labels.

themes.name: Flow matching and diffusion as the default action/trajectory generator
themes.whats_new: Generative policies are now assumed infrastructure; the novelty is on top of them. Flow matching is explicitly displacing diffusion (HOGraspFlow's controlled comparison shows FM beats a diffusion variant for SE(3) grasp synthesis; Crowd-FM learns conditional flow-matching trajectory primitives plus a learned human-likeness scorer for inference-time selection). The other frontier is making chunked open-loop policies reactive: DCDP injects real-time dynamic corrections into pretrained diffusion policy chunks without retraining (+19% adaptability, 5% extra compute).

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting as the default scene representation, now specialized
themes.whats_new: 3DGS has fully displaced NeRF in this batch (NeRF appears only as the baseline being beaten). New work pushes it into hard regimes: physically-based BRDF rendering for nighttime driving scenes (ThI1I.81), event-camera fusion for motion-blurred reconstruction (ThI1I.416), pose-free sparse-view reconstruction bootstrapped from MASt3R priors (WeI2I.424), Gaussians replacing CAD models in 6D unseen-object detection (WeAT3.1), and Gaussian anchors as a pre-training signal for AD perception (TuI1I.261).

themes.name: Foundation models/VLMs retrofitted into control stacks — smaller, faster, with guarantees
themes.whats_new: The shift is from 'bolt a 7B VLM on' to engineering for deployment: FastDrive is a 0.9B VLM trained on structured (machine-friendly) labels with 10x inference speedup; PSS-VLMPC gives rigorous closed-loop stability guarantees for a VLM-in-the-loop MPC on a soft robot; GUIDES injects foundation-model guidance embeddings into legacy pre-trained policies rather than replacing them; SETLabs runs an agentic Qwen2.5-VL-3B orchestration for clinical scene graphs.

themes.name: Manipulation skills mined from ordinary human RGB videos
themes.whats_new: Demonstrations are moving from teleop to uncalibrated monocular human video: Actron3D distills geometry, contact priors, and action flows from 2-3 RGB-only videos per task into a neural affordance function (+14.9pp over prior art); RoboPCA's Human2Afford pipeline auto-annotates pose-centered affordances from hand-object interaction; JFTO treats human videos as object-centric guides and optimizes robot-feasible grasps and SE(3) flow-matched object trajectories rather than imitating hand motion.

themes.name: Sim2real RL via curricula, randomized-failure injection, and rapid online adaptation
themes.whats_new: Beyond vanilla domain randomization: staged curricula that rotate the gravity vector and inject stochastic adhesion failures for magnetic wall climbing (TuI2I.304); two-stage locomotion-then-skill curricula with in-sim virtual camera FOV models for humanoid dribbling (TuI2I.237); and instead of pre-deployment robustness, online policy adaptation inside a differentiable simulator that updates a quadrotor policy within 5 seconds of real-world disturbance data (TuI1I.401). RMA is extended from rigid to deformable objects via privileged particle-state encoders (TuI1I.34).

themes.name: Contact-rich and deformable simulation plus tactile sensing maturing into usable tooling
themes.whats_new: Differentiable and convex contact machinery is becoming a commodity layer: convex global-optimality formulation for filament-rigid contact enabling shoelace tying sims (TuI1I.216), distance-based randomized smoothing for differentiable collision detection on concave meshes feeding dexterous grasp refinement (WeI2I.263), FEM-based visuotactile simulation with ray-traced multi-medium refraction that transfers sim-trained tactile policies to real peg-in-hole (WeI1I.420), and zero-shot tactile-only connector insertion at 86.8% real success (TuI1I.219).

methods_shift: The clearest displacement in this batch: 3DGS has replaced NeRF as the default 3D representation (every novel-view/reconstruction paper here builds on Gaussians, with NeRF relegated to baseline status), and flow matching is starting to displace diffusion for action generation — HOGraspFlow runs the head-to-head and finds FM more stable in SE(3); Crowd-FM and JFTO also pick FM over diffusion. Diffusion policies remain the workhorse for manipulation (SCOOP'D, GD2P, RoboPCA, DCDP) but the research energy is in patching their weaknesses: closed-loop correction of action chunks, inference-time trajectory scoring/selection, and physics priors (Port-Hamiltonian modules in TuI1I.259). For locomotion, RL-in-sim with staged curricula plus domain/failure randomization is simply the default recipe (humanoid dribbling, magnetic wall climbing), and it now beats MPC baselines in those papers. Classical MPC has not disappeared — it has been recomposed as the outer loop around learned components: learned collision models inside risk-aware MPC (MonoMPC), learned NN dynamics approximated for real-time MPC with stability proofs (PSS-VLMPC), Koopman/Kalman-implicit latent linear dynamics for MPC (KALIKO), data-driven DLO models updated online inside MPC (WeI2I.436). VLMs/MLLMs are now standard in autonomous-driving stacks, but the trend is compression (0.9B FastDrive vs 7B LLaVA) and structured outputs rather than scale. Notably absent from this 98-paper sample: almost no large end-to-end VLA-policy papers — guidance, distillation, and retrofit layers dominate over monolithic policy replacement.

notable.why: Claims video diffusion models have internalized the optics of transparency and converts one into a zero-shot SOTA depth/normal estimator for transparent objects via LoRA — a striking case of repurposing generative priors label-free for a classically unsolvable perception problem.

Motional's TreeIRL is, per the authors, the first public-road demonstration of MCTS-based planning — validated over 500+ miles of real autonomous driving in Las Vegas, beating both classical and learned planners.

A quadrotor policy that retrains itself within 5 seconds of encountering an unseen real-world disturbance (wind, payload) by backpropagating through a continuously-refined differentiable simulator, cutting hovering error up to 81% vs L1-MPC.

An artificial muscle powered by literal dimethyl-ether combustion inside a McKibben actuator, claiming biological-muscle-level force and response speed from a cheap pneumatic structure — actuation by internal combustion is a genuine outlier.

Flips the LfD problem on its head: instead of better algorithms, train the human teachers — machine-teaching-trained novices show a 75% improvement in robot-teaching ability that persists long-term and generalizes to new skills.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: A production AV planner combining MCTS candidate generation with an IRL-trained human-likeness scorer, validated on 500+ miles of public-road driving in Las Vegas.
Physically-based 3D Gaussian Splatting reconstruction of nighttime driving scenes for autonomous-driving simulation/replay on nuScenes and Waymo data.
GaussianPretrain — 3D-Gaussian-anchor self-supervised visual pre-training for their AD perception stack (detection, HD map, occupancy), cheaper and faster than NeRF-based pre-training.
RT-BEVFormer — a distilled, edge-deployable transformer BEV perception network that beats prior SOTA on accuracy and speed on NVIDIA Jetson Orin, with no custom plugins, aimed at production vehicles.
FastDrive, a 0.9B-parameter VLM plus a structured-label dataset (NuScenes-S) for end-to-end driving decisions at 10x the inference speed of 7B baselines — autonomous trucking deployment focus.
Mimir, a hierarchical goal-driven diffusion planner with Laplace-distributed goal uncertainty for end-to-end autonomous driving (+20% EPDMS on NavHard).
An untethered quadrupedal magnetic wall-climbing robot with RL locomotion robust to adhesion failure, targeting steel-structure (ship/industrial) inspection.
Unattended gray-box sensor-model identification and calibration-state initialization, so new sensors can be hot-integrated into localization stacks on modular robot platforms.
A lightweight agentic multimodal framework (Qwen2.5-VL-3B + SmolAgent orchestration) producing structured scene graphs for healthcare/surgical robot perception.

contrarian.finding: Negative result on foundation models: depth estimates from vision foundation models are too noisy for zero-shot collision checking in clutter; the fix is demoting them to context features inside a learned collision model wrapped by risk-aware classical MPC.

3D surgical needle-tip localization from multi-view X-ray solved entirely with classical image processing — bilateral filtering, thresholding, morphology, flood-fill, A* trajectory extraction, and triangulation — no deep learning at all.

Highly dynamic non-prehensile ball juggling on a Franka solved with pure model-based two-stage optimal control and offline-computed trajectory libraries for real-time correction — no learning anywhere in a task that looks tailor-made for RL.

A minimalist optical-flow + uncertainty navigation stack on tiny aerial robots matches depth-based (heavier, learned) methods with orders-of-magnitude less computation — and honestly reports a 70% success rate.

Contrary to LfD's core promise, novices are bad at demonstrating tasks to robots; the effective intervention is machine-teaching training for the humans (75% improvement, retained), not a better learning algorithm.

Classical Monte Carlo tree search (with an IRL scoring function) outperforms state-of-the-art learned planners across 500+ real road miles — search plus learned cost beats end-to-end learning in deployed driving.

humanoid.signal: Humanoid soccer dribbling via a two-stage curriculum (locomotion first, then dribbling fine-tune) with a virtual camera model in sim replicating the real robot's FOV limits plus active-sensing rewards — perception-constrained sim2real is now part of the standard humanoid RL recipe.

Legged sim2real recipe for vertical surfaces: physics-based magnetic adhesion model in sim, curriculum that gradually rotates the gravity vector to vertical, then injects stochastic adhesion failures to teach slip recovery — the RL policy keeps walking through intermittent attachment loss where the perfect-adhesion MPC baseline fails, confirmed untethered on real steel.

Whole-body tactile skin path for humanoids: a fully 3D-printed-and-spray-coated EIT + pneumatic hybrid skin (Tikhonov inverse reconstruction, per-pad pneumatic calibration) demonstrated chest-mounted on a humanoid and handling multiple simultaneous contacts — cheap, scalable large-area sensing rather than expensive taxel arrays.

themes.name: VLAs/VLMs treated as deployable infrastructure, not research demos
themes.whats_new: The questions have shifted from 'can a VLA do the task' to deployment engineering: inference latency (Fast ECoT cuts embodied chain-of-thought latency 7.5x via reasoning caching + async scheduling, no retraining), non-RGB grounding (OmniVLA folds IR camera, mmWave radar, and microphone arrays into 'sensor-masked images' on an RGB-pretrained VLA backbone), and statistically rigorous evaluation (SureSim uses prediction-powered inference to combine cheap sim rollouts with few real trials, giving confidence intervals on pi_0 and diffusion-policy performance with 20-25% less hardware testing). VLMs also do structured task parsing — assembly manuals to connector-aware graphs (Manual2Skill++), scene-graph planning (SAGE), garment-pile retrieval (GarmentPile++).

themes.name: Force/contact-aware manipulation with purpose-built data-collection hardware
themes.whats_new: Compliance is becoming a learned policy output, not a controller afterthought, enabled by new capture hardware: UMI-FT mounts 6-axis F/T sensors on each finger of a handheld UMI-style collector so policies predict position + grasp force + stiffness; MiniBEE (Columbia/Samsung) is a wearable reduced-DOF bimanual rig for kinesthetic data; APEX-Glove is a <$700 open-source exoskeleton glove with 3D kinesthetic feedback. Sensing is also getting exotic: active audio emitted through the grasped object to localize extrinsic contacts (WeI2I.196, sim-trained with 'real-to-sim audio hallucination'), tactile-proprioceptive fusion via pneumatic skins + motor currents (WeI2I.214), and energy-flow abstractions for slip-aware grasp force (TuI2I.403, Toyota).

themes.name: Diffusion (and now consistency) models as universal generators beyond action policies
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is the default generative engine for grasps (T(R,O) Grasp: graph diffusion over hand-object transforms, 94.8% success, 41 grasps/s, cross-embodiment), vectorized HD maps (RefDiffMap: denoising as progressive refinement, +11.3 mAP over MapTRv2 with ResNet-18), semantically controlled traffic-scenario generation (SPATSG: spatiotemporal polygon anchors steering denoising), and uncertainty modeling inside end-to-end driving (EgoFSD). The new frontier is sampling speed: consistency models for real-time closed-loop planning (ConsistencyPlanner, Waymax) instead of iterative DDPM-style sampling.

themes.name: 3D Gaussian Splatting as the standard scene representation, displacing NeRF
themes.whats_new: 3DGS has moved from novel-view-synthesis demos to systems plumbing: LiDAR-supervised splatting with LiDAR rendering for editable highway scenes (LiHi-GS, Ford/Latitude AI), decoupled classical VO + octree-structured Gaussians for real-time dense monocular SLAM (WeI1I.392), keyframe/mapping strategies tuned for whole-scene quality (SupGS-SLAM), and 3DGS as a markerless localization backbone aligning a HoloLens and a UAV for MR-based inspection control (3DGS-Holo-Inspector, ~9 cm RMSE).

themes.name: Hybrid model-based + learned control with explicit division of labor
themes.whats_new: Rather than end-to-end RL, the recipe is: model-based optimization provides structure/feedforward, learning provides residual corrections or reference learning. RAMBO solves a QP for whole-body feedforward torques with an RL feedback policy adding corrective terms (quadruped loco-manipulation incl. bipedal walking); MULE pairs a nominal locomotion policy with an adaptive corrective policy for 2-10 kg unknown payloads; soft-robot PPO is trained inside an exact Geometric Variable-Strain physics parameterization (WeI2I.189); MPC tracks while Bayesian optimization learns the reference (ThI1I.165); imitation learning warm-starts trajectory optimization for aerial manipulators (ThI1I.397).

methods_shift: Diffusion-family models are now the assumed generative tool well beyond action policies — grasp synthesis, HD-map regression, traffic-scenario generation — and the race is to make them real-time: consistency models (ConsistencyPlanner) and inference-time caching/parallelization (Fast ECoT for VLA chain-of-thought) are displacing slow iterative sampling. VLAs (pi_0, ECoT-style models) appear as the substrate other papers accelerate, evaluate (SureSim), or extend with new sensors (OmniVLA) — i.e., the field treats them as given, displacing task-specific behavior cloning. 3D Gaussian Splatting has effectively completed its displacement of NeRF as the scene representation in SLAM and driving-scene reconstruction (SupGS-SLAM, LiHi-GS, GaussR-SLAM, WeI1I.392). In legged robotics, pure end-to-end RL-in-sim (Isaac Gym/Lab remains the standard trainer) is being displaced by hybrid stacks: QP/MPC feedforward plus RL residual feedback (RAMBO), nominal+adaptive policy pairs (MULE). Meanwhile classical estimation and control are far from dead and are still being actively extended rather than replaced: a new EM-derived Kalman filter for data association (PKF) matches learned MOT pipelines, ESGVI variational inference gets Lie-group and heavy-tailed extensions for UWB, and MPPI gets frequency-domain shaping (LP-MPPI) for real F1TENTH racing and quadrupeds.

notable.why: Claims quadruped locomotion policies trainable from scratch, entirely offline, from only a few seconds of demonstration, backed by a principled limit-cycle/Poincare-map analysis of why imitation works in the small-data regime — and verified on hardware.

A training-free, model-unchanged inference trick (caching/reusing chain-of-thought across timesteps + async decoupling of reasoning from action decoding) cuts VLA ECoT latency up to 7.5x with equal or better success rate.

Reduces multi-agent path-execution graph construction from O(R^2 T^2) to O(RT), achieving 300-8000x speedups over Action Dependency Graphs at 10,000-agent scale — turning execution, not planning, into the solved bottleneck.

A gripper that emits sound through the grasped object via conduction speakers and contact microphones to localize extrinsic contacts, trained entirely in simulation and zero-shot transferred using a 'real-to-sim audio hallucination' trick.

Graph diffusion over robot-object transforms generates dexterous grasps across multiple hand embodiments at 41 grasps/second (0.21 s inference), fast enough for closed-loop dexterous manipulation rather than open-loop grasp synthesis.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: SureSim — statistically rigorous policy evaluation that combines large-scale sim with small real-world test sets via prediction-powered inference, cutting hardware evaluation cost 20-25%.

industry_signal.company: Microsoft / Microsoft Research Asia (with Princeton, UCLA)
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: OmniVLA — a multi-sensor VLA that fuses infrared, mmWave radar, and microphone arrays into RGB-pretrained backbones via sensor-masked images (84% success vs 25-56% for baselines).
MiniBEE — a compact, wearable reduced-DOF (3+3) bimanual form factor for kinesthetic data collection and deployment of imitation policies.
TriCoSphere — an open-sourced 12-DoF three-finger coaxial spherical parallel manipulator with base-mounted actuators (4.1 kg per-finger payload, 6.5 Hz bandwidth) aimed at fast, strong robot hands.
SCU-Hand-SV — a soft funnel-shaped hand with integrated valve for milligram-scale (+/-2 mg) powder dispensing in laboratory automation for materials discovery.
LiHi-GS — LiDAR-supervised Gaussian splatting for reconstructing and editing highway driving scenes, including LiDAR rendering, to synthesize safety-critical training data.
EgoFSD — a fully sparse, ego-centric end-to-end driving stack claiming 59% lower L2 error, 92% lower collision rate, and 6.9x faster runtime than UniAD.
Tactile-driven grasp-force control using a physics-informed energy-container abstraction that learns slip-aware force regulation from scratch in minutes on hardware.

industry_signal.company: Robotics and AI Institute (with CMU, ETH Zurich)
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: RAMBO — hybrid QP-feedforward + RL-feedback whole-body control for quadruped loco-manipulation (cart pushing, plate balancing, bipedal-mode walking).
TEGA — assistive teleoperation combining EMG-based force control, AR pose tracking, visuo-tactile sensing, and a haptic vest for users with upper-limb disabilities.
Fast ECoT — inference-time acceleration (thought caching, parallel decoding, async scheduling) to make chain-of-thought VLAs real-time deployable.

industry_signal.company: Alibaba DAMO Academy (with CUHK, Shanghai AI Lab)
industry_signal.what_theyre_building: SurgVidLM — a large language model for multi-grained video understanding in robot-assisted surgery.

contrarian.finding: Case study on small local LLMs (<=14B) for highway driving: LLM-shaped rewards give RL+LLM hybrids that consistently land BETWEEN RL-only and LLM-only performance, with a systematic conservative bias that persists despite explicit efficiency instructions — a negative result for the LLM-for-control narrative.

FlightBench's head-to-head benchmark shows learning-based ego-vision quadrotor navigation still loses to classical optimization-based planners in hard cases (sharp corners, view occlusion); its wins are confined to high-speed flight and inference speed.

A purely geometric, model-based depth-perception pipeline for soft hand-exoskeleton grasp control hits state-of-the-art 91% Grasping Ability Score and generalizes to unseen objects better than data-driven learning baselines.

For robot fault/risk detection from camera input, a Gaussian Process on a low-dimensional latent space reliably detects NOVEL faults from a small amount of user data, while a standard MLP only catches faults seen in training — small-data classical ML beating deep learning where it matters.

An RL mission replanner only matches the classical LKH3 heuristic to within 10% solution quality (90% of the time) — the classical solver still wins on quality; RL's sole advantage is 85-370x faster runtime on a Raspberry Pi.

A newly derived classical Kalman filter with EM-based probabilistic data association (PKF) beats JPDAF/PMHT and matches modern multi-object tracking pipelines on MOT17/MOT20/DanceTrack in real time, without learned association stages.

In event-camera SLAM, the hybrid model-based pipeline (Edged USLAM) is more stable with less drift than the learning-based DEVO in slow/structured trajectories — the paper openly reports that no single paradigm wins across scenarios.

humanoid.signal: RAMBO (RAI Institute/CMU/ETH) recipe for loco-manipulation: model-based QP computes feedforward whole-body torques, RL policy adds feedback corrections — yields precise force modulation (cart pushing, plate balancing) in both quadrupedal AND bipedal walking modes on a quadruped.

Quadruped walking policies trained from scratch, fully offline, from only seconds of demonstration — a data-efficiency counterpoint to the massive-sim-RL recipe, grounded in limit-cycle/Poincare-map structure.

MULE: nominal + adaptive RL policy pair handles 2-10 kg unknown/shifting payloads and stairs/slopes on a Unitree Go1 with no post-deployment fine-tuning and no contact-force sensing — relevant to payload-carrying humanoid torsos.

PolygMap humanoid stair-climbing: real-time polygonal staircase plane semantic mapping from LiDAR+RGB-D+IMU fusion feeding a footstep planner, running 20-30 Hz whole-body planning on a single NVIDIA Orin.

Acoustic noise is emerging as a first-class locomotion metric: a guide-dog controller for Unitree Go1 halves walking noise vs the default controller while keeping human walking speed and stair capability — quietness as a usability requirement, validated with blind/low-vision-oriented user studies.

Fourier Intelligence (humanoid maker) open-sourced TriCoSphere: a 12-DoF 3-finger hand using coaxial spherical parallel mechanisms with all actuators at the base — 4.1 kg payload per finger, 6.5 Hz bandwidth, explicitly pitched as cost-effective; a hand architecture datapoint for EUH-1-class BOM thinking.

APEX-Glove: open-source, <700 USD dorsal hand exoskeleton (Dynamixel XL330 actuators) giving 300 Hz finger tracking + 3D kinesthetic force feedback, with motion retargeting to humanoid robot hands demonstrated — cheap teleop data collection for humanoid hands.

themes.name: Generative action policies move past vanilla diffusion
themes.whats_new: Diffusion policy is now the baseline being improved upon, not the contribution. Flow matching replaces diffusion for sampling speed but collapses modes, so VFP (TuI1I.183) adds a variational latent prior + Kantorovich OT + MoE decoder; PegasusFlow (ThI1I.64) trains the denoiser from parallel-sim environment interaction (WBFO score sampling), removing the expert-demonstration requirement entirely; FARM (TuI1I.229) conditions a diffusion policy on GelSight tactile streams and predicts grip *force* as an action dimension; S^2-Diffusion (ThI1I.367) pushes instance-level skills to category-level; diffusion also shows up as a motion-prior initializer inside force-feedback MPC (TuI1I.203).

themes.name: VLA post-training: alignment, equivariance, RL finetuning
themes.whats_new: The frontier for vision-language-action models has shifted from pretraining scale to the post-training phase, mirroring the LLM playbook. GRAPE (ThI1I.94) does trajectory-level preference alignment (learning from failures as well as successes, +58% on unseen tasks); ThI2I.164 distills real-world RL policies into chunk-based VLAs via offline reinforced finetuning; WeI1I.87 reframes cross-embodiment pretraining as designing action spaces equivariant to embodiment-configuration transforms instead of just adding data.

themes.name: Foundation-model features distilled into robot perception and maps
themes.whats_new: Rather than calling VLMs at inference, papers distill them into deployable robot-specific encoders and queryable maps: AnyThermal (TuI2I.134) distills DINOv2 into a universal thermal backbone across 4 environments; QuASH (WeI2I.158) queries VLM-embedding maps using synonym/antonym heuristics in language space; Affordance RAG (TuI1I.403) builds affordance-aware embodied memory for zero-shot mobile manipulation (85% real-world success); TuI2I.373 distills relative-depth foundation models into real-time metric surround depth via depth-bin probability transfer.

themes.name: RL recipes that eliminate expert data and hand-designed rewards
themes.whats_new: The expensive ingredients of RL (mocap datasets, reward engineering) are being replaced: KiRAS (ThI2I.142) uses keyframes + self-imitation instead of expert datasets for multi-skill quadruped locomotion; SPLC (TuI1I.121) auto-generates preference data to replace reward design for social navigation; ThI2I.278 mines failed trajectories with segmented credit assignment (Failure-Aversion Learning); TuI1I.30 uses a bootstrap curriculum seeded with preprogrammed behavior to learn decentralized give-way maneuvers.

themes.name: Classical SLAM/odometry thrives on exotic sensors and CPU-only real-time
themes.whats_new: Estimation papers compete on sensor novelty and compute frugality, not learning: RaCo-SLAM (ThAT3.1) does 4D mmWave radar SLAM at 40 Hz on a standard CPU with a physics-informed noise model; MarsLGPR (WeI1I.369) localizes a Mars-analog rover with ground-penetrating radar, beating wheel odometry in high slip; TCB-VIO (ThI1I.354) runs an MSCKF at 250 FPS on a focal-plane sensor-processor array (per-pixel compute), outperforming ORB-SLAM3/VINS-Mono; D-LIO (WeI2I.350) and DB-TSDF (ThI2I.140) both advertise CPU-only TSDF mapping with resolution-independent update time.

themes.name: Contact-rich manipulation gets non-visual senses: force, touch, sound
themes.whats_new: Force and audio move from observation to control variable: FARM (TuI1I.229) makes grip force an explicit policy output via a tactile-instrumented UMI gripper; CAVER (WeI2I.312) builds curiosity-driven audiovisual object representations with a purpose-built striking end-effector and imitates audio-only demonstrations; TuI1I.197 does non-contact micro peg-in-hole using a holographic acoustic end-effector (phased ultrasound array) with GAN-designed holograms; TactEx (WeI1I.198) fuses GelSight + LLM grounding for explainable hardness estimation.

methods_shift: In manipulation, diffusion policies and VLAs are now the unmarked default — papers assume them as baselines and contribute fixes: flow matching (VFP) displacing diffusion for inference speed, preference alignment (GRAPE) and offline RL finetuning displacing pure behavior cloning, and tactile/force conditioning displacing vision-only policies. In locomotion and agile aerial control, massively-parallel RL-in-sim with domain randomization and curricula is the assumed recipe (JuggleRL, KiRAS, multi-quadruped transport), with the innovation moving to where supervision comes from (keyframes, self-imitation, failures, preferences) rather than the algorithm. But classical optimal control is not being displaced — it is consolidating as the deployment layer: ThI1I.172 shows plain iLQR with finite-difference MuJoCo derivatives does real-time whole-body MPC on a real humanoid with minimal sim2real effort; FilterDDP (ThI1I.40) advances constrained DDP theory; the dominant hybrid pattern is learning-inside-MPC (diffusion priors initializing force-feedback MPC in TuI1I.203, online residual-dynamics learning with random Fourier features feeding MPC in ThI2I.369, RL adapting planner dynamics fidelity in TuI2I.131). In perception, supervised task-specific training is being displaced by distillation from visual foundation models (DINOv2 into thermal in TuI2I.134, depth foundation models into metric surround depth in TuI2I.373), and 3D Gaussian Splatting is displacing NeRF as the scene representation of choice (WeI1I.291, ThI2I.51). SLAM remains resolutely classical (MSCKF, factor graphs, TSDF) and increasingly markets CPU-only real-time as a feature.

notable.why: CMU/DeepMind/MIT show that bare iLQR with finite-difference MuJoCo derivatives — no learning, no offline training — achieves real-time whole-body MPC on a real full-sized humanoid and a quadruped walking on two legs, with 'few sim-to-real considerations'.
An RL policy deployed zero-shot juggles a ball on a real quadrotor for 311 hits on average (max 462) versus 14 max for the model-based baseline, and generalizes to an unseen lighter ball.

Award-session paper showing standard BC policies (ACT, Diffusion Policy, SmolVLA) secretly infer camera pose from static backgrounds — a shortcut that collapses under viewpoint shift — and that simply conditioning on camera extrinsics via Plücker ray embeddings fixes it.

First use of ground-penetrating radar for Mars rover localization: subsurface feature matching beats wheel odometry in high-slip sandy terrain, exploiting GPR sensors already flying on Perseverance and ExoMars.

Claims five orders of magnitude better solution optimality than sampling-based approaches for manipulator base placement via hierarchical sequential linear programming — an unusually strong quantitative claim.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: A cable-driven five-finger soft robotic hand with an in-palm RGB-D camera for vision-guided grasping, slip detection, and visually servoed in-hand manipulation — a rare Apple robotics-hardware publication.
View-invariant imitation-learning policies via camera-extrinsics conditioning, hardening BC/VLA policies against camera placement shifts for deployable manipulation.
CaFe-TeleVision: an immersive coarse-to-fine VR teleoperation system on a humanoid collaborative robot, aimed at ergonomic large-scale bimanual demonstration data collection.
A plug-and-play goal-driven planner that replaces coarse driving commands with explicit goal points in end-to-end driving stacks (UniAD, VAD, DiffusionDrive).
ParkDiffusion++: ego-intention-conditioned joint trajectory prediction with diffusion models for automated parking.
GRAPE: trajectory-level preference alignment for VLA models — the RLHF-style post-training layer for generalist robot policies.
MuJoCo-based real-time whole-body iLQR MPC tooling (open-sourced) for quadrupeds and humanoids.
Real-time metric-scale full-surround monocular depth via knowledge distillation from depth foundation models for production vehicles.
Normalizing-flow hierarchical goal-conditioned RL (NF-HIQL) for data-efficient long-horizon locomotion and manipulation.

contrarian.finding: A 'very simple' classical iLQR-MPC with finite-difference derivatives matches the real-world capability narrative of learned locomotion policies on real quadrupeds and a full-sized humanoid — no learning required.
Maps-of-Dynamics (spatio-temporal pattern maps) outperform learning-based long-term human motion predictors by up to 50% ADE on 60-second horizons.

Negative result on pre-trained visual representations: time-invariant PVRs are 'temporally entangled' and inherently miss task-progression cues, directly correlating with visuomotor policy failure; naive temporal feature enrichment does not fix it.
Standard imitation policies pass benchmarks by exploiting static-background shortcuts to infer camera pose; performance collapses when workspace geometry or camera placement shifts.

ScaleMaster benchmark shows SOTA deep monocular visual SLAM systems suffer severe scale drift and inter-session scale failures in realistic large-scale multi-floor indoor environments despite strong scores on existing benchmarks.
Honest sim2real degradation curve: 99% success in clean simulation drops to 73% with sensor noise and 60% on field robots for curriculum-RL multi-robot gap negotiation.

Award-session result: a distributed sparse Gaussian process with belief propagation beats DiNNO, a distributed neural-network optimizer, on accuracy, robustness to sparse communication, and continual learning.

Proves no polynomial-time algorithm can guarantee optimal sensor-range coverage even for constant-velocity straight-line targets, then solves practical 500-object instances with classical geometric algorithms, not learning.

humanoid.signal: Real-time whole-body MPC on a full-sized humanoid using only iLQR + MuJoCo finite-difference dynamics with whole-body collision detection; interactive GUI tuning on hardware; code released — drastically lowers the software barrier for humanoid locomotion.

KiRAS locomotion recipe: keyframes as minimal skill representations + self-imitation removes the mocap-dataset requirement for multi-skill quadruped policies; validated on Solo-8 and Unitree Go1 with skill transitions on rough terrain.

Online residual-dynamics learning (random Fourier features, least-squares updates) bolted onto locomotion MPC handles 8 kg unknown payloads, 20-degree slopes, and time-varying friction with sublinear regret guarantees — adaptivity without retraining.

2-10 mechanically independent quadrupeds with arms learn decentralized pinch-lift-move transport of ungraspable objects through contact forces alone (no communication), with sim2real transfer — legged teams as a forklift alternative.

Huawei is building VR teleoperation infrastructure on a humanoid collaborative robot explicitly framed as a proprioceptive-data-collection pipeline — industry investment in the humanoid data flywheel.

EquiMus: energy-equivalent MuJoCo modeling of rigid-soft musculoskeletal legs driven by linear elastic actuators, validated on a bionic robotic leg — a route to simulating compliant humanoid actuation without surrogate-model overhead.
SO(3) parallel mechanism variants applied to a humanoid eye — niche but indicative of humanoid subsystem (gaze) hardware design getting dedicated attention.

themes.name: Force/tactile signals wired into learned manipulation policies
themes.whats_new: Touch and force/torque are no longer concatenated as extra input channels — they get structural treatment: per-modality diffusion experts with a learned consensus router (TuI1I.51), random cross-modal masking so a diffusion model learns action-to-force dependencies (WeI1I.209 'Multimodal Diffusion Forcing'), FiLM conditioning of a VLA's visual features on 6-axis F/T (TuI1LB.22), and tactile-flow-derived dynamics latents feeding a one-step diffusion policy (WeI2I.55). Operator grip force is even used as a continuous arbitration signal between impedance and admittance control (TuI1I.98).

themes.name: Human video and hand demos replacing teleoperated robot data
themes.whats_new: The data bottleneck is being attacked by mining human motion instead of collecting robot demos: Alibaba's RynnVLA-001 pretrains a VLA via ego-centric video generation plus keypoint-trajectory prediction (TuI1I.270); ByteDance's DemoBot extracts hand+object trajectories from a single unannotated third-person RGB-D video as RL motion priors for bimanual dexterous assembly (ThI1I.107); HAND retrieves robot play sub-trajectories matching a human hand demo, fine-tuning a policy in under four minutes (WeI2I.182). Same pattern in rehab — learning a 'synthetic therapist' from therapist-exoskeleton-patient interaction (WeI2I.231).

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs as the glue around classical planners, not as the policy
themes.whats_new: The dominant integration is hybrid: an LLM grounds symbols and relaxes infeasible goals for a PDDL planner over 3D scene graphs (+52% SR, ThI1I.280); a VLM writes a prehensile/non-prehensile plan skeleton that a digital-twin layer and low-level controllers execute (WeI2I.184); an LLM generates and closed-loop-debugs calibration code with inserted probes (ThI1I.424); LLMs disambiguate reward functions from demos via state-relevance masks (ThI2I.233) or decompose preferences into multi-objective reward ensembles (TuI2I.174). The LLM does semantics; symbolic/optimal control keeps the guarantees.

themes.name: Frozen vision foundation models + lightweight adapters as default perception
themes.whats_new: Task-specific perception training is being displaced by frozen DINOv2/v3, VGGT, SAM and FoundationStereo backbones with tiny tuned heads: VO-DP fuses frozen VGGT geometric and DINOv2 semantic features for a vision-only diffusion policy that beats point-cloud DP3 in the real world (WeI1I.252); CaLoRA-Stereo adapts FoundationStereo to endoscopy with camera-aware LoRA gates (WeI2I.325); DMTrack does multimodal tracking with 0.93M trainable parameters via dual adapters (WeI2I.110); retail SKU identification uses frozen DINOv3 with index updates in minutes (TuI1I.208).

themes.name: Legged/humanoid terrain adaptation: MoE-RL on one side, resurgent model-based MPC on the other
themes.whats_new: On the learning side, contrastive losses are used to force mixture-of-experts gating to actually specialize per terrain (CMoE on Unitree G1, 20 cm steps / 80 cm gaps, ThI2I.243) and deep disturbance observers harden sim-trained policies (WeI1I.25). On the model-based side, a closed-loop LIP MPC walks five different humanoids with no stabilizer at all (WeBT3.9), and mixed-integer trajectory optimization picks when to switch foot vs wheel end-effectors to cut cost of transport (TuI1I.247).

themes.name: Systems hygiene: reproducibility, energy, and middleware audits
themes.whats_new: A small but pointed cluster audits the plumbing the field builds on: ThI2I.417 shows non-deterministic implementations are the major (possibly sole) cause of non-reproducible LiDAR-inertial odometry results; ThI2I.78 shows Nav2 Costmap2D configuration materially changes navigation energy use; ThI2I.186 maps ROS 2-on-Kubernetes latency/throughput bottlenecks across DDS, CNI plugins and Wi-Fi 7; ROVER (WeAT3.6) benchmarks visual SLAM across seasons and finds broad failure in low light and dense vegetation.

methods_shift: Diffusion policies are now the unmarked default for manipulation imitation learning: DP, DP3, ACT and pi_0 appear as baselines to be extended (with force masking in WeI1I.209, view synthesis in ThI2I.258, VLM keyframe inpainting in WeI1I.23, foundation-model features in WeI1I.252) rather than as contributions themselves. VLAs are treated as pretrained backbones to condition or wrap — FiLM-on-F/T (TuI1LB.22), modular stepwise decomposition for edge deployment (ThI2LB.11) — and are even directly challenged (TuI2I.336). Frozen vision foundation models (DINOv2/v3, VGGT, SAM, FoundationStereo) plus adapters/LoRA are displacing end-to-end trained perception stacks. RL-in-sim (Isaac Gym/Isaac Lab) with explicit sim2real machinery (deep DOB in WeI1I.25, OOD state initialization in TuI1I.129) remains the default for legged locomotion. 3D Gaussian Splatting is displacing NeRF for driving-scene reconstruction (LR-SGS TuBT1.8 vs the lone Bayesian NeRF ThI2I.15). Meanwhile classical control is not retreating: LIP-model MPC without stabilizers (WeBT3.9), passivity/energy-tank methods for pHRI (TuI1I.419, TuI1I.98), and sliding-mode NMPC on microcontrollers (ThI2I.33) all claim wins where guarantees and compute budgets matter; LLMs are mostly slotted in as semantic front-ends to PDDL/TAMP rather than replacing planners.

notable.why: Head-to-head with energy metering: a PDDL-based neuro-symbolic stack hits 95% on Towers of Hanoi manipulation vs 34% for fine-tuned pi_0, generalizes to 4 blocks where VLAs score 0%, and trains on ~100x less energy.
First vine robot used as a projectile launcher, with a validated dynamic eversion model and a fastest-on-record 60 m/s tip extension.
A single closed-loop LIP-based MPC walks five different humanoids over uneven and compliant terrain with no stabilizer or learned policy at all ('Adios Stabilizers').
Shows state-of-the-art LiDAR-inertial odometry results are often not reproducible run-to-run, isolates five non-deterministic implementation patterns as the cause, and gives deterministic fixes.
Worst-case predictive control with online adversarial friction estimation achieves 100% success with zero cell loss in robotic embryo vitrification, versus 66.6% open-loop.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: RynnVLA-001, a VLA model pretrained via ego-centric video generation and human trajectory modeling, with an ActionVAE latent action space — Alibaba's entry into generalist manipulation foundation models.
DemoBot: bimanual dexterous-hand skill acquisition from a single unannotated human video via RL refinement — scalable robot data generation without teleoperation.
A full multi-robot platform (sim + distributed RL + hardware) for cooperative-competitive robot game play, with OODSI for multi-agent sim2real transfer.
V2V-LLM: multimodal LLM fusing perception across connected autonomous vehicles for cooperative driving QA and planning, plus the V2V-QA benchmark.
Deployment reliability for learned policies: visual anomaly detection plus a three-level recovery process (pause, perturb, reset) wrapped around policy execution.
VO-DP, a vision-only single-view diffusion policy on frozen VGGT/DINOv2 features, plus the open-source DRRM multi-GPU manipulation training library.
ROVER, a multi-season visual SLAM benchmark for garden/outdoor robots — infrastructure for autonomous lawn/garden equipment.
DMTrack: parameter-efficient (0.93M trainable params) dual-adapter multimodal visual tracking for on-device perception.
Bilevel-optimization noise covariance auto-calibration for state estimation pipelines (invariant EKF with parallel derivative filter).

contrarian.finding: Explicitly titled against the VLA narrative: PDDL planning + learned low-level control beats fine-tuned pi_0 (95% vs 34%), generalizes where VLAs fail completely, and uses ~two orders of magnitude less training energy.

Classical closed-loop LIP MPC with contact-force modeling needs no stabilizer or learned policy and walks five different humanoids robustly — pushing back on RL as the necessary recipe for bipedal locomotion.

Negative result on the field's own evaluation practice: state-of-the-art LIO systems give non-reproducible accuracy numbers under identical conditions, driven by five identifiable non-deterministic implementation choices.
Benchmark-as-negative-result: most visual SLAM systems, including deep-learning-based ones, perform poorly in low light and dense vegetation across seasons — outdoor robustness remains unsolved.

Rejects end-to-end VLAs for edge deployment (memory, black-box debugging) in favor of a modular pipeline — MobileSAM + depth-guided sampling + classical ORB matching — reporting >99% unknown-object detection at 30 FPS on a Jetson.

Reduces grasped-object pose uncertainty to optical-tracker-level repeatability with no camera and no fixture, using three orthogonal flat-pad grasps and admittance control — pure mechanics where others add perception.

humanoid.signal: Contrastive MoE single-stage RL on a Unitree G1 forces gating networks to actually specialize per terrain, yielding traversal of 20 cm continuous steps and 80 cm gaps — Unitree G1 is the de facto research humanoid testbed.

Stabilizer-free closed-loop LIP MPC with dynamic step re-planning validated across five different humanoid platforms, including uneven and compliant terrain — a portable model-based locomotion recipe.

Adaptive motion tracking for whole-body humanoid control from a large Shanghai AI Lab / SJTU / Berkeley consortium — motion-tracking-based whole-body control is consolidating as the mainstream humanoid recipe.

First mixed-integer trajectory optimization that co-optimizes when legged robots should switch between point-foot and wheeled end-effectors, cutting cost of transport and raising speed — hybrid wheel-leg morphology as an efficiency lever.

Deep disturbance observer + deep state estimator trained in Isaac Gym transfer to a real quadruped with no extra tuning — DOB-style uncertainty compensation is becoming standard armor around sim-trained legged policies.
Quadruped loco-manipulation (pushing large objects) with obstacle avoidance trained directly into the RL objective via low-dimensional safe corridors instead of high-dimensional perception pipelines.
APREBot pairs omnidirectional LiDAR with an actively-pointed camera for reflexive obstacle evasion on a quadruped — active hierarchical perception for fast-reaction legged autonomy.

themes.name: VLA second wave: instrumenting, not just building
themes.whats_new: Papers no longer propose a VLA — they wrap one. Token-level uncertainty introspection to trigger help requests (INSIGHT on Pi0-FAST), online Monte Carlo Tree Search at inference (VLA-Reasoner), training-free attention/action-guided implicit reasoning (ATA), and a ROS2-native token-streaming runtime that starts acting before the plan finishes (Stream-to-Act). The VLA is treated as fixed infrastructure; the contribution is runtime machinery around it.

themes.name: Tactile sensing goes mainstream in manipulation
themes.whats_new: Beyond GelSight clones: a transparent UV-marker elastomer that does vision AND touch in one device for ~$70 (TransTac), MPM-based differentiable optical-tactile simulation calibrated real2sim in minutes (DOT-Sim), and biologically-inspired sensory attenuation where the robot learns to predict and subtract its own self-touch signals (TaSA). Tactile is shifting from sensing demos to policy-learning infrastructure.

themes.name: Demonstration data is the bottleneck — attack it from every angle
themes.whats_new: Gamified crowdsourced teleop with leaderboards/badges that measurably improves downstream policies (+16-56% success, RoboCade), depth-aware video-diffusion models that synthesize demonstration videos from drawn trajectories (DRAW2ACT, Huawei), zero-demonstration VLM+RL pipelines (CoDex), and even hardware redesign — fingertip suction cups explicitly motivated by making teleop data collection easier (Suction Leap-Hand).

themes.name: Safety filters wrapped around learned and LLM policies
themes.whats_new: The recipe is now standardized: let an RL policy or LLM be creative, and bolt a formal layer on top — CBF filters keeping RL-controlled UAV swarms inside 'air roads' (WeI2I.92), a safety filter making LLM-choreographed 200-drone shows deployable (SwarmGPT), conformal prediction calibrating LLM-to-LLM robot messages (CommCP), and latency-adaptive velocity scaling for transatlantic retinal surgery (LATIOS). Verification moved from the policy to the wrapper.

themes.name: LLMs/VLMs as zero-shot navigation brains, plus a benchmark wave
themes.whats_new: LLMs promoted from 'target proposer' to direct action policy via experience-injected prompts, no fine-tuning (Deja Vu nearly doubles ObjNav SR; ReThinkNav adds loop detection and runs on a Unitree G1). Simultaneously, purpose-built evaluation arrives: NaviTrace scores VLM-drawn navigation traces against 3000 expert traces and finds consistent failure in spatial grounding.

themes.name: Surgical/medical robotics absorbing the modern learning stack
themes.whats_new: VLA models for ultrasound-guided needle insertion (TuI2I.277), offline-GAN-augmented PID for multi-DoF pneumatic surgical robots with 0.16mm tip error (TuI1I.428), residual learning for continuum-robot hysteresis (ThI1I.310), and dual-console dVRK haptic skill transfer with learned force estimation (WeI1I.230). Learning is now layered onto, not replacing, classical kinematic control in medicine.

methods_shift: Diffusion is now the default action head for manipulation — DexKnot, SEM (Horizon), DAM-VLA (Samsung) all condition diffusion policies on keypoints, unified 3D representations, or VLM routing, displacing plain behavior cloning regression. VLAs are the default high-level framing, but the frontier has moved to test-time machinery on frozen models: MCTS (VLA-Reasoner), uncertainty introspection (INSIGHT), training-free attention guidance (ATA), token streaming (Stream-to-Act) — fine-tuning is being displaced by training-free adaptation, also visible in PAGTM token merging and LLM-as-navigator papers (Deja Vu, ReThinkNav). For legged robots, RL-in-sim with zero-shot sim2real is unquestioned default (FT-CPG on Unitree Go1, U2E, SoftMimic); the new ingredient is uncertainty-aware actuator modeling and compliance-shaped rewards instead of stiff motion tracking. Classical methods have retreated to — and remain dominant in — the safety and low-level layer: NMPC+CBF (NOVA), MPPI with Mahalanobis chance constraints, CBF filters over RL/LLM outputs (WeI2I.92, SwarmGPT), and convex decomposition (FIRI). DINOv2/foundation features are the default perception backbone even in coral-reef AUV search (WeI1I.250). Net pattern: learned components for generation/proposal, classical optimization and control for guarantees.

notable.why: Explicitly anti-anthropomorphic: fingertip suction cups on a Leap hand replace force closure with single-point adhesion, enabling one-handed paper cutting and in-hand writing — tasks a human hand cannot do — while making teleop data collection trivial.
Rollbot is the first spherical robot to achieve controllable 2D planar motion with a single actuator, breaking the long-standing two-actuator assumption via quasi-stable circular-trajectory dynamics.

A real transatlantic surgical teleoperation study — six North American operators controlling a retinal surgery robot in Europe — with statistically significant force reduction from latency-adaptive velocity scaling.

CoDex gets a 16-DoF hand to aim and actuate spray bottles, glue guns, and pepper grinders on unseen objects with zero demonstrations, using VLM-derived semantic constraints to seed constrained optimization plus RL.
A 10-micron magnetically-steered, acoustically-oscillated microbubble built entirely from FDA-approved materials that mechanically kills breast cancer cells — robotics as a direct cancer therapy.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: DAM-VLA, a VLA framework with action routing between arm/gripper diffusion experts, aimed at warehouse, hospital, and home manipulation — at least five Samsung units co-authoring suggests a coordinated robot-foundation-model push.
DRAW2ACT: video-diffusion world models that turn drawn depth-encoded trajectories into synthetic robot demonstration videos, attacking the manipulation data bottleneck.
SEM, a diffusion manipulation policy with unified 3D spatial representation — the chip/AD company building generalist manipulation stacks.
TrackVLA++: VLA with polar-coordinate chain-of-thought and gated target memory for embodied person-following — core capability for their commercial service/companion robots.
ROS2-native token-streaming runtime (llama-ros extension) so VLM/VLA plans execute continuously on Jetson-class hardware — productionizing foundation-model control.
Tactile contact-mode strategies (grasp/slide/roll) for object shape reconstruction with 34% fewer interactions — tactile perception for warehouse grasping.
Many-to-many multi-agent pickup-and-delivery assignment for automated warehouses, completing up to 22,000 more tasks over 8-hour operations.
Coverage path planning with a (1.5+epsilon) approximation guarantee for their commercial floor-cleaning robots.
Quantifying how failure types (slips vs lapses vs mistakes) damage perceived robot reliability and how successes repair trust — groundwork for home robot deployment.
Assistant Placement Aria benchmark for egocentric placement assistance — feeding Aria-glasses data toward embodied assistants.
Gamified remote teleoperation platform crowdsourcing manipulation demonstrations from the public, validated to improve downstream policy success 16-56%.
Haptic-feedback teleoperation of dexterous robot hands — first study showing operators discriminate object stiffness from tactile fingertips alone, key to their humanoid teleop data pipeline.
MASAR: end-to-end joint 3D detection and trajectory forecasting (>20% minADE/minFDE gains on nuScenes) for production autonomous driving.

contrarian.finding: Bibliometric analysis of 2,500+ papers across 13 functional modules of autonomy concludes AI is NOT actually pervasive — several functions where AI impact 'would be expected to be significant' remain surprisingly untouched, deflating the everything-is-learned narrative.

Particle filters trained only on single-step transitions with denoising score matching match tuned end-to-end sequence-trained estimators — exploiting the Markov property beats expensive unrolled training, and keeps classical composability (swap sensors without retraining).
A commercial cleaning-robot company (Avidbots) ships a purely classical coverage planner with a provable (1.5+epsilon) approximation bound — no learning anywhere, guarantees over benchmarks.
For human trajectory prediction, just the 3D lower-body keypoints cut error by 13% — legs alone carry the signal, arguing for cheaper, simpler sensing rather than full-body pose pipelines.

For warehouse multi-agent pathfinding, a constraint tweak to classical Conflict-Based Search (reversible lanes forcing bypass usage) beats both corridor reasoning and designed highways — incremental classical search still wins where learned MAPF struggles.

FIRI hand-tailors convex optimization to the structure of free-space polytope generation and gets orders-of-magnitude speedups over generic solvers — domain-specific classical math, not GPUs or learning, is the speed lever.

humanoid.signal: MIT's SoftMimic: the emerging anti-stiffness recipe for humanoid whole-body RL — reward compliant responses (via IK-augmented compliant motion datasets) instead of aggressive reference tracking, yielding safe contact behavior from a single motion clip on real hardware.

IIT puts five Xela magnetic tactile fingertips on a humanoid and does whole-body (torso+arm+wrist+finger) force redistribution via CoP-centroid control — 82.7% success balancing unstable objects; tactile-driven whole-body control arriving on humanoids.
Unitree G1 used as a commodity validation platform for a zero-shot LLM navigation paper — humanoids are becoming the default real-world demo vehicle even for non-locomotion research.

Knee-inspired hinge with high-static-low-dynamic stiffness passively absorbs longitudinal impacts in walking robots, cutting ground reaction forces and body acceleration — mechanical compliance as a bolt-on safety layer for legged designs.

FT-CPG: CPG+RL fault-tolerant locomotion surviving arbitrary multi-joint locking/power-loss failures on a single leg, zero-shot sim2real on Unitree Go1 — failure robustness now trained in, not engineered.

U2E attacks the legged sim2real gap at its source: deep-ensemble actuator models with uncertainty estimates actively guide which real-world data to collect, refining actuator models for quadruped jumping and tracking.

London humanoid startup 'Humanoid' co-authoring teleop haptics research — first evidence operators can discriminate remote object stiffness purely from tactile-fingertip-driven haptic feedback, directly relevant to humanoid teleop data collection economics.

themes.name: Gaussian splatting as the universal scene substrate (beyond rendering)
