themes.whats_new: 3DGS is no longer just a NeRF replacement for view synthesis — it is being coupled to physics and decision-making: GaussTwin anchors Gaussians to position-based dynamics + Cosserat rod primitives for a correctable digital twin; ThI2I.225 builds tail-risk (AVaR) maps directly on an online Gaussian-splat radiance field for risk-averse planning; PLOP runs a particle filter over 3D Gaussians to learn deformable-object dynamics; S3LAM swaps 3D ellipsoids for 2D surfels and derives pose Jacobians from the splatting formulation; ROOM-3D and E2-BKI use Gaussian primitives for online open-vocabulary semantic mapping.

themes.name: Foundation models (VLM/MLLM/video-gen) as zero-shot robot planners
themes.whats_new: The pattern has shifted from fine-tuning to zero-shot use with structured scaffolding to contain hallucination: ThI2I.90 feeds a topological graph + visit history into MLLM prompts for VLN (41% SR on R2R-CE zero-shot); RANGER does monocular open-vocab navigation with no depth/pose using 3D foundation models; NovaFlow distills generated videos into 3D actionable object flow for demonstration-free manipulation; VisuaLLMPlanner constrains the MLLM to select among pre-validated maneuvers rather than generate plans; PStar uses pseudocode libraries to structure VLM reasoning; RAG-RUSS adds retrieval-augmented generation to make robotic ultrasound interpretable.

themes.name: Diffusion policies are the default generative backbone — and the latency backlash has begun
themes.whats_new: Diffusion is now the unremarkable default for manipulation and motion generation (ADCS for constrained bimanual sampling, RoTri-Diff for triadic bimanual IL, MAKP's diffusion motion generator for humanoid kicking, AeroScene for 3D scene synthesis, DiffuDepGrasp for sensor-noise modeling). The new frontier is escaping its iterative-denoising cost: TuI2I.301 shows Implicit Maximum Likelihood Estimation (IMLE) matches diffusion planners on offline-RL benchmarks at two orders of magnitude faster inference, enabling real-time closed-loop MPC.

themes.name: Humanoids graduating from walking to dynamic, interactive whole-body skills
themes.whats_new: The recipe has converged: model-based high-level planner + RL whole-body tracking controller + human motion references, deployed on commodity platforms (Unitree G1, Booster T1). HITTER achieves 106 consecutive table-tennis rallies against a human with sub-second reaction; MAKP does multi-mode accurate soccer kicking on Booster T1 via diffusion motion generation + multi-critic curriculum RL; CoTaP adds task-space compliance modulation on the SPD manifold to position-based humanoid RL; MimicDroid gets in-context learning for humanoid manipulation purely from human play videos; LapSurgie puts a teleoperated humanoid behind a laparoscope.

themes.name: Classical SLAM/odometry persists, upgraded with learned components and dropping old assumptions
themes.whats_new: Not end-to-end replacement but surgical insertion of learning: ThI1I.353 learns IMU bias prediction with a network so the invariant MSCKF keeps its Lie-group symmetry; TuI2I.359 and GMM-LIO break the static-world assumption in LiDAR-inertial odometry via spatio-temporal normal analysis and GMM voxel maps; EC3R-SLAM and SurgCUT3R bolt feed-forward 3D reconstruction models (CUT3R/MASt3R-style) into SLAM to eliminate calibration; LST-SLAM does km-scale stereo thermal SLAM with self-supervised features.

themes.name: Occupancy world models for driving: predict residuals and tokens, not full scenes
themes.whats_new: The driving world-model papers converge on avoiding full future-scene reconstruction: OccTENS reformulates occupancy forecasting as temporal next-scale prediction (VAR-style) with unified pose-occupancy sequence modeling; IR-WM predicts only the action-conditioned residual over the previous BEV state; OccLLaMA tokenizes semantic occupancy into an LLaMA-based occupancy-language-action model so understanding, forecasting and planning share one autoregressive backbone.

methods_shift: Diffusion policies are now the unmarked default for manipulation/motion generation — papers no longer justify using them, they extend them (constraint-energy composition in ADCS, hierarchical diffusion in RoTri-Diff/AeroScene) — but their inference latency is the acknowledged weakness, with IMLE (TuI2I.301) and feedback-gain-augmented MPPI (F-MPPI, WeI1I.364, Riccati-style gains from rollout sensitivity to skip re-optimization) attacking it from generative and sampling-MPC sides respectively. VLAs (OpenVLA) appear as priors/teachers to be distilled into RL rather than deployed policies (TuI1LB.7, MLA TuI2I.195), and VLMs/MLLMs are used zero-shot with structured scaffolds (topo-graphs, pseudocode, pre-validated action menus) rather than fine-tuned. RL-in-sim with domain randomization is the unquestioned default for legged locomotion, now layered with risk conditioning (CVaR policies + bandit selection, WeI1I.182) and residual-RL on top of model-based contact controllers (ThI1I.385). In perception, 3D Gaussian splatting is displacing NeRF and TSDF/mesh maps across SLAM, digital twins and planning, while feed-forward reconstruction foundation models (MASt3R/CUT3R lineage) are displacing calibrated multi-view geometry pipelines (EC3R-SLAM, SurgCUT3R, MASt3R-Nav). Classical estimation (invariant EKF, ICP, point-line SLAM) is not dying — it is absorbing small learned modules (learned IMU bias in ThI1I.353) and getting hardware acceleration (FPGA ICP, WeI2I.171).

notable.why: HITTER (award session): a general-purpose humanoid sustains up to 106 consecutive table-tennis shots against a human opponent — sub-second perceive-predict-strike whole-body control, a milestone for dynamic humanoid interaction.

First systematic ROS 2 fingerprinting framework plus an Internet-wide DDS scan showing deployed robots are identifiable and exploitable even with SROS2 available — a rare, alarming security measurement paper at a robotics venue.
IMLE matches diffusion-based planners on offline-RL benchmarks while running two orders of magnitude faster — a direct challenge to the diffusion-policy hegemony on latency grounds.

LapSurgie is the first humanoid-robot laparoscopic teleoperation framework, doing RCM-constrained surgery with off-the-shelf manual instruments — humanoids pitched as infrastructure-free surgical robots for low-resource hospitals.

Flash does visual place recognition from sub-millisecond slices of event-camera data using binary frames and bitwise operations, improving sub-ms Recall@1 by up to 11.3x — a paradigm break from dense event accumulation.

industry_signal.what_theyre_building: Generative occupancy world models (next-scale autoregressive prediction) for autonomous driving stacks.
Data-efficiency tooling for imitation-learned autonomous driving — VQ-VAE cluster-based priority sampling evaluated in CARLA/Bench2Drive.
SHOPPER: a mobile manipulation platform field-tested on hundreds of grasps in real grocery stores, plus a 1200+ grasp-attempt dataset.
PLOP: particle-filter-over-3D-Gaussians dynamics learning for deformable objects (dough cutting) from robot interaction videos.

LogoNav: a long-horizon visual navigation foundation policy trained by model-based reannotation of crowd-sourced teleop data and YouTube video, deployed on robot fleets in six cities on three continents.
DSPv2: dense policies for whole-body mobile manipulation on their humanoid-style mobile platform.
MLA: a multisensory (vision + 3D + tactile) language-action model for contact-rich manipulation, beating 2D/3D VLA baselines by 12-24%.
ActiveUMI: robot-free VR-based portable data collection with egocentric active perception for bimanual manipulation policies.
DiffuDepGrasp: diffusion-based depth-sensor noise modeling enabling zero-shot sim2real grasping (95.7% success) with simulation-only training.
S3LAM: 2D surfel-splatting RGB-D SLAM, presumably for their commercial service/delivery robots.
SUBTA: shared-autonomy bimanual teleoperation with intention estimation and scene-graph task planning for structured assembly.
NovaFlow: zero-shot manipulation by distilling generated videos into 3D actionable object flow, transferring across embodiments (Franka arm, Spot).
Lidar Teach-and-Repeat autonomy for a one-tonne lunar cargo rover, field-tested at the Canadian Space Agency analogue terrain.
Hybrid model+learning joint torque estimation for their lower-limb exoskeletons.
ClearDepth: stereo transformer depth recovery for transparent objects with a sim2real data pipeline, +18% grasp success.

contrarian.finding: Replaces diffusion planners with IMLE, conceding the field's favorite generative model is too slow for closed-loop MPC — competitive quality at ~100x faster inference.

TRI's grocery-store field test is essentially an honest failure-mode catalog: hundreds of real pick attempts show fundamental grasping failures persist in SOTA mobile manipulation, far from deployment-ready.
ERPoT: pure-LiDAR pose tracking against a compact classical polygon map beats six baselines on reliability, error, map size and runtime — no learning anywhere in the loop.

A game-theoretic optimization formulation (adversarial object escape motions) beats learned dexterous grasp-synthesis baselines by up to 19.6%, with 85-87.5% real-world success on ShadowHand/LeapHand.
Against the deployment-optimism narrative: Internet-wide scans show real ROS 2 robots are fingerprintable and exploitable in default configurations despite existing security tooling (SROS2).
New benchmark shows motion blur — mundane and ubiquitous — substantially degrades modern VPR methods, a robustness gap the learned-features literature had largely ignored.

humanoid.signal: The winning humanoid skill recipe is now standardized: model-based ball-trajectory planner + RL whole-body controller trained with human motion references — 106 consecutive table-tennis rallies, sub-second reaction.
Booster T1 as research platform; accurate multi-mode soccer kicking via diffusion motion generator + three-stage curriculum with multi-critic RL to balance single-leg stability vs kick accuracy.
CoTaP tackles the compliance gap of position-based humanoid RL: dual-agent RL distillation plus model-based task-space compliance modulated on the SPD manifold.

Tooling commoditization: a MATLAB/Simulink + MuJoCo + ROS 2 sim2real workflow for the Unitree G1 — the G1 is now standard enough to warrant Simulink integration, lowering the entry barrier for humanoid control research.

Directly relevant to low-cost actuators: Q-CYC, an open-source 3D-printed quadruple-disk cycloidal compact-cam reducer with measurably reduced gear play and improved backdrivability and speed regularity vs the standard compact-cam design — a cheap gearbox path worth checking against the EUH-1 BOM.

MimicDroid: humanoid manipulation via in-context learning trained only on unlabeled human play videos (wrist-pose retargeting + patch masking), ~2x real-world success over SOTA — scalable data without teleoperation.
